trans-unit.source: The current protocol amendment is considered substantial as a key secondary objective was updated. trans-unit.source: Protocol Title: A Phase III, Multicentre, Randomised, Double-blind, Chronic-dosing, Parallel-group, Placebo-controlled Extension Study to Evaluate the Long-term Efficacy and Safety of Tozorakimab in Participants with Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) with a History of Exacerbations (PROSPERO)
trans-unit.target: Protocol Title: A Phase III, Multicentre, Randomised, Double-blind, Chronic-dosing, Parallel-group, Placebo-controlled Extension Study to Evaluate the Long-term Efficacy and Safety of Tozorakimab in Participants with Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) with a History of Exacerbations (PROSPERO)

trans-unit.source: Title: Long-term Efficacy and Safety of Tozorakimab in Participants with Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease with a History of Exacerbations
trans-unit.target: Title: Long-term Efficacy and Safety of Tozorakimab in Participants with Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease with a History of Exacerbations

trans-unit.source: This is a Phase III, multicentre, randomised, double-blind, chronic-dosing, parallel-group, placebo-controlled extension study to evaluate the long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab versus placebo on top of standard of care (SoC) in participants with COPD with a history of exacerbations (PROSPERO) who completed the 52-week double-blind treatment period of D9180C00003 (OBERON) or D9180C00004 (TITANIA) studies (hereafter referred to as ‘predecessor studies’). Eligible participants that are willing to continue treatment must have completed the treatment period and not been prematurely discontinued from investigational product (IP) in the predecessor studies.
trans-unit.target: This is a Phase III, multicentre, randomised, double-blind, chronic-dosing, parallel-group, placebo-controlled extension study to evaluate the long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab versus placebo on top of standard of care (SoC) in participants with COPD with a history of exacerbations (PROSPERO) who completed the 52-week double-blind treatment period of D9180C00003 (OBERON) or D9180C00004 (TITANIA) studies (hereafter referred to as ‘predecessor studies’). Eligible participants that are willing to continue treatment must have completed the treatment period and not been prematurely discontinued from investigational product (IP) in the predecessor studies.

trans-unit.source: This extension study is primarily designed to evaluate the efficacy of tozorakimab on severe COPD exacerbations and to characterise the long-term safety profile of tozorakimab in participants with COPD treated for longer than 1 year.
trans-unit.target: This extension study is primarily designed to evaluate the efficacy of tozorakimab on severe COPD exacerbations and to characterise the long-term safety profile of tozorakimab in participants with COPD treated for longer than 1 year.

trans-unit.source: All data related to the efficacy endpoints will be combined with the 52 weeks of data obtained from the predecessor studies as part of an integrated analysis. As with the primary and key secondary objectives, other secondary objectives will also be analysed in the population of former or current smokers as well as in former smokers.
trans-unit.target: All data related to the efficacy endpoints will be combined with the 52 weeks of data obtained from the predecessor studies as part of an integrated analysis. As with the primary and key secondary objectives, other secondary objectives will also be analysed in the population of former or current smokers as well as in former smokers.

trans-unit.source: To evaluate the long-term effect of tozorakimab as an add on to SoC compared with SoC plus placebo on the rate of severe COPD exacerbations in former smokers.
trans-unit.target: To evaluate the long-term effect of tozorakimab as an add on to SoC compared with SoC plus placebo on the rate of severe COPD exacerbations in former smokers.

trans-unit.target: For exploratory objectives and endpoints, refer to Section 3 of the clinical study protocol (CSP).

trans-unit.source: This is a Phase III, multicentre, randomised, double-blind, chronic dosing, parallel-group, placebo-controlled extension study to evaluate the long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab in participants with COPD with a history of COPD exacerbations (PROSPERO). Pooled analysis with data from the predecessor studies will be performed to assess efficacy starting from randomisation in the predecessor studies, allowing for collection of greater numbers of severe COPD exacerbation events, given the low rates of hospitalisation due to COPD since the severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) pandemic (Alqahtani et al 2021). Given that tozorakimab is expected to be a long-term, chronic maintenance treatment, this study will also characterise the long-term safety profile of tozorakimab in COPD patients treated for longer than 1 year.
trans-unit.target: This is a Phase III, multicentre, randomised, double-blind, chronic dosing, parallel-group, placebo-controlled extension study to evaluate the long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab in participants with COPD with a history of COPD exacerbations (PROSPERO). Pooled analysis with data from the predecessor studies will be performed to assess efficacy starting from randomisation in the predecessor studies, allowing for collection of greater numbers of severe COPD exacerbation events, given the low rates of hospitalisation due to COPD since the severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) pandemic (Alqahtani et al 2021). Given that tozorakimab is expected to be a long-term, chronic maintenance treatment, this study will also characterise the long-term safety profile of tozorakimab in COPD patients treated for longer than 1 year.

trans-unit.source: The placebo cohort is included to provide a control arm, so as to enable a continued comparison of long-term efficacy, in particular the comparison of time to first severe exacerbations and to act as a comparator to contextualise safety observations.
trans-unit.target: The placebo cohort is included to provide a control arm, so as to enable a continued comparison of long-term efficacy, in particular the comparison of time to first severe exacerbations and to act as a comparator to contextualise safety observations.

trans-unit.source: Participants that are willing to continue in the study and who have completed the study treatment period in one of the predecessor studies and have not been prematurely discontinued from IP may be eligible to enrol into this long-term extension (LTE) study (PROSPERO) if they fulfil the inclusion/exclusion criteria.
trans-unit.target: Participants that are willing to continue in the study and who have completed the study treatment period in one of the predecessor studies and have not been prematurely discontinued from IP may be eligible to enrol into this long-term extension (LTE) study (PROSPERO) if they fulfil the inclusion/exclusion criteria.

trans-unit.source: Visit 1 of PROSPERO will be conducted on the same day as the last scheduled visit of the treatment period in the predecessor study (Visit 15, Week 52). If this is not feasible, Visit 1 of PROSPERO may be conducted up to 12 weeks after the last dose of IP in the predecessor study (scheduled at Visit 14, Week 48), provided that the participant has remained in predecessor study follow-up during this time.
trans-unit.target: Visit 1 of PROSPERO will be conducted on the same day as the last scheduled visit of the treatment period in the predecessor study (Visit 15, Week 52). If this is not feasible, Visit 1 of PROSPERO may be conducted up to 12 weeks after the last dose of IP in the predecessor study (scheduled at Visit 14, Week 48), provided that the participant has remained in predecessor study follow-up during this time.

trans-unit.source: Participants who meet the eligibility criteria for PROSPERO will exit the predecessor study and receive the first dose in PROSPERO at Visit 1, otherwise, participants will continue in the predecessor study follow-up period until completion (refer to the respective predecessor study protocol).
trans-unit.target: Participants who meet the eligibility criteria for PROSPERO will exit the predecessor study and receive the first dose in PROSPERO at Visit 1, otherwise, participants will continue in the predecessor study follow-up period until completion (refer to the respective predecessor study protocol).

trans-unit.source: IP will be administered via accessorised pre-filled syringe (APFS) every 4 weeks. Starting from Visit 5, participants will have an option for self-administration of IP by the participant or their caregiver.
trans-unit.target: IP will be administered via accessorised pre-filled syringe (APFS) every 4 weeks. Starting from Visit 5, participants will have an option for self-administration of IP by the participant or their caregiver.

trans-unit.source: At the end of the treatment period participants will enter 8-week post-treatment follow-up period with the final follow-up visit performed 12 weeks after the last IP dose.
trans-unit.target: At the end of the treatment period participants will enter 8-week post-treatment follow-up period with the final follow-up visit performed 12 weeks after the last IP dose.

trans-unit.source: The primary reporting of the study may take place once both predecessor studies have been unblinded and reported without waiting for all participants to complete treatment in this study. To ensure consistency with reporting PROSPERO and its predecessor studies, some participants may not complete the full 28-week treatment period. By the time of PROSPERO’s primary reporting, IP dosing will be discontinued for all participants. As a result, some participants may conclude the study prior to finishing the full treatment duration. Participants will be made aware of this during the consent process. The participants who do not complete the full treatment period will be asked to attend the E/D visit, scheduled
trans-unit.target: The primary reporting of the study may take place once both predecessor studies have been unblinded and reported without waiting for all participants to complete treatment in this study. To ensure consistency with reporting PROSPERO and its predecessor studies, some participants may not complete the full 28-week treatment period. By the time of PROSPERO’s primary reporting, IP dosing will be discontinued for all participants. As a result, some participants may conclude the study prior to finishing the full treatment duration. Participants will be made aware of this during the consent process. The participants who do not complete the full treatment period will be asked to attend the E/D visit, scheduled

trans-unit.source: 12 weeks after their final IP dose, to complete their participation.
trans-unit.target: 12 weeks after their final IP dose, to complete their participation.

trans-unit.source: This is a randomised, double-blind, chronic dosing, parallel-group, placebo-controlled, extension study with 3 treatment arms that will evaluate the long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab in participants with COPD with a history of COPD exacerbations.
trans-unit.target: This is a randomised, double-blind, chronic dosing, parallel-group, placebo-controlled, extension study with 3 treatment arms that will evaluate the long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab in participants with COPD with a history of COPD exacerbations.

trans-unit.source: The total sample size will be determined by the number of participants completing the predecessor studies but is anticipated to be up to 2120.
trans-unit.target: The total sample size will be determined by the number of participants completing the predecessor studies but is anticipated to be up to 2120.

trans-unit.source: Participants will be assigned to treatment using randomisation and trial supply management (RTSM).
trans-unit.target: Participants will be assigned to treatment using randomisation and trial supply management (RTSM).

trans-unit.source: Under CSP Version 1.0, 50% of participants from the placebo arm in the predecessor studies will be re-randomised to active treatment, equally to tozorakimab 300 mg every 4 weeks (Q4W) or tozorakimab 300 mg every 8 weeks (Q8W), and 50% of the participants from the placebo arm will continue to receive placebo alongside SoC. All participants from active treatment arms in predecessor studies will remain on the same treatment. Tozorakimab
trans-unit.target: Under CSP Version 1.0, 50% of participants from the placebo arm in the predecessor studies will be re-randomised to active treatment, equally to tozorakimab 300 mg every 4 weeks (Q4W) or tozorakimab 300 mg every 8 weeks (Q8W), and 50% of the participants from the placebo arm will continue to receive placebo alongside SoC. All participants from active treatment arms in predecessor studies will remain on the same treatment. Tozorakimab

trans-unit.source: 300 mg Q8W will have alternating matching placebo between active tozorakimab administrations. The duration of the treatment period is 52 weeks, with the last dose of IP administered at Week 48.
trans-unit.target: 300 mg Q8W will have alternating matching placebo between active tozorakimab administrations. The duration of the treatment period is 52 weeks, with the last dose of IP administered at Week 48.

trans-unit.source: Under CSP Version 2.0, 50% of participants from the placebo arm in the predecessor studies will be re-randomised to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W, and 50% of the participants from the placebo arm will continue to receive placebo alongside SoC. Participants from tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W in predecessor studies will remain on 300 mg Q4W and participants from tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W in predecessor studies will switch to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W at the point of entry to PROSPERO. The duration of the treatment period is 28 weeks, with the last dose of IP administered at Week 24. Those participants who are already randomised under CSP Version 1.0 can continue to complete the study with the same treatment regimen as assigned and for a treatment period of 52 weeks after the amendment.
trans-unit.target: Under CSP Version 2.0, 50% of participants from the placebo arm in the predecessor studies will be re-randomised to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W, and 50% of the participants from the placebo arm will continue to receive placebo alongside SoC. Participants from tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W in predecessor studies will remain on 300 mg Q4W and participants from tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W in predecessor studies will switch to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W at the point of entry to PROSPERO. The duration of the treatment period is 28 weeks, with the last dose of IP administered at Week 24. Those participants who are already randomised under CSP Version 1.0 can continue to complete the study with the same treatment regimen as assigned and for a treatment period of 52 weeks after the amendment.

trans-unit.source: All study intervention will be supplied via blinded APFS. The re-randomisation of PROSPERO from the predecessor studies will allow more participants to receive the potentially effective experimental treatment rather than extended SoC, while retaining a comparator arm.
trans-unit.target: All study intervention will be supplied via blinded APFS. The re-randomisation of PROSPERO from the predecessor studies will allow more participants to receive the potentially effective experimental treatment rather than extended SoC, while retaining a comparator arm.

trans-unit.source: The primary database lock will occur after the readout of the predecessor studies. Once the blind is broken in the predecessor studies, then for those participants who had been on active treatment in the predecessor studies, the Sponsor will become unblinded to regimen. However, study conduct and blinding at the site and for the participants will remain unchanged. The study team in direct contact with sites or with influence over management of participants or recording of endpoints in PROSPERO will remain blinded and be separate from the team involved in reporting of the predecessor studies.
trans-unit.target: The primary database lock will occur after the readout of the predecessor studies. Once the blind is broken in the predecessor studies, then for those participants who had been on active treatment in the predecessor studies, the Sponsor will become unblinded to regimen. However, study conduct and blinding at the site and for the participants will remain unchanged. The study team in direct contact with sites or with influence over management of participants or recording of endpoints in PROSPERO will remain blinded and be separate from the team involved in reporting of the predecessor studies.

trans-unit.source: An independent data monitoring committee (IDMC) will be established to oversee the safety of the study participants and to make appropriate recommendations regarding further study conduct based on the available data, including regular review of safety data and at a planned interim analysis where the opportunity to report on the study early for overwhelming efficacy may be considered. The committee will operate in accordance with an IDMC charter.
trans-unit.target: An independent data monitoring committee (IDMC) will be established to oversee the safety of the study participants and to make appropriate recommendations regarding further study conduct based on the available data, including regular review of safety data and at a planned interim analysis where the opportunity to report on the study early for overwhelming efficacy may be considered. The committee will operate in accordance with an IDMC charter.

trans-unit.source: An Independent Adjudication Committee will provide an independent, external, systematic, and unbiased assessment of heart failure events (worsening and new onset), major acute cardiovascular events (MACE), and all adverse events (AEs) with fatal outcome.
trans-unit.target: An Independent Adjudication Committee will provide an independent, external, systematic, and unbiased assessment of heart failure events (worsening and new onset), major acute cardiovascular events (MACE), and all adverse events (AEs) with fatal outcome.

trans-unit.source: The number of participants entering PROSPERO is determined by the number of participants who complete the treatment period in the predecessor studies (OBERON and TITANIA) and who have not been prematurely discontinued from IP. As such, the sample size entering PROSPERO is anticipated to be up to 2120 participants, although, this is dependent upon the number of participants recruited to the tozorakimab Q8W arm and the proportion of participants who complete the predecessor study and consent to enter this extension, so the actual number may be either higher or lower. All participants randomised and dosed in the predecessor studies will be used in integrated analyses.
trans-unit.target: The number of participants entering PROSPERO is determined by the number of participants who complete the treatment period in the predecessor studies (OBERON and TITANIA) and who have not been prematurely discontinued from IP. As such, the sample size entering PROSPERO is anticipated to be up to 2120 participants, although, this is dependent upon the number of participants recruited to the tozorakimab Q8W arm and the proportion of participants who complete the predecessor study and consent to enter this extension, so the actual number may be either higher or lower. All participants randomised and dosed in the predecessor studies will be used in integrated analyses.

trans-unit.source: The primary endpoint is the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations. This will be assessed for each dose of tozorakimab versus placebo primarily in the population of former smokers, although confirmatory hypothesis tests will only be conducted on the comparison of tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W to placebo.
trans-unit.target: The primary endpoint is the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations. This will be assessed for each dose of tozorakimab versus placebo primarily in the population of former smokers, although confirmatory hypothesis tests will only be conducted on the comparison of tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W to placebo.

trans-unit.source: Analyses will be conducted in the full analysis set (FAS), using all available data regardless of treatment or study discontinuation, treatment switching, or change in smoking status, aside from data after re-randomisation from placebo to active therapy. The use of this population will provide an estimate of the treatment policy estimand.
trans-unit.target: Analyses will be conducted in the full analysis set (FAS), using all available data regardless of treatment or study discontinuation, treatment switching, or change in smoking status, aside from data after re-randomisation from placebo to active therapy. The use of this population will provide an estimate of the treatment policy estimand.

trans-unit.source: The rate ratio of severe COPD exacerbations in each tozorakimab dose regimen group relative to the placebo group will be derived using a negative binomial model. The response variable in the model will be the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations. The model will include treatment, predecessor study, region, maintenance inhaled therapy, and baseline COPD exacerbation history as categorical covariates, and baseline post-bronchodilator (BD) percent predicted forced expiratory volume in 1 second (FEV1) and log blood eosinophil count as continuous covariates. The logarithm of the participant’s corresponding follow-up time will be used as an offset variable in the model. For the analysis in the overall population, smoking status will also be included as a covariate.
trans-unit.target: The rate ratio of severe COPD exacerbations in each tozorakimab dose regimen group relative to the placebo group will be derived using a negative binomial model. The response variable in the model will be the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations. The model will include treatment, predecessor study, region, maintenance inhaled therapy, and baseline COPD exacerbation history as categorical covariates, and baseline post-bronchodilator (BD) percent predicted forced expiratory volume in 1 second (FEV1) and log blood eosinophil count as continuous covariates. The logarithm of the participant’s corresponding follow-up time will be used as an offset variable in the model. For the analysis in the overall population, smoking status will also be included as a covariate.

trans-unit.source: The estimated treatment effect (ie, rate ratios) relative to the placebo comparator will be displayed along with the associated 2-sided 95% confidence interval (CI) and p-values. In addition, the model adjusted exacerbation rate in each treatment group will be presented.
trans-unit.target: The estimated treatment effect (ie, rate ratios) relative to the placebo comparator will be displayed along with the associated 2-sided 95% confidence interval (CI) and p-values. In addition, the model adjusted exacerbation rate in each treatment group will be presented.

trans-unit.source: Data after withdrawal from study or re-randomisation from placebo to active will be imputed using Dropout Reason-based Multiple Imputation.
trans-unit.target: Data after withdrawal from study or re-randomisation from placebo to active will be imputed using Dropout Reason-based Multiple Imputation.

trans-unit.source: An IDMC will oversee a futility analysis in the predecessor studies to assess whether the studies can be stopped for futility, and may oversee an interim analysis in this study and the predecessor studies based upon the primary endpoint in each study to assess whether the
trans-unit.target: An IDMC will oversee a futility analysis in the predecessor studies to assess whether the studies can be stopped for futility, and may oversee an interim analysis in this study and the predecessor studies based upon the primary endpoint in each study to assess whether the

trans-unit.source: programme can be reported early for overwhelming efficacy. The IDMC will also periodically review safety summary tables and listings to evaluate participant safety and make appropriate recommendations, including at the time of interim analysis (Section 9.5).
trans-unit.target: programme can be reported early for overwhelming efficacy. The IDMC will also periodically review safety summary tables and listings to evaluate participant safety and make appropriate recommendations, including at the time of interim analysis (Section 9.5).

trans-unit.source: The interim analysis for futility conducted in the predecessor studies may impact PROSPERO. Refer to Section 4.4.1 for details.
trans-unit.target: The interim analysis for futility conducted in the predecessor studies may impact PROSPERO. Refer to Section 4.4.1 for details.

trans-unit.source: An interim analysis for efficacy may be conducted and alpha adjustment will be made only if in the Primary Population of former smokers the following point is reached:
trans-unit.target: An interim analysis for efficacy may be conducted and alpha adjustment will be made only if in the Primary Population of former smokers the following point is reached:

trans-unit.source: At least 80% information fraction for the rate of moderate or severe exacerbations is reached in each predecessor study, and
trans-unit.target: At least 80% information fraction for the rate of moderate or severe exacerbations is reached in each predecessor study, and

trans-unit.source: At least 70% information fraction for the rate of severe exacerbations are observed in a pooled analysis of this study and the 2 predecessor studies, and
trans-unit.target: At least 70% information fraction for the rate of severe exacerbations are observed in a pooled analysis of this study and the 2 predecessor studies, and

trans-unit.source: This is reached by at least 6 months prior to the point where every participant has been treated for 1 year in the predecessor studies.
trans-unit.target: This is reached by at least 6 months prior to the point where every participant has been treated for 1 year in the predecessor studies.

trans-unit.source: Decision guidance will be provided to the IDMC.
trans-unit.target: Decision guidance will be provided to the IDMC.

trans-unit.source: Note: Refer to Section 4.1.1 if Visit 1 of PROSPERO is not conducted on the same day as Visit 15 of the predecessor studies.
trans-unit.target: Note: Refer to Section 4.1.1 if Visit 1 of PROSPERO is not conducted on the same day as Visit 15 of the predecessor studies.

trans-unit.source: Under CSP Version 1.0: The placebo arm will be re-randomised in a 1:1:2 ratio (tozorakimab Q4W, tozorakimab Q8W, and matching placebo, respectively).
trans-unit.target: Under CSP Version 1.0: The placebo arm will be re-randomised in a 1:1:2 ratio (tozorakimab Q4W, tozorakimab Q8W, and matching placebo, respectively).

trans-unit.source: Under CSP Version 2.0: the placebo arm will be re-randomised in a 1:1 ratio (tozorakimab Q4W and matching placebo, respectively) and participants in the tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm will switch to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W. Those participants who are already randomised under CSP Version 1.0 can continue to complete the study with the same treatment regimen as assigned and for a treatment period of 52 weeks after this amendment. CSP, clinical study protocol; FU, follow-up; LTE, long-term extension; O/T, OBERON or TITANIA predecessor studies; Q4W, every 4 weeks; Q8W, every 8 weeks; SC, subcutaneous(ly); V, Visit.
trans-unit.target: Under CSP Version 2.0: the placebo arm will be re-randomised in a 1:1 ratio (tozorakimab Q4W and matching placebo, respectively) and participants in the tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm will switch to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W. Those participants who are already randomised under CSP Version 1.0 can continue to complete the study with the same treatment regimen as assigned and for a treatment period of 52 weeks after this amendment. CSP, clinical study protocol; FU, follow-up; LTE, long-term extension; O/T, OBERON or TITANIA predecessor studies; Q4W, every 4 weeks; Q8W, every 8 weeks; SC, subcutaneous(ly); V, Visit.

trans-unit.target: Schedule of Activities for the 52-Week Treatment Period

trans-unit.source: The Schedule of Activities for the 52-week treatment period applies to participants who are enrolled under CSP Version 1.0 and is provided in Table 1 (see Section 4.1.2 for more details).
trans-unit.target: The Schedule of Activities for the 52-week treatment period applies to participants who are enrolled under CSP Version 1.0 and is provided in Table 1 (see Section 4.1.2 for more details).

trans-unit.source: Starting from Week 16 (Visit 5 of PROSPERO), and every other visit going forward, the participants who have opted in for virtual visits, completed training, and been deemed suitable for self-administration/home visits will have an opportunity to conduct their visits as a remote telemedicine option with at-home IP self-administration or caregiver administration (refer to Section 6.2.2.3 for details). At-home self-administration is optional and available at Visit 5, Visit 7, Visit 9, Visit 11, and Visit 13. If the study visit is not planned to be conducted via telemedicine, then this visit should be conducted onsite.
trans-unit.target: Starting from Week 16 (Visit 5 of PROSPERO), and every other visit going forward, the participants who have opted in for virtual visits, completed training, and been deemed suitable for self-administration/home visits will have an opportunity to conduct their visits as a remote telemedicine option with at-home IP self-administration or caregiver administration (refer to Section 6.2.2.3 for details). At-home self-administration is optional and available at Visit 5, Visit 7, Visit 9, Visit 11, and Visit 13. If the study visit is not planned to be conducted via telemedicine, then this visit should be conducted onsite.

trans-unit.source: a The E/D Visit should be performed 12 weeks post-last dose of IP and should be performed onsite. By the time of primary reporting, IP dosing will be discontinued for all participants. As a result, some participants may conclude the study prior to finishing the full treatment duration. The participants who do not complete the full treatment period will be asked to attend the E/D visit 12 weeks post-last dose of IP. Refer to Section 7.1.1 for full details of procedures for IP discontinuation.

trans-unit.source: f The follow-up CT (optional) will be performed only in participants with a qualified baseline CT in the predecessor study (as confirmed by the imaging vendor). For participants who discontinue IP early, it is highly recommended that CT is performed at the E/D Visit instead of at Visit 14. However, it should not be performed if less than 24 weeks have elapsed since PROSPERO enrolment. The allowance for CT is ± 2 weeks.
trans-unit.target: f The follow-up CT (optional) will be performed only in participants with a qualified baseline CT in the predecessor study (as confirmed by the imaging vendor). For participants who discontinue IP early, it is highly recommended that CT is performed at the E/D Visit instead of at Visit 14. However, it should not be performed if less than 24 weeks have elapsed since PROSPERO enrolment. The allowance for CT is ± 2 weeks.

trans-unit.source: h Post-BD spirometry assessment is optional and can be skipped if it cannot be tolerated by the participant or otherwise considered not feasible.
trans-unit.target: h Post-BD spirometry assessment is optional and can be skipped if it cannot be tolerated by the participant or otherwise considered not feasible.

trans-unit.source: i Echocardiography is to be performed in participants with an echocardiogram in a predecessor study or this study. During the study, additional echocardiography assessments must be performed as soon as practical if new onset or worsening of heart failure is suspected, and may also be performed for other reasons as clinically indicated.
trans-unit.target: i Echocardiography is to be performed in participants with an echocardiogram in a predecessor study or this study. During the study, additional echocardiography assessments must be performed as soon as practical if new onset or worsening of heart failure is suspected, and may also be performed for other reasons as clinically indicated.

trans-unit.source: j FSH test performed only for female participants to confirm postmenopausal status in females aged < 50 years who have been amenorrhoeic for ≥ 12 months.
trans-unit.target: j FSH test performed only for female participants to confirm postmenopausal status in females aged < 50 years who have been amenorrhoeic for ≥ 12 months.

trans-unit.source: k The sample must be collected before administration of IP. Participants can be dosed at Visit 1 based on a negative urine pregnancy test.
trans-unit.target: k The sample must be collected before administration of IP. Participants can be dosed at Visit 1 based on a negative urine pregnancy test.

trans-unit.source: l A serum pregnancy test should be used to confirm any positive urine pregnancy tests. Participant FOCBP who are doing optional telemedicine visits with IP self- administration (starting from Visit 5, Week 16) will be provided with urine pregnancy dipstick tests and advised to perform the test prior to IP self-administration. If the urine pregnancy test result is positive, the participant should contact the site and should not administer IP. A positive urine test result must be confirmed with a serum beta-human chorionic gonadotropin.
trans-unit.target: l A serum pregnancy test should be used to confirm any positive urine pregnancy tests. Participant FOCBP who are doing optional telemedicine visits with IP self- administration (starting from Visit 5, Week 16) will be provided with urine pregnancy dipstick tests and advised to perform the test prior to IP self-administration. If the urine pregnancy test result is positive, the participant should contact the site and should not administer IP. A positive urine test result must be confirmed with a serum beta-human chorionic gonadotropin.

trans-unit.source: m Samples should be collected before administration of IP.
trans-unit.target: m Samples should be collected before administration of IP.

trans-unit.source: n IP should be administered after all other assessments have been completed at the scheduled visit. APFS will be used at the site as well as in potential at-home self or caregiver administration.
trans-unit.target: n IP should be administered after all other assessments have been completed at the scheduled visit. APFS will be used at the site as well as in potential at-home self or caregiver administration.

trans-unit.source: o Training for optional IP self-administration should be performed for applicable participants before initiation of IP self-administration. Training should be performed at least once, and more trainings may be required at the discretion of the Investigator.

trans-unit.source: a The E/D Visit should be performed 12 weeks post-last dose of IP and should be performed onsite. By the time of primary reporting, IP dosing will be discontinued for all participants. As a result, some participants may conclude the study prior to finishing the full treatment duration. The participants who do not complete the full treatment period will be asked to attend the E/D visit 12 weeks post-last dose of IP. Refer to Section 7.1.1 for full details of procedures for IP discontinuation.

trans-unit.source: l A serum pregnancy test should be used to confirm any positive urine pregnancy tests. Participant FOCBP who are doing optional telemedicine visits with IP self- administration (starting from Visit 5, Week 16) will be provided with urine pregnancy dipstick tests and advised to perform the test prior to IP self-administration. If the urine pregnancy test result is positive, the participant should contact the site and should not administer IP. A positive urine test result must be confirmed with a serum beta-human chorionic gonadotropin.

trans-unit.source: Tozorakimab is a human immunoglobulin G1 mAb that potently and specifically binds to
trans-unit.target: Tozorakimab is a human immunoglobulin G1 mAb that potently and specifically binds to

trans-unit.source: IL-33 and blocks its signalling. Tozorakimab is being developed for the treatment of asthma, COPD, diabetic kidney disease, and severe viral lung infection. A Phase II study has been conducted in atopic dermatitis, but development in this indication has since been terminated. The mechanism of action of tozorakimab makes it a potential treatment option for the high unmet need in patients with COPD experiencing COPD symptom burden and at risk for exacerbations.
trans-unit.target: IL-33 and blocks its signalling. Tozorakimab is being developed for the treatment of asthma, COPD, diabetic kidney disease, and severe viral lung infection. A Phase II study has been conducted in atopic dermatitis, but development in this indication has since been terminated. The mechanism of action of tozorakimab makes it a potential treatment option for the high unmet need in patients with COPD experiencing COPD symptom burden and at risk for exacerbations.

trans-unit.source: Tozorakimab is hypothesised to have a beneficial effect in patients with COPD by reducing the frequency and severity of COPD exacerbations and improving COPD symptoms and health-related quality of life. Furthermore, a subsequent improvement is hypothesised for lung function as well. Neutralisation of IL-33 activity with tozorakimab has potential to disrupt the cycle of inflammatory structural damage in lungs of patients with COPD.
trans-unit.target: Tozorakimab is hypothesised to have a beneficial effect in patients with COPD by reducing the frequency and severity of COPD exacerbations and improving COPD symptoms and health-related quality of life. Furthermore, a subsequent improvement is hypothesised for lung function as well. Neutralisation of IL-33 activity with tozorakimab has potential to disrupt the cycle of inflammatory structural damage in lungs of patients with COPD.

trans-unit.source: Two pivotal Phase III studies in COPD started in early 2022 (Study D9180C00003 [OBERON] and Study D9180C00004 [TITANIA]). Both studies enrol adults with symptomatic COPD and history of COPD exacerbations; each study will recruit up to 424 participants in the 300 mg Q4W and placebo treatment arms and approximately
trans-unit.target: Two pivotal Phase III studies in COPD started in early 2022 (Study D9180C00003 [OBERON] and Study D9180C00004 [TITANIA]). Both studies enrol adults with symptomatic COPD and history of COPD exacerbations; each study will recruit up to 424 participants in the 300 mg Q4W and placebo treatment arms and approximately

trans-unit.source: 212 participants in the 300 mg Q8W arm (refer to Section 9.2 for details) and treat them for a period of 52 weeks. The primary endpoint of both studies is the annualised rate of moderate to severe COPD exacerbations, analysed in the Primary Population of former smokers, followed by an analysis in the Overall Population of former or current smokers. Both studies have identical protocols but are conducted in different countries.
trans-unit.target: 212 participants in the 300 mg Q8W arm (refer to Section 9.2 for details) and treat them for a period of 52 weeks. The primary endpoint of both studies is the annualised rate of moderate to severe COPD exacerbations, analysed in the Primary Population of former smokers, followed by an analysis in the Overall Population of former or current smokers. Both studies have identical protocols but are conducted in different countries.

trans-unit.source: The purpose of this Phase III study (PROSPERO) is to continue the evaluation of long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab in adult participants with symptomatic COPD completing the predecessor studies OBERON or TITANIA.
trans-unit.target: The purpose of this Phase III study (PROSPERO) is to continue the evaluation of long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab in adult participants with symptomatic COPD completing the predecessor studies OBERON or TITANIA.

trans-unit.source: It is expected that this study, in conjunction with the predecessor studies, will provide sufficient severe exacerbation events to be able to demonstrate efficacy of tozorakimab versus placebo on top of SoC in reducing severe COPD exacerbations (time to first hospitalisation and death due to COPD). Pooled analysis with data from the predecessor studies will be performed to assess efficacy starting from randomisation in the predecessor studies, allowing for collection of greater numbers of severe COPD exacerbation events, given that rates of hospitalisation have been low since the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic (Alqahtani et al 2021). This study will also assess the long-term safety profile of tozorakimab in COPD patients to allow for further characterisation of the benefit-risk profile. The placebo cohort is included to provide a control arm, so as to enable a continued comparison of long-term efficacy, in particular the comparison of severe exacerbations rates, and to act as a comparator to contextualise safety observations. However, the size of the placebo cohort will be
trans-unit.target: It is expected that this study, in conjunction with the predecessor studies, will provide sufficient severe exacerbation events to be able to demonstrate efficacy of tozorakimab versus placebo on top of SoC in reducing severe COPD exacerbations (time to first hospitalisation and death due to COPD). Pooled analysis with data from the predecessor studies will be performed to assess efficacy starting from randomisation in the predecessor studies, allowing for collection of greater numbers of severe COPD exacerbation events, given that rates of hospitalisation have been low since the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic (Alqahtani et al 2021). This study will also assess the long-term safety profile of tozorakimab in COPD patients to allow for further characterisation of the benefit-risk profile. The placebo cohort is included to provide a control arm, so as to enable a continued comparison of long-term efficacy, in particular the comparison of severe exacerbations rates, and to act as a comparator to contextualise safety observations. However, the size of the placebo cohort will be

trans-unit.source: substantially reduced comparing to the predecessor studies: 50% of placebo participants will be re-randomised to active treatment (refer to Section 4.1 for details).

trans-unit.source: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease is the fourth leading cause of death in the world and is projected to be the third leading cause of death worldwide by 2030 (Adeloye et al 2015).
trans-unit.target: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease is the fourth leading cause of death in the world and is projected to be the third leading cause of death worldwide by 2030 (Adeloye et al 2015).

trans-unit.source: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease is characterised by persistent respiratory symptoms and airflow limitation (post-BD FEV1/FVC < 0.70) that is due to airway and/or alveolar abnormalities, usually caused by significant exposure to noxious particles or gases (where tobacco smoke is dominating), and influenced by host factors including abnormal lung development. Significant comorbidities may have an impact on morbidity and mortality (GOLD 2022). Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease is a life-threatening respiratory condition, and the disease course is characterised by a long period of disability, which results in considerable loss of health-related quality of life and restrictions in daily life (van Manen et al 2003).
trans-unit.target: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease is characterised by persistent respiratory symptoms and airflow limitation (post-BD FEV1/FVC < 0.70) that is due to airway and/or alveolar abnormalities, usually caused by significant exposure to noxious particles or gases (where tobacco smoke is dominating), and influenced by host factors including abnormal lung development. Significant comorbidities may have an impact on morbidity and mortality (GOLD 2022). Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease is a life-threatening respiratory condition, and the disease course is characterised by a long period of disability, which results in considerable loss of health-related quality of life and restrictions in daily life (van Manen et al 2003).

trans-unit.source: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease is not fully reversible, usually progressive, and associated with an enhanced chronic inflammatory response in the lung. There is an increasing evidence base demonstrating that the overall symptomatic burden has a substantial detrimental impact on health-related quality of life and also contributes to increased risk of exacerbations and a worse disease prognosis (Miravitlles and Ribera 2017).
trans-unit.target: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease is not fully reversible, usually progressive, and associated with an enhanced chronic inflammatory response in the lung. There is an increasing evidence base demonstrating that the overall symptomatic burden has a substantial detrimental impact on health-related quality of life and also contributes to increased risk of exacerbations and a worse disease prognosis (Miravitlles and Ribera 2017).

trans-unit.source: Acute exacerbations of COPD are episodes of symptom worsening that have significant adverse consequences for patients (Wedzicha and Seemungal 2007). Greater frequency of exacerbations is associated with accelerated lung function decline, health-related quality of life impairment, and increased mortality (Donaldson et al 2002, Seemungal et al 1998, Soler- Cataluna et al 2005). Furthermore, as the incidence of COPD increases, exacerbations place a greater burden on health care systems, accounting for more than 10 million unscheduled attendances per year in the United States (Mannino and Braman 2007). The direct costs of COPD treatment in the United States are greater than $32 billion per year, with exacerbations estimated to account for 50% to 75% of these healthcare costs (Celli et al 2004, Guarascio et al 2013, Toy et al 2010). Exacerbations are also important outcome measures in COPD, with acute treatment targeting accelerated recovery, whereas long-term maintenance inhaled therapy is designed to prevent and reduce their frequency and severity (Ritchie and Wedzicha 2020).
trans-unit.target: Acute exacerbations of COPD are episodes of symptom worsening that have significant adverse consequences for patients (Wedzicha and Seemungal 2007). Greater frequency of exacerbations is associated with accelerated lung function decline, health-related quality of life impairment, and increased mortality (Donaldson et al 2002, Seemungal et al 1998, Soler- Cataluna et al 2005). Furthermore, as the incidence of COPD increases, exacerbations place a greater burden on health care systems, accounting for more than 10 million unscheduled attendances per year in the United States (Mannino and Braman 2007). The direct costs of COPD treatment in the United States are greater than $32 billion per year, with exacerbations estimated to account for 50% to 75% of these healthcare costs (Celli et al 2004, Guarascio et al 2013, Toy et al 2010). Exacerbations are also important outcome measures in COPD, with acute treatment targeting accelerated recovery, whereas long-term maintenance inhaled therapy is designed to prevent and reduce their frequency and severity (Ritchie and Wedzicha 2020).

trans-unit.source: Despite adequate treatment with optimised maintenance inhaled therapy, approximately 30% to 40% of patients continue to have moderate or severe exacerbations (Müllerová et al 2017, Vestbo et al 2017). Severe exacerbations of COPD worsen the course of COPD disease and involve a rapid decline in health status, disease progression, and high mortality, with each severe COPD exacerbation increasing the risk of further exacerbations (Wedzicha et al 2013),
trans-unit.target: Despite adequate treatment with optimised maintenance inhaled therapy, approximately 30% to 40% of patients continue to have moderate or severe exacerbations (Müllerová et al 2017, Vestbo et al 2017). Severe exacerbations of COPD worsen the course of COPD disease and involve a rapid decline in health status, disease progression, and high mortality, with each severe COPD exacerbation increasing the risk of further exacerbations (Wedzicha et al 2013),

trans-unit.source: re-hospitalisation (Myers et al 2020), and death (Suissa 2012). Even maximal triple therapy (LABA + LAMA + ICS) may still be insufficient to prevent all such events (Rabe et al 2020); hence, a substantial unmet medical need remains.
trans-unit.target: re-hospitalisation (Myers et al 2020), and death (Suissa 2012). Even maximal triple therapy (LABA + LAMA + ICS) may still be insufficient to prevent all such events (Rabe et al 2020); hence, a substantial unmet medical need remains.

trans-unit.source: Tozorakimab is a fully human IgG1 mAb that binds to IL-33 and potently and specifically blocks all forms of IL-33 to prevent their signalling (Kearley et al 2015). By binding to the reduced form of IL-33 (IL-33red), tozorakimab potently inhibits ST2-dependent inflammation responses in several primary human cells and in an allergen-driven in vivo model of COPD. The oxidised form of IL-33 (IL-33ox), which cannot bind ST2 (Cohen et al 2015), signals via the RAGE/EGFR pathway (Scott et al 2022, Strickson et al 2022). Tozorakimab cannot bind IL-33ox but can prevent the oxidation of IL-33 and IL-33ox-dependent signalling via the RAGE/EGFR complex and mimic the action of soluble ST2. Inhibition of IL-33ox signalling by tozorakimab can improve airway epithelial repair functions and reverse airway epithelial dysfunction in respiratory disease, including mucous hypersecretion (Strickson et al 2022, Scott et al 2022).
trans-unit.target: Tozorakimab is a fully human IgG1 mAb that binds to IL-33 and potently and specifically blocks all forms of IL-33 to prevent their signalling (Kearley et al 2015). By binding to the reduced form of IL-33 (IL-33red), tozorakimab potently inhibits ST2-dependent inflammation responses in several primary human cells and in an allergen-driven in vivo model of COPD. The oxidised form of IL-33 (IL-33ox), which cannot bind ST2 (Cohen et al 2015), signals via the RAGE/EGFR pathway (Scott et al 2022, Strickson et al 2022). Tozorakimab cannot bind IL-33ox but can prevent the oxidation of IL-33 and IL-33ox-dependent signalling via the RAGE/EGFR complex and mimic the action of soluble ST2. Inhibition of IL-33ox signalling by tozorakimab can improve airway epithelial repair functions and reverse airway epithelial dysfunction in respiratory disease, including mucous hypersecretion (Strickson et al 2022, Scott et al 2022).

trans-unit.source: Neutralisation of IL‑33 may disrupt the cycle of inflammatory damage to allow epithelial recovery. Recent clinical data reveal a significant beneficial effect of inhibiting IL-33 on reducing moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations on top of optimised inhaled therapy (Rabe et al 2021).
trans-unit.target: Neutralisation of IL‑33 may disrupt the cycle of inflammatory damage to allow epithelial recovery. Recent clinical data reveal a significant beneficial effect of inhibiting IL-33 on reducing moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations on top of optimised inhaled therapy (Rabe et al 2021).

trans-unit.source: A detailed description of the chemistry, pharmacology, mechanism of action, and safety of tozorakimab is provided in the IB.
trans-unit.target: A detailed description of the chemistry, pharmacology, mechanism of action, and safety of tozorakimab is provided in the IB.

trans-unit.source: In order to evaluate the clinical benefit/risk balance for tozorakimab, nonclinical and clinical data were taken into consideration, as well as review of the available information for mAbs that are in development for the treatment of COPD.
trans-unit.target: In order to evaluate the clinical benefit/risk balance for tozorakimab, nonclinical and clinical data were taken into consideration, as well as review of the available information for mAbs that are in development for the treatment of COPD.

trans-unit.source: More detailed information about the known and expected benefits and potential risks of tozorakimab may be found in the IB.

trans-unit.source: Potential risks of tozorakimab are the following (refer to the IB for further details):
trans-unit.target: Potential risks of tozorakimab are the following (refer to the IB for further details):

trans-unit.source: The IL-33/ST2 axis is involved in the host response to pathogens, however, the relationship between IL-33 suppression and serious infection has not been fully established. Therefore, serious infections, including opportunistic infections, are considered a theoretical risk of tozorakimab. Risk minimisation measures include exclusion of participants in a predecessor study with conditions predisposing to infections (eg, known primary or secondary immune deficiency syndromes) or recent severe infections. No significant increase in the incidence of serious or opportunistic infections
trans-unit.target: The IL-33/ST2 axis is involved in the host response to pathogens, however, the relationship between IL-33 suppression and serious infection has not been fully established. Therefore, serious infections, including opportunistic infections, are considered a theoretical risk of tozorakimab. Risk minimisation measures include exclusion of participants in a predecessor study with conditions predisposing to infections (eg, known primary or secondary immune deficiency syndromes) or recent severe infections. No significant increase in the incidence of serious or opportunistic infections

trans-unit.source: with tozorakimab has been observed in the Phase I study or Phase II studies reported to date in atopic dermatitis and asthma.
trans-unit.target: with tozorakimab has been observed in the Phase I study or Phase II studies reported to date in atopic dermatitis and asthma.

trans-unit.source: Note: The Sponsor is not aware of any current evidence of a direct link between IL-33, or IL33 suppression, and contracting SARS-CoV-2 and early stages- of COVID-19 illness post-infection. It has been hypothesised that IL-33 suppression might be beneficial in the later, hyperinflammatory phase of severe COVID-19 illness (Stanczak et al 2021). Any additional infection control measures in accordance with COVID-19-related local and national guidelines should also be followed.
trans-unit.target: Note: The Sponsor is not aware of any current evidence of a direct link between IL-33, or IL33 suppression, and contracting SARS-CoV-2 and early stages- of COVID-19 illness post-infection. It has been hypothesised that IL-33 suppression might be beneficial in the later, hyperinflammatory phase of severe COVID-19 illness (Stanczak et al 2021). Any additional infection control measures in accordance with COVID-19-related local and national guidelines should also be followed.

trans-unit.source: Progression of heart failure is considered a theoretical potential risk of tozorakimab. Preclinical data exist for both protective and detrimental effect of IL-33/ST2 pathway in animal models of heart failure and endothelial dysfunction (Parikh et al 2016). Individuals that express an IL-33 loss-of-function variant (rs146597587-C) do not appear to have an increased risk of developing heart failure in 3 separate cohorts (unpublished observations of the Sponsor), and homozygotes for this rare IL-33 variant do not show reduced life expectancy nor reduced fertility (Smith et al 2017). There were no cases of heart failure in the completed Phase I study of tozorakimab Q2W or the final analyses of the Phase II studies evaluating the use of tozorakimab for atopic dermatitis and asthma. Mitigation of progression of heart failure progression risk includes exclusion of participants with any unstable cardiovascular disease, heart failure of NYHA class III-IV, and/or LVEF < 30% in the predecessor studies, as well as echocardiography monitoring in participants with heart failure of NYHA class I-II and/or high risk, ECG and NT-proBNP monitoring throughout the study.
trans-unit.target: Progression of heart failure is considered a theoretical potential risk of tozorakimab. Preclinical data exist for both protective and detrimental effect of IL-33/ST2 pathway in animal models of heart failure and endothelial dysfunction (Parikh et al 2016). Individuals that express an IL-33 loss-of-function variant (rs146597587-C) do not appear to have an increased risk of developing heart failure in 3 separate cohorts (unpublished observations of the Sponsor), and homozygotes for this rare IL-33 variant do not show reduced life expectancy nor reduced fertility (Smith et al 2017). There were no cases of heart failure in the completed Phase I study of tozorakimab Q2W or the final analyses of the Phase II studies evaluating the use of tozorakimab for atopic dermatitis and asthma. Mitigation of progression of heart failure progression risk includes exclusion of participants with any unstable cardiovascular disease, heart failure of NYHA class III-IV, and/or LVEF < 30% in the predecessor studies, as well as echocardiography monitoring in participants with heart failure of NYHA class I-II and/or high risk, ECG and NT-proBNP monitoring throughout the study.

trans-unit.source: Serious hypersensitivity (including Type 1 to 4 hypersensitivity reaction): As mAbs are foreign proteins, they have the potential to provoke hypersensitivity reactions. In order to mitigate the risk of serious hypersensitivity reactions, participants are excluded from the predecessor study if they have a history of severe allergic reaction requiring hospitalisation.
trans-unit.target: Serious hypersensitivity (including Type 1 to 4 hypersensitivity reaction): As mAbs are foreign proteins, they have the potential to provoke hypersensitivity reactions. In order to mitigate the risk of serious hypersensitivity reactions, participants are excluded from the predecessor study if they have a history of severe allergic reaction requiring hospitalisation.

trans-unit.source: Injection-site reactions are a potential risk based on the SC route of administration and the nature of tozorakimab as an exogenous protein substance. Mitigations include instructions to the site personnel on injection technique, rotation of injection sites, and routine injection site assessment at each visit.
trans-unit.target: Injection-site reactions are a potential risk based on the SC route of administration and the nature of tozorakimab as an exogenous protein substance. Mitigations include instructions to the site personnel on injection technique, rotation of injection sites, and routine injection site assessment at each visit.

trans-unit.source: An incidental finding of an intestinal AE (colitis) was observed in the long-term toxicity preclinical study of tozorakimab. To mitigate any potential risks, participants with recent history of active inflammatory bowel diseases are excluded from the predecessor study. Gastrointestinal AEs are subject to close safety monitoring during the study.
trans-unit.target: An incidental finding of an intestinal AE (colitis) was observed in the long-term toxicity preclinical study of tozorakimab. To mitigate any potential risks, participants with recent history of active inflammatory bowel diseases are excluded from the predecessor study. Gastrointestinal AEs are subject to close safety monitoring during the study.

trans-unit.source: Risks of the study procedures are as follows:
trans-unit.target: Risks of the study procedures are as follows:

trans-unit.source: Forced spirometry may generate aerosols with risk of spreading respiratory infectious disease. The risk would be to staff and other participants nearby, rather than the participant undergoing the procedure. Usual infection control measures should be followed during procedures, including use of filters during forced spirometry, per the instructions of the central vendor. Any additional infection control measures in accordance with COVID-19-related local and national guidelines should also be followed.
trans-unit.target: Forced spirometry may generate aerosols with risk of spreading respiratory infectious disease. The risk would be to staff and other participants nearby, rather than the participant undergoing the procedure. Usual infection control measures should be followed during procedures, including use of filters during forced spirometry, per the instructions of the central vendor. Any additional infection control measures in accordance with COVID-19-related local and national guidelines should also be followed.

trans-unit.source: The ionising radiation from low dose CT may cause a very small increased risk of developing cancer in participants who also may have an elevated risk of cancer due to smoking history. Participants will be informed of the risks associated with CT before entering the study, and not every participant in the study will receive a CT (Section 8.1.4). In keeping with the ALARA concept, (As Low as Reasonably Achievable) the trial recommends using optimised CT scan protocols, using the lowest possible radiation dose to each participant in order to provide acceptable image quality for the quantitative measurements performed in the study. For more detailed information related to CT radiation dose, imaging risks, and best imaging practices, please refer to the study Radiology Manual.

Participants will be informed of the risks associated with CT before entering the study, and not every participant in the study will receive a CT (Section 8.1.4). In keeping with the ALARA concept, (As Low as Reasonably Achievable) the trial recommends using optimised CT scan protocols, using the lowest possible radiation dose to each participant in order to provide acceptable image quality for the quantitative measurements performed in the study. For more detailed information related to CT radiation dose, imaging risks, and best imaging practices, please refer to the study Radiology Manual.

trans-unit.source: Risk minimisation measures include the exclusion of participants in a predecessor study and this study with allergy or reaction to any component of the IP formulation, active or recent malignancy, unstable cardiovascular comorbidities, inflammatory bowel disease, and active significant infection, in conjunction with the performance of routine pharmacovigilance activities. Additionally, an unblinded IDMC and a blinded Medical Monitor will perform regular evaluations of safety data from this study (Section 9.6). Additionally, an IDMC will perform regular evaluations of safety data throughout the predecessor studies.
trans-unit.target: Risk minimisation measures include the exclusion of participants in a predecessor study and this study with allergy or reaction to any component of the IP formulation, active or recent malignancy, unstable cardiovascular comorbidities, inflammatory bowel disease, and active significant infection, in conjunction with the performance of routine pharmacovigilance activities. Additionally, an unblinded IDMC and a blinded Medical Monitor will perform regular evaluations of safety data from this study (Section 9.6). Additionally, an IDMC will perform regular evaluations of safety data throughout the predecessor studies.

trans-unit.source: The purpose of this Phase III study is to evaluate the long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab in participants with symptomatic COPD who completed the treatment period in predecessor studies OBERON or TITANIA. Participants will be required to continue on stable doses of their COPD maintenance therapy throughout the study to minimise the risk of disease worsening during the study and will receive medically indicated treatment for COPD exacerbations. For participants randomised to tozorakimab, there is a potential benefit in terms of reduction in COPD exacerbations, improvement in COPD symptoms and health-related quality of life. A recent Phase II clinical trial showed that treatment with itepekimab (formerly REGN3500/SAR440340), a competitor mAb binding IL-33 with a very similar mechanism of action to tozorakimab, resulted in a marked reduction of COPD exacerbations as well as a significant improvement of FEV1 in former smokers (Rabe et al 2021). While the clinical effect of tozorakimab in patients with COPD is yet to be determined, it is conceivable that treatment with tozorakimab will lead to similar improvement. However, it is possible that participants will not receive any individual benefit from participating in this study.
trans-unit.target: The purpose of this Phase III study is to evaluate the long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab in participants with symptomatic COPD who completed the treatment period in predecessor studies OBERON or TITANIA. Participants will be required to continue on stable doses of their COPD maintenance therapy throughout the study to minimise the risk of disease worsening during the study and will receive medically indicated treatment for COPD exacerbations. For participants randomised to tozorakimab, there is a potential benefit in terms of reduction in COPD exacerbations, improvement in COPD symptoms and health-related quality of life. A recent Phase II clinical trial showed that treatment with itepekimab (formerly REGN3500/SAR440340), a competitor mAb binding IL-33 with a very similar mechanism of action to tozorakimab, resulted in a marked reduction of COPD exacerbations as well as a significant improvement of FEV1 in former smokers (Rabe et al 2021). While the clinical effect of tozorakimab in patients with COPD is yet to be determined, it is conceivable that treatment with tozorakimab will lead to similar improvement. However, it is possible that participants will not receive any individual benefit from participating in this study.

trans-unit.source: As of May 2023, an estimated 1500 participants have been dosed with tozorakimab across multiple indications. Safety data collected to date, including data from the Phase II studies of tozorakimab in asthma and atopic dermatitis, support the favourable safety profile of tozorakimab for all dose regimens evaluated in the PROSPERO study.
trans-unit.target: As of May 2023, an estimated 1500 participants have been dosed with tozorakimab across multiple indications. Safety data collected to date, including data from the Phase II studies of tozorakimab in asthma and atopic dermatitis, support the favourable safety profile of tozorakimab for all dose regimens evaluated in the PROSPERO study.

trans-unit.source: Taking into account the measures taken to minimise risk to participants in this study, the benefit/risk assessment for tozorakimab in participants with symptomatic COPD and a history of exacerbations appears favourable.
trans-unit.target: Taking into account the measures taken to minimise risk to participants in this study, the benefit/risk assessment for tozorakimab in participants with symptomatic COPD and a history of exacerbations appears favourable.

trans-unit.source: Under CSP Version 1.0: The placebo arm will be re-randomised in a 1:1:2 ratio (tozorakimab Q4W, tozorakimab Q8W, and matching placebo, respectively).

trans-unit.source: Under CSP Version 2.0: the placebo arm will be re-randomised in a 1:1 ratio (tozorakimab Q4W and matching placebo, respectively) and participants in the tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm will switch to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W. Those participants who are already randomised under CSP Version 1.0 can continue to complete the study with the same treatment regimen as assigned and for a treatment period of 52 weeks after this amendment.

trans-unit.source: This is a Phase III, multicentre, randomised, double-blind, chronic-dosing, parallel-group, placebo-controlled extension study to evaluate the long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W and 300 mg Q8W administered SC, in participants with COPD with history of COPD exacerbations.
trans-unit.target: This is a Phase III, multicentre, randomised, double-blind, chronic-dosing, parallel-group, placebo-controlled extension study to evaluate the long-term efficacy and safety of tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W and 300 mg Q8W administered SC, in participants with COPD with history of COPD exacerbations.

trans-unit.source: Eligible participants must have completed the treatment period and have not prematurely discontinued from IP in the predecessor studies OBERON or TITANIA and fulfil the inclusion/exclusion criteria. Participants will be encouraged to continue on the same stable COPD maintenance therapy throughout the study.
trans-unit.target: Eligible participants must have completed the treatment period and have not prematurely discontinued from IP in the predecessor studies OBERON or TITANIA and fulfil the inclusion/exclusion criteria. Participants will be encouraged to continue on the same stable COPD maintenance therapy throughout the study.

trans-unit.source: It is anticipated that PROSPERO will enrol up to 2120 participants.
trans-unit.target: It is anticipated that PROSPERO will enrol up to 2120 participants.

trans-unit.source: The study design is updated in this amendment; participants will therefore be enrolled in the study prior to the amendment under CSP version 1.0 or under this version (Version 2.0).
trans-unit.target: The study design is updated in this amendment; participants will therefore be enrolled in the study prior to the amendment under CSP version 1.0 or under this version (Version 2.0).

trans-unit.source: Participants previously randomised to tozorakimab in either of the predecessor studies will remain on the same active treatment and dosing regimen in PROSPERO. Participants previously randomised to the placebo arm in the predecessor study will be re-randomised in a 1:1:2 ratio to receive either tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W, tozorakimab 300mg Q8W, or placebo on top of SoC.
trans-unit.target: Participants previously randomised to tozorakimab in either of the predecessor studies will remain on the same active treatment and dosing regimen in PROSPERO. Participants previously randomised to the placebo arm in the predecessor study will be re-randomised in a 1:1:2 ratio to receive either tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W, tozorakimab 300mg Q8W, or placebo on top of SoC.

trans-unit.source: The study will consist of an Enrolment Visit, preferably on the same day as Visit 15 of the predecessor study, and a 52-week treatment period in which IP will be administered Q4W. The last dose of IP will be administered at Week 48 and the end of treatment assessments will be performed at Week 52. Participants will enter the 8-week post-treatment follow-up, with the final follow-up visit at Week 60 (12 weeks after the last dose of IP).
trans-unit.target: The study will consist of an Enrolment Visit, preferably on the same day as Visit 15 of the predecessor study, and a 52-week treatment period in which IP will be administered Q4W. The last dose of IP will be administered at Week 48 and the end of treatment assessments will be performed at Week 52. Participants will enter the 8-week post-treatment follow-up, with the final follow-up visit at Week 60 (12 weeks after the last dose of IP).

trans-unit.source: Participants previously randomised to the placebo arm in the predecessor studies will be re- randomised in a 1:1 ratio to receive either tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W or placebo on top of SoC in PROSPERO. Participants previously randomised to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W in the predecessor studies will remain on tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W. Participants previously randomised to tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W in the predecessor studies will switch to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W at the point of entry to PROSPERO. Those participants who are already randomised under CSP Version 1.0 can continue to complete the study with the same treatment regimen as assigned and for a treatment period of 52 weeks after this amendment.

Participants previously randomised to tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W in the predecessor studies will switch to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W at the point of entry to PROSPERO. Those participants who are already randomised under CSP Version 1.0 can continue to complete the study with the same treatment regimen as assigned and for a treatment period of 52 weeks after this amendment.

trans-unit.source: The study will consist of an Enrolment Visit, preferably on the same day as Visit 15 of the predecessor study, and a 28-week treatment period in which IP will be administered Q4W. The last dose of IP will be administered at Week 24 and the end of treatment assessments will be performed at Week 28. Participants will enter the 8-week post-treatment follow-up, with the final follow-up visit at Week 36 (12 weeks after the last dose of IP).
