The last dose of IP will be administered at Week 24 and the end of treatment assessments will be performed at Week 28. Participants will enter the 8-week post-treatment follow-up, with the final follow-up visit at Week 36 (12 weeks after the last dose of IP).

trans-unit.source: The placebo cohort is included to provide a control arm, so as to enable a continued comparison of long-term efficacy, in particular the comparison of severe exacerbations rates, and to act as a comparator to contextualise safety observations.
trans-unit.target: The placebo cohort is included to provide a control arm, so as to enable a continued comparison of long-term efficacy, in particular the comparison of severe exacerbations rates, and to act as a comparator to contextualise safety observations.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor reserves the right to extend the PROSPERO study or to terminate it early depending on the overall development programme.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor reserves the right to extend the PROSPERO study or to terminate it early depending on the overall development programme.

trans-unit.source: The primary reporting of the study may occur once both predecessor studies have been unblinded and reported without waiting for all participants to complete treatment in this study. To ensure consistency with reporting PROSPERO and its predecessor studies, some participants may not complete the full 28-week treatment period. By the time of PROSPERO’s primary reporting, IP dosing will be discontinued for all participants. As a result, some participants may conclude the study prior to finishing the full treatment duration. Participants will be made aware of this during the consent process. The participants who do not complete the full treatment period will be asked to attend the E/D visit, scheduled
trans-unit.target: The primary reporting of the study may occur once both predecessor studies have been unblinded and reported without waiting for all participants to complete treatment in this study. To ensure consistency with reporting PROSPERO and its predecessor studies, some participants may not complete the full 28-week treatment period. By the time of PROSPERO’s primary reporting, IP dosing will be discontinued for all participants. As a result, some participants may conclude the study prior to finishing the full treatment duration. Participants will be made aware of this during the consent process. The participants who do not complete the full treatment period will be asked to attend the E/D visit, scheduled

trans-unit.source: 12 weeks after their final IP dose, to complete their participation.
trans-unit.target: 12 weeks after their final IP dose, to complete their participation.

trans-unit.source: Once the blind is broken in the predecessor studies, the Sponsor will be unblinded to which participants received active tozorakimab in those studies. As such until the primary analysis of PROSPERO, the study team in direct contact with sites or with influence over management of participants or recording of endpoints in PROSPERO will remain blinded and be separate from the team involved in reporting of the predecessor studies. The study conduct and blinding at the site and for the participants will remain unchanged.
trans-unit.target: Once the blind is broken in the predecessor studies, the Sponsor will be unblinded to which participants received active tozorakimab in those studies. As such until the primary analysis of PROSPERO, the study team in direct contact with sites or with influence over management of participants or recording of endpoints in PROSPERO will remain blinded and be separate from the team involved in reporting of the predecessor studies. The study conduct and blinding at the site and for the participants will remain unchanged.

trans-unit.source: An IDMC will be established to oversee the safety of the study participants and make appropriate recommendations regarding further study conduct based on the available data, including regular review of safety data and at planned interim analyses where the opportunity to report on the study early for overwhelming efficacy may be considered. The committee will operate in accordance with an IDMC charter.
trans-unit.target: An IDMC will be established to oversee the safety of the study participants and make appropriate recommendations regarding further study conduct based on the available data, including regular review of safety data and at planned interim analyses where the opportunity to report on the study early for overwhelming efficacy may be considered. The committee will operate in accordance with an IDMC charter.

trans-unit.source: An Independent Adjudication Committee will provide an independent, external, systematic, and unbiased assessment of heart failure events (worsening and new onset), MACE, and all AEs with fatal outcome.
trans-unit.target: An Independent Adjudication Committee will provide an independent, external, systematic, and unbiased assessment of heart failure events (worsening and new onset), MACE, and all AEs with fatal outcome.

trans-unit.source: Visit 1 of PROSPERO will be conducted on the same day as the last scheduled visit of the treatment period in the predecessor study (Visit 15, Week 52). If this is not feasible, Visit 1 of PROSPERO may be conducted up to 12 weeks after the last dose of IP in the predecessor study (scheduled at Visit 14, Week 48), provided that the participant has remained in predecessor study follow-up during this time. In these circumstances, the Sponsor Medical Monitor should be contacted. Visit 1 of PROSPERO will not, under any circumstances, occur later than the time for Visit 16 (Week 60) of the predecessor study.
trans-unit.target: Visit 1 of PROSPERO will be conducted on the same day as the last scheduled visit of the treatment period in the predecessor study (Visit 15, Week 52). If this is not feasible, Visit 1 of PROSPERO may be conducted up to 12 weeks after the last dose of IP in the predecessor study (scheduled at Visit 14, Week 48), provided that the participant has remained in predecessor study follow-up during this time. In these circumstances, the Sponsor Medical Monitor should be contacted. Visit 1 of PROSPERO will not, under any circumstances, occur later than the time for Visit 16 (Week 60) of the predecessor study.

trans-unit.source: In order to provide participants time to consider participation in this study and to minimise the risk of an interrupted dosing regimen when participants transition between the predecessor study and this study, participants may be provided with the ICF at Week 48 (Visit 14) of the
trans-unit.target: In order to provide participants time to consider participation in this study and to minimise the risk of an interrupted dosing regimen when participants transition between the predecessor study and this study, participants may be provided with the ICF at Week 48 (Visit 14) of the

trans-unit.source: predecessor study and will be asked to sign the ICF of PROSPERO study prior to any study- specific procedures for PROSPERO being performed.
trans-unit.target: predecessor study and will be asked to sign the ICF of PROSPERO study prior to any study- specific procedures for PROSPERO being performed.

trans-unit.source: Procedures of Visit 15 (Week 52) of the predecessor study should be completed in the predecessor study before initiating PROSPERO. If Visit 1 of PROSPERO is not performed on the same day as the predecessor study Visit 15 (Week 52), any new information about AEs, SAEs, COPD exacerbations, concomitant medications, smoking, and supplemental O2 status, and HRU will be collected, and urine pregnancy test (FOCBP only) will be repeated prior to dosing. In cases when Visit 1 of PROSPERO is delayed beyond 4 weeks after Visit 15 of the predecessor study, safety-related assessments (physical exam, ECG, vital signs, SpO2, safety laboratory tests, PK/ADA, etc) will also be repeated prior to dosing as described in the SoA (Section 1.3).
trans-unit.target: Procedures of Visit 15 (Week 52) of the predecessor study should be completed in the predecessor study before initiating PROSPERO. In cases when Visit 1 of PROSPERO is delayed beyond 4 weeks after Visit 15 of the predecessor study, safety-related assessments (physical exam, ECG, vital signs, SpO2, safety laboratory tests, PK/ADA, etc) will also be repeated prior to dosing as described in the SoA (Section 1.3).

trans-unit.source: Before randomisation and first IP administration in PROSPERO, all relevant data, including, but not limited to, AEs, SAEs, concomitant medication, HRU, and COPD exacerbations will be collected as part of the predecessor study and should be recorded and up to date in the predecessor study. Information about AEs, SAEs, concomitant medications, and COPD exacerbations ongoing at time of entry to PROSPERO will be recorded in the PROSPERO study eCRF.

Information about AEs, SAEs, concomitant medications, and COPD exacerbations ongoing at time of entry to PROSPERO will be recorded in the PROSPERO study eCRF.

trans-unit.source: The study information, such as medical history and demographics, are to be generally collected in the predecessor studies and will be additionally collected in PROSPERO as needed.
trans-unit.target: The study information, such as medical history and demographics, are to be generally collected in the predecessor studies and will be additionally collected in PROSPERO as needed.

trans-unit.source: Participants who do meet the eligibility criteria for PROSPERO and are willing to enter this study will exit the predecessor study and receive the first dose in PROSPERO at Visit 1; otherwise, participants will continue in the predecessor study follow-up period until completion (refer to the respective predecessor study protocol).

trans-unit.source: The participants who have signed the ICF and meet the eligibility criteria should be registered in a RTSM system on Visit 1. The participant will keep the same e-code as in the predecessor study.
trans-unit.target: The participants who have signed the ICF and meet the eligibility criteria should be registered in a RTSM system on Visit 1. The participant will keep the same e-code as in the predecessor study.

trans-unit.source: Randomisation and first IP dosing in PROSPERO must occur on the same day. The complete list of Visit 1 procedures is provided in the SoA (Section 1.3).

trans-unit.source: The study design is updated in this amendment; participants will therefore be enrolled in the study prior to the amendment under CSP Version 1.0 or under this version (Version 2.0) (Figure 2).
trans-unit.target: The study design is updated in this amendment; participants will therefore be enrolled in the study prior to the amendment under CSP Version 1.0 or under this version (Version 2.0) (Figure 2).

trans-unit.source: Participants previously randomised to the placebo arm in the predecessor study will be re- randomised in a 1:1:2 ratio to receive either tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W, tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W, or placebo on top of SoC. The treatment period is 52 weeks.

trans-unit.source: Participants will attend scheduled clinic visits Q4W to complete protocol-specific assessments listed in the SoA (Section 1.3). Unscheduled visits may be initiated as needed, and assessments performed at these visits are at the discretion of the Investigator.

trans-unit.source: During treatment period, participants and/or caregivers will first receive trainings for self- administration of IP, if applicable (Section 6.2.2.3). Starting from Visit 5, participants may attend study visits remotely with self-administration of IP at every other visit (see Section 1.3 for details).

trans-unit.source: The Investigator must evaluate the participant to ensure the participant/caregiver is capable of performing self-administration of IP (refer to separate self-administration instructions). At- home self-administration is optional: If the study visit is not planned to be conducted via telemedicine, then this visit should be conducted onsite.

trans-unit.source: Participants will be encouraged to continue on the same COPD maintenance therapy throughout the study (Section 6.5.2). In case of a COPD worsening/exacerbation
trans-unit.target: Participants will be encouraged to continue on the same COPD maintenance therapy throughout the study (Section 6.5.2). In case of a COPD worsening/exacerbation

trans-unit.source: (Section 8.1.1), participants should be evaluated at the study site at an unscheduled visit, when feasible, or a scheduled visit, if the worsening happens to fall within a scheduled visit window.
trans-unit.target: (Section 8.1.1), participants should be evaluated at the study site at an unscheduled visit, when feasible, or a scheduled visit, if the worsening happens to fall within a scheduled visit window.

trans-unit.source: Participants who prematurely discontinue IP should return to the study site and complete the E/D visit procedures 12 weeks after the last IP dose, or as soon as feasible after 12 weeks have passed from the last dose of IP (refer to Section 1.3 and Section 7.1.1). In cases when the

In cases when the

trans-unit.source: participant is not able or not willing to return to the site for the E/D visits 12 weeks after the last IP dose, E/D visit assessments can be performed earlier than this, at a time point feasible for the participant.

trans-unit.source: Completion or E/D of IP will be registered via RTSM for each participant.

trans-unit.source: Study Conduct Mitigation During Study Disruptions Due to Cases of Civil Crisis, Natural Disaster, or Public Health Crisis
trans-unit.target: Study Conduct Mitigation During Study Disruptions Due to Cases of Civil Crisis, Natural Disaster, or Public Health Crisis

trans-unit.source: The guidance given below supersedes instructions provided elsewhere in this CSP and should be implemented only during cases of civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis (eg, during quarantines and resulting site closures, regional travel restrictions, and considerations if site personnel or study participants become infected with SARS-CoV-2 or similar pandemic infection) which would prevent the conduct of study-related activities at study sites, thereby compromising the study site staff or the participant’s ability to conduct the study. The Investigator or designee should contact the study Sponsor to discuss whether the mitigation plans below should be implemented.
trans-unit.target: The guidance given below supersedes instructions provided elsewhere in this CSP and should be implemented only during cases of civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis (eg, during quarantines and resulting site closures, regional travel restrictions, and considerations if site personnel or study participants become infected with SARS-CoV-2 or similar pandemic infection) which would prevent the conduct of study-related activities at study sites, thereby compromising the study site staff or the participant’s ability to conduct the study. The Investigator or designee should contact the study Sponsor to discuss whether the mitigation plans below should be implemented.

trans-unit.source: To ensure continuity of the clinical study during a civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis, changes may be implemented to ensure the safety of study participants, maintain compliance with GCP, and minimise risks to study integrity.
trans-unit.target: To ensure continuity of the clinical study during a civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis, changes may be implemented to ensure the safety of study participants, maintain compliance with GCP, and minimise risks to study integrity.

trans-unit.source: Where allowable by local health authorities, ethics committees, healthcare provider guidelines (eg, hospital policies), or local government, these changes may include the following options:
trans-unit.target: Where allowable by local health authorities, ethics committees, healthcare provider guidelines (eg, hospital policies), or local government, these changes may include the following options:

trans-unit.source: Obtaining consent/reconsent for the mitigation procedures (note, in the case of verbal consent/reconsent, the ICF should be signed as soon as feasible [ie, at the participant’s next visit]).
trans-unit.target: Obtaining consent/reconsent for the mitigation procedures (note, in the case of verbal consent/reconsent, the ICF should be signed as soon as feasible [ie, at the participant’s next visit]).

trans-unit.source: Home visit or visit in remote location: performed by a site qualified HCP or HCP provided by a TPV.
trans-unit.target: Home visit or visit in remote location: performed by a site qualified HCP or HCP provided by a TPV.

trans-unit.source: Telemedicine visit: remote contact with the participants using telecommunications technology including phone calls, virtual or video visits, and mobile health devices.
trans-unit.target: Telemedicine visit: remote contact with the participants using telecommunications technology including phone calls, virtual or video visits, and mobile health devices.

trans-unit.source: At-home IP self-administration: performed by a site qualified HCP, HCP provided by a TPV, if possible, or the participant/caregiver. Additional information related to the visit can be obtained via telemedicine.
trans-unit.target: At-home IP self-administration: performed by a site qualified HCP, HCP provided by a TPV, if possible, or the participant/caregiver. Additional information related to the visit can be obtained via telemedicine.

trans-unit.source: Investigators, blinded Sponsors and investigator site staff, and study participants should remain blinded throughout the course of the study including the aforementioned mitigation procedures. For further details on study conduct during civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis, refer to Appendix H.
trans-unit.target: Investigators, blinded Sponsors and investigator site staff, and study participants should remain blinded throughout the course of the study including the aforementioned mitigation procedures. For further details on study conduct during civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis, refer to Appendix H.

trans-unit.source: Rationale for Selection of Participant Population
trans-unit.target: Rationale for Selection of Participant Population

trans-unit.source: The study population for the predecessor studies were identified based on tozorakimab mechanism of action, unmet need in patients experiencing COPD symptom burden and at risk for exacerbations, and outcomes of recent Phase II study of itepekimab, with a similar MoA to tozorakimab, which resulted in a marked reduction of COPD exacerbations as well as a significant improvement of FEV1 in former smokers (Rabe et al 2021).
trans-unit.target: The study population for the predecessor studies were identified based on tozorakimab mechanism of action, unmet need in patients experiencing COPD symptom burden and at risk for exacerbations, and outcomes of recent Phase II study of itepekimab, with a similar MoA to tozorakimab, which resulted in a marked reduction of COPD exacerbations as well as a significant improvement of FEV1 in former smokers (Rabe et al 2021).

trans-unit.source: Interleukin-33 has been implicated in the pathogenesis of goblet cell hypertrophy and mucous hypersecretion (Allinne et al 2019, Schmitz et al 2005). A large proportion of patients with high levels of mucins and mucous plugging have a higher cough and sputum burden (Okajima et al 2020, Radicioni et al 2021) and risk of exacerbations (Kim et al 2015, Kim et al 2019, Lahousse et al 2017, Stott-Miller et al 2020). For that reason, a population of patients with symptomatic COPD and at risk for exacerbations, with a CAT score of 10 or more, with a score 2 or more from sputum and phlegm, has been selected for the predecessor studies.
trans-unit.target: Interleukin-33 has been implicated in the pathogenesis of goblet cell hypertrophy and mucous hypersecretion (Allinne et al 2019, Schmitz et al 2005). A large proportion of patients with high levels of mucins and mucous plugging have a higher cough and sputum burden (Okajima et al 2020, Radicioni et al 2021) and risk of exacerbations (Kim et al 2015, Kim et al 2019, Lahousse et al 2017, Stott-Miller et al 2020). For that reason, a population of patients with symptomatic COPD and at risk for exacerbations, with a CAT score of 10 or more, with a score 2 or more from sputum and phlegm, has been selected for the predecessor studies.

trans-unit.source: The rationale for the selection of participants for PROSPERO is that they have completed the predecessor study treatment period and not been prematurely discontinued from IP.
trans-unit.target: The rationale for the selection of participants for PROSPERO is that they have completed the predecessor study treatment period and not been prematurely discontinued from IP.

trans-unit.source: Rationale for Selection of Primary Endpoint
trans-unit.target: Rationale for Selection of Primary Endpoint

trans-unit.source: Severe exacerbations of COPD worsen the course of COPD disease and involve a rapid decline in health status, disease progression and high mortality, with each severe COPD exacerbation increasing the risk of further exacerbations (Wedzicha et al 2013),
trans-unit.target: Severe exacerbations of COPD worsen the course of COPD disease and involve a rapid decline in health status, disease progression and high mortality, with each severe COPD exacerbation increasing the risk of further exacerbations (Wedzicha et al 2013),

trans-unit.source: re-hospitalisation (Myers et al 2020) and death (Suissa et al 2012).
trans-unit.target: re-hospitalisation (Myers et al 2020) and death (Suissa et al 2012).

trans-unit.source: Annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations is a well-accepted and clinically meaningful endpoint to evaluate whether tozorakimab plus SoC versus placebo plus SoC can reduce the risk of severe COPD exacerbations.
trans-unit.target: Annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations is a well-accepted and clinically meaningful endpoint to evaluate whether tozorakimab plus SoC versus placebo plus SoC can reduce the risk of severe COPD exacerbations.

trans-unit.source: The comparator in this study is placebo. Both tozorakimab and placebo will be administered on top of optimised SoC maintenance inhaled therapy; therefore, the treatment of the placebo group will correspond to current SoC for patients with COPD. The use of placebo will allow for a double-blinded treatment period and to provide a control arm, so as to enable a continued comparison of long-term efficacy, in particular the comparison of severe exacerbations rates, and to act as a comparator to contextualise safety observations.
trans-unit.target: The comparator in this study is placebo. Both tozorakimab and placebo will be administered on top of optimised SoC maintenance inhaled therapy; therefore, the treatment of the placebo group will correspond to current SoC for patients with COPD. The use of placebo will allow for a double-blinded treatment period and to provide a control arm, so as to enable a continued comparison of long-term efficacy, in particular the comparison of severe exacerbations rates, and to act as a comparator to contextualise safety observations.

trans-unit.source: Placebo was used as the comparator during the OBERON/TITANIA predecessor studies and will continue as the comparator in this LTE study. Fifty percent of the participants in the predecessor placebo arm will be assigned to active treatment and 50% of the participants in the predecessor placebo arm will continue to receive placebo. The re-randomisation of PROSPERO from the predecessor studies will allow more participants to receive the potentially effective experimental treatment rather than extended SoC, while retaining a comparator arm.
trans-unit.target: Placebo was used as the comparator during the OBERON/TITANIA predecessor studies and will continue as the comparator in this LTE study. Fifty percent of the participants in the predecessor placebo arm will be assigned to active treatment and 50% of the participants in the predecessor placebo arm will continue to receive placebo. The re-randomisation of PROSPERO from the predecessor studies will allow more participants to receive the potentially effective experimental treatment rather than extended SoC, while retaining a comparator arm.

trans-unit.source: Rationale for Selection of Study Duration
trans-unit.target: Rationale for Selection of Study Duration

trans-unit.source: The treatment period was selected to maximise the number of events of severe COPD exacerbation by the time of unblinding of the predecessor studies and to give a predictable duration to participants given the uncertainty in recruitment time of the predecessor studies. The primary reporting of the study may take place once both predecessor studies have been unblinded and reported without waiting for all participants to complete treatment in this study to enable the timely completion of regulatory submission.
trans-unit.target: The treatment period was selected to maximise the number of events of severe COPD exacerbation by the time of unblinding of the predecessor studies and to give a predictable duration to participants given the uncertainty in recruitment time of the predecessor studies. The primary reporting of the study may take place once both predecessor studies have been unblinded and reported without waiting for all participants to complete treatment in this study to enable the timely completion of regulatory submission.

trans-unit.source: Patient feedback on the study design of the predecessor studies was obtained from a panel of 5 patients with COPD and 1 caregiver of a patient with COPD within the Sponsor Patient Partnership Program, an internal working group that enables patient feedback on clinical trial study designs. During the discussion, patients shared which aspects of the proposed study they found appealing or concerning and provided their feedback on visit frequency, study procedures, and use of digital solutions. Patient feedback received during design of predecessor studies had a significant influence on the design of the PROSPERO study. Site visit frequency and number of study assessments were reduced. In addition, APFS was introduced to the PROSPERO study to allow for optional at home administration, further reducing the number of clinic visits and burden for the patient.
trans-unit.target: Patient feedback on the study design of the predecessor studies was obtained from a panel of 5 patients with COPD and 1 caregiver of a patient with COPD within the Sponsor Patient Partnership Program, an internal working group that enables patient feedback on clinical trial study designs. During the discussion, patients shared which aspects of the proposed study they found appealing or concerning and provided their feedback on visit frequency, study procedures, and use of digital solutions. Patient feedback received during design of predecessor studies had a significant influence on the design of the PROSPERO study. Site visit frequency and number of study assessments were reduced. In addition, APFS was introduced to the PROSPERO study to allow for optional at home administration, further reducing the number of clinic visits and burden for the patient.

trans-unit.source: The present study is a LTE study that will enable participants to continue on their IP regimen or be re-randomised from predecessor studies OBERON or TITANIA. The 2 dose regimens of SC tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W and 300 mg Q8W were initially proposed in the predecessor studies. These dose regimens were selected based on an integrative PK/PD model created with the systemic target engagement information from a Phase I study (D9180C00001) that evaluated single doses of tozorakimab up to 300 mg SC or IV in healthy adult participants with a history of mild atopy and multiple doses up to 300 mg Q2W in patients with mild COPD. This PK/PD model was used to predict IL33 suppression in lung at trough assumed similar systemic exposure in patients and healthy subjects. However, emerging Phase II data in moderate to severe asthma patients (Study D9181C0001) and moderate to severe atopic dermatitis (Study D9182C0001) has shown 30% to 35% lower trough concentrations in
trans-unit.target: The present study is a LTE study that will enable participants to continue on their IP regimen or be re-randomised from predecessor studies OBERON or TITANIA. The 2 dose regimens of SC tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W and 300 mg Q8W were initially proposed in the predecessor studies. These dose regimens were selected based on an integrative PK/PD model created with the systemic target engagement information from a Phase I study (D9180C00001) that evaluated single doses of tozorakimab up to 300 mg SC or IV in healthy adult participants with a history of mild atopy and multiple doses up to 300 mg Q2W in patients with mild COPD. This PK/PD model was used to predict IL33 suppression in lung at trough assumed similar systemic exposure in patients and healthy subjects. However, emerging Phase II data in moderate to severe asthma patients (Study D9181C0001) and moderate to severe atopic dermatitis (Study D9182C0001) has shown 30% to 35% lower trough concentrations in

trans-unit.source: patients compared with healthy volunteers in Phase I (D9180C00001). The reduction in PK concentrations predicts a slightly below 80% IL33 lung inhibition for 300 mg Q8W but remains above 90% for tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W. For this reason, the sample size of the tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm has been reduced in the predecessor studies, and as of the protocol amendment (Version 2.0) participants receiving 300 mg Q8W regimen in the predecessor studies will be re-allocated to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W in PROSPERO study.
trans-unit.target: patients compared with healthy volunteers in Phase I (D9180C00001). The reduction in PK concentrations predicts a slightly below 80% IL33 lung inhibition for 300 mg Q8W but remains above 90% for tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W. For this reason, the sample size of the tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm has been reduced in the predecessor studies, and as of the protocol amendment (Version 2.0) participants receiving 300 mg Q8W regimen in the predecessor studies will be re-allocated to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W in PROSPERO study.

trans-unit.source: For the purpose of Clinical Trial Transparency the definition of the end of the study differs under FDA and EU regulatory requirements:
trans-unit.target: For the purpose of Clinical Trial Transparency the definition of the end of the study differs under FDA and EU regulatory requirements:

trans-unit.source: EU requirements define study completion as the last visit of the last subject for any protocol-related activity.
trans-unit.target: EU requirements define study completion as the last visit of the last subject for any protocol-related activity.

trans-unit.source: Primary Completion Date – the date that the final participant is examined or receives an intervention for the purposes of final collection of data for the primary outcome measure, whether the clinical study concluded according to the pre-specified protocol or was terminated. In the case of clinical studies with more than one primary outcome measure with different completion dates, this term refers to the date on which data collection is completed for all of the primary outcomes.
trans-unit.target: Primary Completion Date – the date that the final participant is examined or receives an intervention for the purposes of final collection of data for the primary outcome measure, whether the clinical study concluded according to the pre-specified protocol or was terminated. In the case of clinical studies with more than one primary outcome measure with different completion dates, this term refers to the date on which data collection is completed for all of the primary outcomes.

trans-unit.source: Study Completion Date – the date the final participant is examined or receives an intervention for purposes of final collection of data for the primary and secondary outcome measures and AEs (eg, last participant’s last visit), whether the clinical study concludes according to the pre-specified protocol or is terminated.
trans-unit.target: Study Completion Date – the date the final participant is examined or receives an intervention for purposes of final collection of data for the primary and secondary outcome measures and AEs (eg, last participant’s last visit), whether the clinical study concludes according to the pre-specified protocol or is terminated.

trans-unit.source: An individual participant will be considered to have completed the study if the participant was followed through their last protocol-specified visit/assessment regardless of the number of doses of IP that was received.
trans-unit.target: An individual participant will be considered to have completed the study if the participant was followed through their last protocol-specified visit/assessment regardless of the number of doses of IP that was received.

trans-unit.source: Participants who prematurely discontinue IP should complete procedures described for the E/D visit and further phone follow-up of severe exacerbation and death information, if applicable (refer to Section 7.1.1 for details).
trans-unit.target: Participants who prematurely discontinue IP should complete procedures described for the E/D visit and further phone follow-up of severe exacerbation and death information, if applicable (refer to Section 7.1.1 for details).

trans-unit.source: Participants will not be considered to have completed the study if consent was withdrawn or the participant was lost to follow-up (see Section 7.2 and Section 7.3, respectively).
trans-unit.target: Participants will not be considered to have completed the study if consent was withdrawn or the participant was lost to follow-up (see Section 7.2 and Section 7.3, respectively).

trans-unit.source: The primary reporting of the study may take place once both predecessor studies have been unblinded and reported without waiting for all participants to complete treatment in this study. To ensure consistency with reporting PROSPERO and its predecessor studies, some participants may not complete the full 28-week treatment period. As a result, some participants may conclude the study prior to finishing the full treatment duration. Participants
trans-unit.target: The primary reporting of the study may take place once both predecessor studies have been unblinded and reported without waiting for all participants to complete treatment in this study. To ensure consistency with reporting PROSPERO and its predecessor studies, some participants may not complete the full 28-week treatment period. As a result, some participants may conclude the study prior to finishing the full treatment duration. Participants

trans-unit.source: will be made aware of this during the consent process. The participants who do not complete the full treatment period will be asked to attend the E/D visit, scheduled 12 weeks after their final IP dose, to complete their participation.
trans-unit.target: will be made aware of this during the consent process. The participants who do not complete the full treatment period will be asked to attend the E/D visit, scheduled 12 weeks after their final IP dose, to complete their participation.

trans-unit.source: The number of severe exacerbations observed in the study will be monitored to estimate statistical power before the decision to unblind is confirmed. The study may also be unblinded to report early at the interim analysis due to overwhelming efficacy. In either scenario, the study team in direct contact with sites or with influence over management of participants or recording of endpoints in PROSPERO will remain blinded and be separate from the team involved in reporting of the predecessor studies. Details will be included in the Study Integrity Plan.
trans-unit.target: The number of severe exacerbations observed in the study will be monitored to estimate statistical power before the decision to unblind is confirmed. The study may also be unblinded to report early at the interim analysis due to overwhelming efficacy. In either scenario, the study team in direct contact with sites or with influence over management of participants or recording of endpoints in PROSPERO will remain blinded and be separate from the team involved in reporting of the predecessor studies. Details will be included in the Study Integrity Plan.

trans-unit.source: Discontinuation of specific sites or of the study as a whole are handled in accordance with Appendix A.
trans-unit.target: Discontinuation of specific sites or of the study as a whole are handled in accordance with Appendix A.

trans-unit.source: An external IDMC will be set up to provide systematic and unbiased assessment of safety for the predecessor studies and PROSPERO. The IDMC will oversee the safety of the study participants and will make appropriate recommendations regarding further study conduct based on the available data. The IDMC may recommend modification or termination of the study based on reviewed data, including regular unblinded safety review and at a planned interim analysis where decision rules relating to early reporting of the study for efficacy will be considered. The details of IDMC process and decision criteria will be detailed in the IDMC charter. If predefined criteria for stopping the predecessor studies for futility is met, and the predecessor studies are stopped for futility, this study will also be stopped.
trans-unit.target: An external IDMC will be set up to provide systematic and unbiased assessment of safety for the predecessor studies and PROSPERO. The IDMC will oversee the safety of the study participants and will make appropriate recommendations regarding further study conduct based on the available data. The IDMC may recommend modification or termination of the study based on reviewed data, including regular unblinded safety review and at a planned interim analysis where decision rules relating to early reporting of the study for efficacy will be considered. The details of IDMC process and decision criteria will be detailed in the IDMC charter. If predefined criteria for stopping the predecessor studies for futility is met, and the predecessor studies are stopped for futility, this study will also be stopped.

trans-unit.source: If the Sponsor determines that temporary suspension or termination of the study is required, the Sponsor will discuss the reasons for taking such action with all participating Investigators (or heads of the medical institutions, where applicable). When feasible, the Sponsor will provide advance notice to all participating Investigators (or heads of the medical institutions, where applicable) of the impending action.
trans-unit.target: If the Sponsor determines that temporary suspension or termination of the study is required, the Sponsor will discuss the reasons for taking such action with all participating Investigators (or heads of the medical institutions, where applicable). When feasible, the Sponsor will provide advance notice to all participating Investigators (or heads of the medical institutions, where applicable) of the impending action.

trans-unit.source: If the study is suspended or terminated for safety reasons, the Sponsor will promptly inform all Investigators, heads of the medical institutions (where applicable), and/or institutions conducting the study. The Sponsor will also promptly inform the relevant regulatory authorities of the suspension/termination along with the reasons for such action. Where required by applicable regulations, the Investigator or head of the medical institution must inform the IRB/IEC promptly and provide the reason(s) for the suspension/termination. If the study is suspended for safety reasons and it is deemed appropriate by the Sponsor to resume the study, approval from the relevant regulatory authorities (and IRBs/IECs when applicable) will be obtained prior to resuming the study. The Investigator shall also promptly inform the participant of the study suspension/termination and should assure appropriate participant therapy and/or follow-up.
trans-unit.target: If the study is suspended or terminated for safety reasons, the Sponsor will promptly inform all Investigators, heads of the medical institutions (where applicable), and/or institutions conducting the study. The Sponsor will also promptly inform the relevant regulatory authorities of the suspension/termination along with the reasons for such action. Where required by applicable regulations, the Investigator or head of the medical institution must inform the IRB/IEC promptly and provide the reason(s) for the suspension/termination. If the study is suspended for safety reasons and it is deemed appropriate by the Sponsor to resume the study, approval from the relevant regulatory authorities (and IRBs/IECs when applicable) will be obtained prior to resuming the study. The Investigator shall also promptly inform the participant of the study suspension/termination and should assure appropriate participant therapy and/or follow-up.

trans-unit.source: Prospective approval of protocol deviations to recruitment and enrolment criteria, also known as protocol waivers or exemptions, is not permitted.
trans-unit.target: Prospective approval of protocol deviations to recruitment and enrolment criteria, also known as protocol waivers or exemptions, is not permitted.

trans-unit.source: Participants are eligible to be included in the study only if all of the following criteria apply:
trans-unit.target: Participants are eligible to be included in the study only if all of the following criteria apply:

trans-unit.source: Participants who have completed the treatment period and have not been prematurely discontinued from IP in the predecessor studies.
trans-unit.target: Participants who have completed the treatment period and have not been prematurely discontinued from IP in the predecessor studies.

trans-unit.source: Participants who received their last dose of IP in the predecessor studies within the previous 12 weeks and were not withdrawn from the predecessor study.
trans-unit.target: Participants who received their last dose of IP in the predecessor studies within the previous 12 weeks and were not withdrawn from the predecessor study.

trans-unit.source: FOCBP must have a negative urine pregnancy test at Visit 1.
trans-unit.target: FOCBP must have a negative urine pregnancy test at Visit 1.

trans-unit.source: For a definition of FOCBP, refer to Appendix E.
trans-unit.target: For a definition of FOCBP, refer to Appendix E.

trans-unit.source: Participants who are willing to continue using contraceptive methods as agreed to for the predecessor OBERON or TITANIA studies. See Table E1 in Appendix E for acceptable methods of contraception.
trans-unit.target: Participants who are willing to continue using contraceptive methods as agreed to for the predecessor OBERON or TITANIA studies. See Table E1 in Appendix E for acceptable methods of contraception.

trans-unit.source: Contraceptive use by men or women should be consistent with local regulations regarding the methods of contraception for those participating in clinical studies.
trans-unit.target: Contraceptive use by men or women should be consistent with local regulations regarding the methods of contraception for those participating in clinical studies.

trans-unit.source: Non-sterile male participants who are sexually active with a FOCPB partner must agree to use a male condom while engaging in sexual activity from enrolment throughout the study duration and until 14 weeks after last dose of IP. For a definition of non-sterile male, refer to Appendix E. In countries where spermicide is available, it is strongly recommended. It is also strongly recommended for the female partner of a male participant to use a highly effective method of contraception throughout this period.
trans-unit.target: Non-sterile male participants who are sexually active with a FOCPB partner must agree to use a male condom while engaging in sexual activity from enrolment throughout the study duration and until 14 weeks after last dose of IP. For a definition of non-sterile male, refer to Appendix E. In countries where spermicide is available, it is strongly recommended. It is also strongly recommended for the female partner of a male participant to use a highly effective method of contraception throughout this period.

trans-unit.source: Non-sterilised male patients should also refrain from biologically fathering a child or donating sperm during the same period.
trans-unit.target: Non-sterilised male patients should also refrain from biologically fathering a child or donating sperm during the same period.

trans-unit.source: FOCBP who are sexually active with a non-sterilised male partner must agree to use one highly effective method of birth control, as defined below, from enrolment throughout the study and until at least 14 weeks after last dose of IP. Cessation of contraception after this point should be discussed with a responsible physician.
trans-unit.target: FOCBP who are sexually active with a non-sterilised male partner must agree to use one highly effective method of birth control, as defined below, from enrolment throughout the study and until at least 14 weeks after last dose of IP. Cessation of contraception after this point should be discussed with a responsible physician.

trans-unit.source: Periodic abstinence (calendar, symptothermal, post-ovulation methods), withdrawal (coitus interruptus), spermicides only, and lactational amenorrhoea method are not
trans-unit.target: Periodic abstinence (calendar, symptothermal, post-ovulation methods), withdrawal (coitus interruptus), spermicides only, and lactational amenorrhoea method are not

trans-unit.source: acceptable methods of contraception. Female condom and male condom should not be used together.
trans-unit.target: acceptable methods of contraception. Female condom and male condom should not be used together.

trans-unit.source: A highly effective method of contraception is defined as one that can achieve a failure rate of less than 1% per year when used consistently and correctly. Highly effective birth control methods can be found in Appendix E. It is highly recommended for the male partner of a FOCBP participant to use a male condom while engaging in sexual activity throughout this period.
trans-unit.target: A highly effective method of contraception is defined as one that can achieve a failure rate of less than 1% per year when used consistently and correctly. Highly effective birth control methods can be found in Appendix E. It is highly recommended for the male partner of a FOCBP participant to use a male condom while engaging in sexual activity throughout this period.

trans-unit.source: Note that there are no contraception requirements for female participants who are not of childbearing potential. However, all female participants should refrain from egg cell donation and breastfeeding throughout the study.
trans-unit.target: Note that there are no contraception requirements for female participants who are not of childbearing potential. However, all female participants should refrain from egg cell donation and breastfeeding throughout the study.

trans-unit.source: Capable of giving signed informed consent as described in Appendix A, which includes compliance with the requirements and restrictions listed in the ICF and in the protocol.
trans-unit.target: Capable of giving signed informed consent as described in Appendix A, which includes compliance with the requirements and restrictions listed in the ICF and in the protocol.

trans-unit.source: Participants are excluded from the study if any of the following criteria apply.
trans-unit.target: Participants are excluded from the study if any of the following criteria apply.

trans-unit.source: Any clinically significant disorder or abnormal findings (clinical, laboratory, instrumental, etc) or major physical and/or cognitive impairment, which, in the opinion of the Investigator, may put the participant at risk because of his/her participation in the study or impact the interpretation of the study results, or otherwise makes the participation of the participant inappropriate.
trans-unit.target: Any clinically significant disorder or abnormal findings (clinical, laboratory, instrumental, etc) or major physical and/or cognitive impairment, which, in the opinion of the Investigator, may put the participant at risk because of his/her participation in the study or impact the interpretation of the study results, or otherwise makes the participation of the participant inappropriate.

trans-unit.source: Participant meeting criteria for IP discontinuation (refer to Section 7.1.1) as judged by the Investigator or the Sponsor.
trans-unit.target: Participant meeting criteria for IP discontinuation (refer to Section 7.1.1) as judged by the Investigator or the Sponsor.

trans-unit.source: Concurrent enrolment in other interventional clinical study or treatment with another IP, with the exception of the OBERON and TITANIA predecessor studies.
trans-unit.target: Concurrent enrolment in other interventional clinical study or treatment with another IP, with the exception of the OBERON and TITANIA predecessor studies.

trans-unit.source: Severe allergic reaction to any monoclonal and polyclonal antibody.
trans-unit.target: Severe allergic reaction to any monoclonal and polyclonal antibody.

trans-unit.source: Allergy or reaction to any component of the IP formulation.
trans-unit.target: Allergy or reaction to any component of the IP formulation.

trans-unit.source: Chronic use (or expected need for chronic use during the study) of immunosuppressive medications (including, but not limited to, systemic corticosteroids), marketed or investigational biologic, or another prohibited medication that, in the opinion of the
trans-unit.target: Chronic use (or expected need for chronic use during the study) of immunosuppressive medications (including, but not limited to, systemic corticosteroids), marketed or investigational biologic, or another prohibited medication that, in the opinion of the

trans-unit.source: Investigator or the Sponsor, may put the participant's safety at risk and/or impact the interpretation of the study results.
trans-unit.target: Investigator or the Sponsor, may put the participant's safety at risk and/or impact the interpretation of the study results.

trans-unit.source: Involvement in the planning and/or conduct of the study (applies to both staff employed by the Sponsor and/or staff at the study site).
trans-unit.target: Involvement in the planning and/or conduct of the study (applies to both staff employed by the Sponsor and/or staff at the study site).

trans-unit.source: Participants who are not able to comply with the study requirements, procedures, and restrictions, as judged by the Investigator or the Sponsor.
trans-unit.target: Participants who are not able to comply with the study requirements, procedures, and restrictions, as judged by the Investigator or the Sponsor.

trans-unit.source: Male participants who are sexually active with a FOCBP and participants that are FOCBP must use highly effective contraceptive methods from enrolment throughout the study and until 14 weeks after last dose of IP, as stated in inclusion criterion 4 (Section 5.1). See Table E1 in Appendix E for acceptable methods of contraception.
trans-unit.target: Male participants who are sexually active with a FOCBP and participants that are FOCBP must use highly effective contraceptive methods from enrolment throughout the study and until 14 weeks after last dose of IP, as stated in inclusion criterion 4 (Section 5.1). See Table E1 in Appendix E for acceptable methods of contraception.

trans-unit.source: There are no contraception requirements for female participants who are not of childbearing potential. However, all female participants should refrain from breastfeeding throughout the study.
trans-unit.target: There are no contraception requirements for female participants who are not of childbearing potential. However, all female participants should refrain from breastfeeding throughout the study.

trans-unit.source: Participants must abstain from donating blood and plasma from the time of informed consent, and for 14 weeks (5.5 half-lives) after last dose of IP.
trans-unit.target: Participants must abstain from donating blood and plasma from the time of informed consent, and for 14 weeks (5.5 half-lives) after last dose of IP.

trans-unit.source: Participants should avoid eating a large meal for at least 2 hours prior to all scheduled lung function assessments at the site.
trans-unit.target: Participants should avoid eating a large meal for at least 2 hours prior to all scheduled lung function assessments at the site.

trans-unit.source: Participants enrolled in the “former smokers” cohort should refrain from smoking throughout the entire duration of the study.
trans-unit.target: Participants enrolled in the “former smokers” cohort should refrain from smoking throughout the entire duration of the study.

trans-unit.source: Participants who are current smokers (currently smoking tobacco) will be allowed to stop smoking during the study if desired.
trans-unit.target: Participants who are current smokers (currently smoking tobacco) will be allowed to stop smoking during the study if desired.

trans-unit.source: Smoking and nicotine use status changes during the course of the study will be captured on the eCRF.
trans-unit.target: Smoking and nicotine use status changes during the course of the study will be captured on the eCRF.

trans-unit.source: Use of e-cigarettes, or vaping of any products (eg, nicotine, THC) is discouraged during the study. Use of heated tobacco products (eg, IQOS) is discouraged in the former smoker cohort.
trans-unit.target: Use of e-cigarettes, or vaping of any products (eg, nicotine, THC) is discouraged during the study. Use of heated tobacco products (eg, IQOS) is discouraged in the former smoker cohort.

trans-unit.source: Participants should avoid smoking for at least one hour prior to all scheduled lung function assessments and blood collection at the site.
trans-unit.target: Participants should avoid smoking for at least one hour prior to all scheduled lung function assessments and blood collection at the site.

trans-unit.source: Participants should avoid engaging in strenuous exertion for at least 30 minutes prior to all scheduled lung function assessments and blood collection at the site.
trans-unit.target: Participants should avoid engaging in strenuous exertion for at least 30 minutes prior to all scheduled lung function assessments and blood collection at the site.

trans-unit.source: Enrolment failures are defined as participants who consent to participate in the clinical study but are not subsequently randomised. A minimal set of enrolment failure information is required to ensure transparent reporting of enrolment failure participants to meet the CONSORT publishing requirements and to respond to queries from regulatory authorities.
trans-unit.target: Enrolment failures are defined as participants who consent to participate in the clinical study but are not subsequently randomised. A minimal set of enrolment failure information is required to ensure transparent reporting of enrolment failure participants to meet the CONSORT publishing requirements and to respond to queries from regulatory authorities.

trans-unit.source: Minimal information includes demography, enrolment failure details, eligibility criteria, and any SAE. These participants should have the reason for enrolment failure recorded in the eCRF.
trans-unit.target: Minimal information includes demography, enrolment failure details, eligibility criteria, and any SAE. These participants should have the reason for enrolment failure recorded in the eCRF.

trans-unit.source: All participants that are enrolment failures must continue with the follow-up period requirements as described in the protocol for the predecessor study.
trans-unit.target: All participants that are enrolment failures must continue with the follow-up period requirements as described in the protocol for the predecessor study.

trans-unit.source: Study intervention is defined as any investigational intervention(s), marketed product(s), placebo, intended to be administered, or medical device(s) utilised by a study participant according to the CSP. Study intervention in this CSP includes tozorakimab and placebo and will be referred to in the CSP as IP.
trans-unit.target: Study intervention is defined as any investigational intervention(s), marketed product(s), placebo, intended to be administered, or medical device(s) utilised by a study participant according to the CSP. Study intervention in this CSP includes tozorakimab and placebo and will be referred to in the CSP as IP.

trans-unit.source: Tozorakimab and placebo will be supplied as solutions for injection in an APFS. Tozorakimab will be supplied as a 300 mg APFS containing 150 mg/mL tozorakimab, 20 mM
trans-unit.target: Tozorakimab and placebo will be supplied as solutions for injection in an APFS. Tozorakimab will be supplied as a 300 mg APFS containing 150 mg/mL tozorakimab, 20 mM

trans-unit.source: L-histidine/L-histidine hydrochloride, 220 mM L-arginine hydrochloride, and 0.03% w/v polysorbate 80 with a pH of 5.5. Placebo will be supplied as an APFS containing 20 mM L-histidine/L-histidine hydrochloride, 220 mM L-arginine hydrochloride, and 0.03% w/v polysorbate 80 with a pH of 5.5. The label-claim volume for tozorakimab and placebo is 2 mL.
trans-unit.target: L-histidine/L-histidine hydrochloride, 220 mM L-arginine hydrochloride, and 0.03% w/v polysorbate 80 with a pH of 5.5. Placebo will be supplied as an APFS containing 20 mM L-histidine/L-histidine hydrochloride, 220 mM L-arginine hydrochloride, and 0.03% w/v polysorbate 80 with a pH of 5.5. The label-claim volume for tozorakimab and placebo is 2 mL.

trans-unit.source: 300 mg Q8W (with alternating matching placebo between active tozorakimab administrations)
trans-unit.target: 300 mg Q8W (with alternating matching placebo between active tozorakimab administrations)

trans-unit.source: Study intervention in APFS with 2 mL label claim volume will be provided in kits. Each APFS and kit will be labelled and labels will be prepared in accordance with GMP and local regulatory requirements.
trans-unit.target: Study intervention in APFS with 2 mL label claim volume will be provided in kits. Each APFS and kit will be labelled and labels will be prepared in accordance with GMP and local regulatory requirements.

trans-unit.source: The kits will include a unique number that is printed on all labels within the kit (ie, the outer carton label and the label of the APFS within the carton).
trans-unit.target: The kits will include a unique number that is printed on all labels within the kit (ie, the outer carton label and the label of the APFS within the carton).

trans-unit.source: Study intervention in APFS with 2 mL label claim volume will be provided in kits. Each APFS and kit will be labelled and labels will be prepared in accordance with GMP and local regulatory requirements.
trans-unit.target: Study intervention in APFS with 2 mL label claim volume will be provided in kits. Each APFS and kit will be labelled and labels will be prepared in accordance with GMP and local regulatory requirements.

trans-unit.source: The kits will include a unique number that is printed on all labels within the kit (ie, the outer carton label and the label of the APFS within the carton).
trans-unit.target: The kits will include a unique number that is printed on all labels within the kit (ie, the outer carton label and the label of the APFS within the carton).

trans-unit.source: Study intervention in APFS with 2 mL label claim volume will be provided in kits. Each APFS and kit will be labelled and labels will be prepared in accordance with GMP and local regulatory requirements.

trans-unit.source: The kits will include a unique number that is printed on all labels within the kit (ie, the outer carton label and the label of the APFS within the carton).
