trans-unit.source: Note: The treatment period will last for 28 weeks or 52 weeks, respectively (see Section 4.1.2 for details). APFS, accessorised pre-filled syringe; GMP, good manufacturing practice; IMP, investigational medicinal product; NIMP, non-investigational medical product; Q4W, every 4 weeks; Q8W, every 8 weeks;
trans-unit.target: Note: The treatment period will last for 28 weeks or 52 weeks, respectively (see Section 4.1.2 for details). APFS, accessorised pre-filled syringe; GMP, good manufacturing practice; IMP, investigational medicinal product; NIMP, non-investigational medical product; Q4W, every 4 weeks; Q8W, every 8 weeks;

trans-unit.source: Every effort should be taken to keep IP administration within the visit window.
trans-unit.target: Every effort should be taken to keep IP administration within the visit window.

trans-unit.source: If a participant presents with a condition that contraindicates dosing, IP dose should be withheld and administered as soon as possible when the contraindicating condition resolves.
trans-unit.target: If a participant presents with a condition that contraindicates dosing, IP dose should be withheld and administered as soon as possible when the contraindicating condition resolves.

trans-unit.source: The IP should not be administered, and the dosing is to be rescheduled in the presence of any AE, laboratory abnormality, or another diagnostic finding that, in the opinion of the Investigator or the Sponsor, contraindicates dosing or could result in complications.
trans-unit.target: The IP should not be administered, and the dosing is to be rescheduled in the presence of any AE, laboratory abnormality, or another diagnostic finding that, in the opinion of the Investigator or the Sponsor, contraindicates dosing or could result in complications.

trans-unit.source: It is recommended that the Sponsor study physician/delegate is contacted with any questions.
trans-unit.target: It is recommended that the Sponsor study physician/delegate is contacted with any questions.

trans-unit.source: If IP cannot be administered at a scheduled treatment visit (eg, due to conditions listed above), it can be postponed as necessary and administered as soon as possible, with the exception of Visit 1, in which the first dosing in PROSPERO should take place. When IP dosing needs to be postponed, it is recommended that all scheduled treatment visit procedures (except for IP administration) are still performed within the visit window.
trans-unit.target: If IP cannot be administered at a scheduled treatment visit (eg, due to conditions listed above), it can be postponed as necessary and administered as soon as possible, with the exception of Visit 1, in which the first dosing in PROSPERO should take place. When IP dosing needs to be postponed, it is recommended that all scheduled treatment visit procedures (except for IP administration) are still performed within the visit window.

trans-unit.source: Rescheduled IP dose can be then administered at an unscheduled visit. Physical exam, vital signs assessment, AE assessment, changes in concomitant medication, and pregnancy test (if applicable) are the minimum procedures at the time of dose administration visit; it may also include remaining visit procedures (not performed at the scheduled visit) and additional assessments as deemed necessary by the Investigator.
trans-unit.target: Rescheduled IP dose can be then administered at an unscheduled visit. Physical exam, vital signs assessment, AE assessment, changes in concomitant medication, and pregnancy test (if applicable) are the minimum procedures at the time of dose administration visit; it may also include remaining visit procedures (not performed at the scheduled visit) and additional assessments as deemed necessary by the Investigator.

trans-unit.source: If the entire scheduled visit cannot be conducted within the window (eg, the participant is unable to attend the study site), then all visit procedures are rescheduled along with the IP dose.
trans-unit.target: If the entire scheduled visit cannot be conducted within the window (eg, the participant is unable to attend the study site), then all visit procedures are rescheduled along with the IP dose.

trans-unit.source: If a dose is significantly delayed it is recommended to keep at least 2 weeks interval before the next dose. If a postponed dose overlaps with the next treatment visit window, the postponed dose will be skipped, and the next dose of IP given at the regularly scheduled visit. The visit schedule will be always calculated from Visit 1 of this study.
trans-unit.target: If a dose is significantly delayed it is recommended to keep at least 2 weeks interval before the next dose. If a postponed dose overlaps with the next treatment visit window, the postponed dose will be skipped, and the next dose of IP given at the regularly scheduled visit. The visit schedule will be always calculated from Visit 1 of this study.

trans-unit.source: If the participant misses 2 consecutive or non-consecutive doses of IP, it is strongly recommended that the site discuss with the Sponsor study physician to review the participant’s adherence to treatment and measures to ensure compliance. If the participant misses 3 or more consecutive doses of IP (>12 weeks elapses since the last dose of IP), the site must discuss with the study physician whether continuation or discontinuation of IP is in their best interests.
trans-unit.target: If the participant misses 2 consecutive or non-consecutive doses of IP, it is strongly recommended that the site discuss with the Sponsor study physician to review the participant’s adherence to treatment and measures to ensure compliance. If the participant misses 3 or more consecutive doses of IP (>12 weeks elapses since the last dose of IP), the site must discuss with the study physician whether continuation or discontinuation of IP is in their best interests.

trans-unit.source: Discontinuation procedures are described in Section 7.1. All participants, whether they remain on IP or not, will be encouraged to stay in the study and be followed until the study completion (Section 7.1.1).
trans-unit.target: Discontinuation procedures are described in Section 7.1. All participants, whether they remain on IP or not, will be encouraged to stay in the study and be followed until the study completion (Section 7.1.1).

trans-unit.source: Preparation, Handling, Storage, and Accountability of Interventions
trans-unit.target: Preparation, Handling, Storage, and Accountability of Interventions

trans-unit.source: The Investigator or designee must confirm appropriate temperature conditions have been maintained during transit for all IP received and any discrepancies are reported and resolved before use of the IP.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator or designee must confirm appropriate temperature conditions have been maintained during transit for all IP received and any discrepancies are reported and resolved before use of the IP.

trans-unit.source: Only participants enrolled in the study may receive IP and only authorised staff (with the exception of self-administration described in Section 6.2.2.3 and circumstances described in Appendix H) may dispense or administer IP. All IP must be stored in a secure, environmentally controlled, and monitored (manual or automated) area in accordance with the labelled storage conditions with access limited to the Investigator and authorised site staff.
trans-unit.target: Only participants enrolled in the study may receive IP and only authorised staff (with the exception of self-administration described in Section 6.2.2.3 and circumstances described in Appendix H) may dispense or administer IP. All IP must be stored in a secure, environmentally controlled, and monitored (manual or automated) area in accordance with the labelled storage conditions with access limited to the Investigator and authorised site staff.

trans-unit.source: For at-home self-administration, the participant will be responsible for refrigerating the product at home. The temperature is not monitored during storage by the participant; standard refrigeration is sufficient to maintain appropriate temperatures while the IP is in the participant’s possession. The participant will be responsible for returning used APFS devices to the study centre.
trans-unit.target: For at-home self-administration, the participant will be responsible for refrigerating the product at home. The temperature is not monitored during storage by the participant; standard refrigeration is sufficient to maintain appropriate temperatures while the IP is in the participant’s possession. The participant will be responsible for returning used APFS devices to the study centre.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator, institution, or the head of the medical institution (where applicable) is responsible for IP accountability, reconciliation, and record maintenance (ie, receipt, reconciliation, and final disposition records).
trans-unit.target: The Investigator, institution, or the head of the medical institution (where applicable) is responsible for IP accountability, reconciliation, and record maintenance (ie, receipt, reconciliation, and final disposition records).

trans-unit.source: Further guidance and information for the final disposition of unused IPs are provided in the IP Manual.
trans-unit.target: Further guidance and information for the final disposition of unused IPs are provided in the IP Manual.

trans-unit.source: Storage of Study Interventions: Tozorakimab and Placebo
trans-unit.target: Storage of Study Interventions: Tozorakimab and Placebo

trans-unit.source: IP (tozorakimab and tozorakimab placebo) APFS are stored at 2°C to 8°C (36°F to 46°F) and must not be frozen. The Investigator, or an approved representative (eg, pharmacist), will ensure that all study intervention is stored in a secured area, in refrigerated temperatures (2°C to 8°C; 36°F to 46°F) and in accordance with applicable regulatory requirements. A temperature log will be used to record the temperature of the storage area. Temperature excursions outside the permissible range listed in the clinical supply packaging are to be reported to the site monitor upon detection. A calibrated temperature‑monitoring device will be used to record the temperature conditions in the drug storage facility. Storage conditions stated in the IB may be superseded by the label storage.
trans-unit.target: IP (tozorakimab and tozorakimab placebo) APFS are stored at 2°C to 8°C (36°F to 46°F) and must not be frozen. The Investigator, or an approved representative (eg, pharmacist), will ensure that all study intervention is stored in a secured area, in refrigerated temperatures (2°C to 8°C; 36°F to 46°F) and in accordance with applicable regulatory requirements. A temperature log will be used to record the temperature of the storage area. Temperature excursions outside the permissible range listed in the clinical supply packaging are to be reported to the site monitor upon detection. A calibrated temperature‑monitoring device will be used to record the temperature conditions in the drug storage facility. Storage conditions stated in the IB may be superseded by the label storage.

trans-unit.source: Study intervention must be kept in original packaging until time of use to prevent prolonged light exposure.
trans-unit.target: Study intervention must be kept in original packaging until time of use to prevent prolonged light exposure.

trans-unit.source: Tozorakimab and Placebo SC Injection Preparation and Administration
trans-unit.target: Tozorakimab and Placebo SC Injection Preparation and Administration

trans-unit.source: Tozorakimab is a sterile, preservative-free, clear to opalescent, colourless to brown-yellow solution, practically free from visible particles.
trans-unit.target: Tozorakimab is a sterile, preservative-free, clear to opalescent, colourless to brown-yellow solution, practically free from visible particles.

trans-unit.source: Each APFS selected for administration should be inspected. If there are any defects noted with the IP, the Investigator and site monitor should be notified immediately. Refer to the Product Complaint section of the IP Manual and to Section 8.3.13.3.
trans-unit.target: Each APFS selected for administration should be inspected. If there are any defects noted with the IP, the Investigator and site monitor should be notified immediately. Refer to the Product Complaint section of the IP Manual and to Section 8.3.13.3.

trans-unit.source: Tozorakimab and placebo should be kept at room temperature for at least 60 minutes inside the original packaging prior to administration. The dose of tozorakimab or placebo must be administered at room temperature within 8 hours after removal of the APFS from refrigeration.
trans-unit.target: Tozorakimab and placebo should be kept at room temperature for at least 60 minutes inside the original packaging prior to administration. The dose of tozorakimab or placebo must be administered at room temperature within 8 hours after removal of the APFS from refrigeration.

trans-unit.source: Tozorakimab and placebo do not contain preservatives; any unused portion of the APFS must be discarded immediately after use.
trans-unit.target: Tozorakimab and placebo do not contain preservatives; any unused portion of the APFS must be discarded immediately after use.

trans-unit.source: A dose of 300 mg tozorakimab or placebo will be administered using one APFS injection according to standard procedures for SC injections.
trans-unit.target: A dose of 300 mg tozorakimab or placebo will be administered using one APFS injection according to standard procedures for SC injections.

trans-unit.source: The first day of dosing in PROSPERO is considered Visit 1 (Week 0).
trans-unit.target: The first day of dosing in PROSPERO is considered Visit 1 (Week 0).

trans-unit.source: All applicable visit procedures, including sample collection, should be completed prior to IP administration. If the participant cannot tolerate a particular visit procedure during the visit (eg, spirometry), IP can be administered, and the procedure should be rescheduled for a different date, preferably within the visit window. No procedures should be performed after the dosing on the day of IP administration. Pharmacokinetic and ADA samples must always be collected before IP administration.
trans-unit.target: All applicable visit procedures, including sample collection, should be completed prior to IP administration. If the participant cannot tolerate a particular visit procedure during the visit (eg, spirometry), IP can be administered, and the procedure should be rescheduled for a different date, preferably within the visit window. No procedures should be performed after the dosing on the day of IP administration. Pharmacokinetic and ADA samples must always be collected before IP administration.

trans-unit.source: FOCBP should have the urine pregnancy test performed before IP administration. The IP will be administered only when the result of the test is negative (Section 8.2.6).
trans-unit.target: FOCBP should have the urine pregnancy test performed before IP administration. The IP will be administered only when the result of the test is negative (Section 8.2.6).

trans-unit.source: Investigator/authorised delegate will evaluate the participant’s condition for potential contraindications for dosing.
trans-unit.target: Investigator/authorised delegate will evaluate the participant’s condition for potential contraindications for dosing.

trans-unit.source: Investigator/authorised delegate will evaluate the injection site as per standards of medical care.
trans-unit.target: Investigator/authorised delegate will evaluate the injection site as per standards of medical care.

trans-unit.source: The IP will be administered SC by the Investigator/or an appropriate designee trained in study drug administration to the abdomen, upper arm, or thigh. The personnel administering the dose will wipe the skin surface of the administration site with alcohol and allow the skin surface to air dry. The skin will be pinched to isolate the SC tissue from the muscle. The needle will be fully inserted at a 45-degree angle into the SC tissue. The IP will be slowly
trans-unit.target: The IP will be administered SC by the Investigator/or an appropriate designee trained in study drug administration to the abdomen, upper arm, or thigh. The personnel administering the dose will wipe the skin surface of the administration site with alcohol and allow the skin surface to air dry. The skin will be pinched to isolate the SC tissue from the muscle. The needle will be fully inserted at a 45-degree angle into the SC tissue. The IP will be slowly

trans-unit.source: injected (at least 10 seconds duration is recommended). The area should not be massaged after injection.
trans-unit.target: injected (at least 10 seconds duration is recommended). The area should not be massaged after injection.

trans-unit.source: It is recommended that the site of injection be rotated so that the participant receives IP injections in different anatomical regions from one visit to the next. In cases when rotation of the injection site is not feasible and/or the participant prefers not to rotate injection sites, the reason for not rotating the injection site should be documented in the source documents.
trans-unit.target: It is recommended that the site of injection be rotated so that the participant receives IP injections in different anatomical regions from one visit to the next. In cases when rotation of the injection site is not feasible and/or the participant prefers not to rotate injection sites, the reason for not rotating the injection site should be documented in the source documents.

trans-unit.source: Each injection site must be documented on the eCRF and in the source documents at each IP visit.
trans-unit.target: Each injection site must be documented on the eCRF and in the source documents at each IP visit.

trans-unit.source: To reduce participant burden after Visit 4, participants will have an option for self- administration of IP using the APFS by the participant or their caregiver. The Investigator must evaluate the participant and/or their caregiver for suitability of performing the IP self- administration and provide them with appropriate training during onsite visits. All necessary supplies and instructions for administration and documentation of IP administration will be provided.
trans-unit.target: To reduce participant burden after Visit 4, participants will have an option for self- administration of IP using the APFS by the participant or their caregiver. The Investigator must evaluate the participant and/or their caregiver for suitability of performing the IP self- administration and provide them with appropriate training during onsite visits. All necessary supplies and instructions for administration and documentation of IP administration will be provided.

trans-unit.source: Starting from Visit 5, some visits do not include extensive onsite assessments and can optionally be performed as remote visits through telemedicine contact (Section 1.3) for participants who opt for IP self-administration. IP kit and urine dipsticks for pregnancy testing (for FOCBP) for the remote visits may be dispensed starting from Visit 4 (a single kit per time for the next remote visit), given that a mandatory training of optional IP self-administration is completed.
trans-unit.target: Starting from Visit 5, some visits do not include extensive onsite assessments and can optionally be performed as remote visits through telemedicine contact (Section 1.3) for participants who opt for IP self-administration. IP kit and urine dipsticks for pregnancy testing (for FOCBP) for the remote visits may be dispensed starting from Visit 4 (a single kit per time for the next remote visit), given that a mandatory training of optional IP self-administration is completed.

trans-unit.source: If the IP is self-administered by the participant/caregiver, IP should be administered on a day of a scheduled visit after all visit assessments are completed.
trans-unit.target: If the IP is self-administered by the participant/caregiver, IP should be administered on a day of a scheduled visit after all visit assessments are completed.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator or qualified designee will conduct a telemedicine contact on the day of the visit prior to IP self-administration to confirm that the participant does not have any contraindications for IP dosing. FOCBP will be asked about any concerns for pregnancy and will be advised to perform a urine pregnancy test.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator or qualified designee will conduct a telemedicine contact on the day of the visit prior to IP self-administration to confirm that the participant does not have any contraindications for IP dosing. FOCBP will be asked about any concerns for pregnancy and will be advised to perform a urine pregnancy test.

trans-unit.source: If the urine pregnancy test result is positive, the participant should contact the site and should not self-administer IP. Site personnel will provide the participant with further instructions.
trans-unit.target: If the urine pregnancy test result is positive, the participant should contact the site and should not self-administer IP. Site personnel will provide the participant with further instructions.

trans-unit.source: The participant must be contacted by the Investigator or qualified designee after the IP is self- administered, to evaluate for any potential acute reactions, in line with local clinical practice. For optional at-home self or caregiver administration, it is strongly recommended that the Investigator, or a designee, remains on the telemedicine call during the IP administration. Any AEs reported by the participant should be recorded in source documents and entered in the
trans-unit.target: The participant must be contacted by the Investigator or qualified designee after the IP is self- administered, to evaluate for any potential acute reactions, in line with local clinical practice. For optional at-home self or caregiver administration, it is strongly recommended that the Investigator, or a designee, remains on the telemedicine call during the IP administration. Any AEs reported by the participant should be recorded in source documents and entered in the

trans-unit.source: eCRF. Furthermore, any device constituent deficiency reported by the participant should be recorded and handled as outlined in Section 8.3.12.
trans-unit.target: eCRF. Furthermore, any device constituent deficiency reported by the participant should be recorded and handled as outlined in Section 8.3.12.

trans-unit.source: Refer to the Study Instructions for Self-Administration of IP by the Participant and/or their Caregiver for step-by-step guidance including Investigator assessment/training of participant and/or caregiver, IP dispensing and drug accountability.
trans-unit.target: Refer to the Study Instructions for Self-Administration of IP by the Participant and/or their Caregiver for step-by-step guidance including Investigator assessment/training of participant and/or caregiver, IP dispensing and drug accountability.

trans-unit.source: The option of self-administration of IP will only be available in countries where allowed according to local regulations.
trans-unit.target: The option of self-administration of IP will only be available in countries where allowed according to local regulations.

trans-unit.source: After the administration of IP, the SC injection site will be assessed for any reactions as detailed in Section 8.3.6, and the participant should be observed for potential acute drug reactions in line with local clinical practice for mAbs. For optional at-home self or caregiver administration, it is strongly recommended that the Investigator, or a designee, remains on the telemedicine call during the IP administration.
trans-unit.target: After the administration of IP, the SC injection site will be assessed for any reactions as detailed in Section 8.3.6, and the participant should be observed for potential acute drug reactions in line with local clinical practice for mAbs. For optional at-home self or caregiver administration, it is strongly recommended that the Investigator, or a designee, remains on the telemedicine call during the IP administration.

trans-unit.source: As with any biologic product, allergic reactions to dose administration are possible. Therefore, appropriate drugs and medical equipment to treat acute anaphylactic reactions should be immediately available, and study personnel should be trained to recognise and treat anaphylaxis (Appendix F and Appendix G).
trans-unit.target: As with any biologic product, allergic reactions to dose administration are possible. Therefore, appropriate drugs and medical equipment to treat acute anaphylactic reactions should be immediately available, and study personnel should be trained to recognise and treat anaphylaxis (Appendix F and Appendix G).

trans-unit.source: The site’s designated IP manager is required to maintain accurate IP accountability records. Upon completion of the study, copies of IP accountability records will be returned to the Sponsor. All unused IP will be disposed of following site procedures and upon authorisation by the Sponsor or will be returned to a Sponsor-authorised depot if a site is unable to dispose of unused IP. The outer carton should be retained onsite to assist with kit assignment checks, if permitted by site procedures.
trans-unit.target: The site’s designated IP manager is required to maintain accurate IP accountability records. Upon completion of the study, copies of IP accountability records will be returned to the Sponsor. All unused IP will be disposed of following site procedures and upon authorisation by the Sponsor or will be returned to a Sponsor-authorised depot if a site is unable to dispose of unused IP. The outer carton should be retained onsite to assist with kit assignment checks, if permitted by site procedures.

trans-unit.source: Measures to Minimise Bias: Randomisation and Blinding
trans-unit.target: Measures to Minimise Bias: Randomisation and Blinding

trans-unit.source: All participants assigned to the placebo arm in the OBERON or TITANIA predecessor studies will be assigned to randomised IP in PROSPERO using an RTSM as follows:
trans-unit.target: All participants assigned to the placebo arm in the OBERON or TITANIA predecessor studies will be assigned to randomised IP in PROSPERO using an RTSM as follows:

trans-unit.source: Participants previously treated with tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W SC will stay on the same blinded treatment.
trans-unit.target: Participants previously treated with tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W SC will stay on the same blinded treatment.

trans-unit.source: Participants previously treated with tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W SC will stay on the same blinded treatment.
trans-unit.target: Participants previously treated with tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W SC will stay on the same blinded treatment.

trans-unit.source: Participants previously randomised to placebo will be re-randomised in 1:1:2 ratio to blinded tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W SC, tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W SC, or placebo.
trans-unit.target: Participants previously randomised to placebo will be re-randomised in 1:1:2 ratio to blinded tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W SC, tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W SC, or placebo.

trans-unit.source: Participants previously treated with tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W SC will stay on the same blinded treatment.
trans-unit.target: Participants previously treated with tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W SC will stay on the same blinded treatment.

trans-unit.source: Participants previously treated with tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W SC will switch to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W SC at the point of entry to PROSPERO.
trans-unit.target: Participants previously treated with tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W SC will switch to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W SC at the point of entry to PROSPERO.

trans-unit.source: Participants previously randomised to placebo will be re-randomised in 1:1 ratio to blinded tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W SC or placebo.
trans-unit.target: Participants previously randomised to placebo will be re-randomised in 1:1 ratio to blinded tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W SC or placebo.

trans-unit.source: Participants allocated or re-randomised to tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W under CSP Version 1.0 will continue on the same treatment until the study completion.
trans-unit.target: Participants allocated or re-randomised to tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W under CSP Version 1.0 will continue on the same treatment until the study completion.

trans-unit.source: The randomisation scheme will be generated for all OBERON and TITANIA participants regardless of predecessor study completion, and not only in those participants that complete OBERON and TITANIA; this will ensure that the comparison of treatment groups in an integrated analysis will be valid from the point of randomisation in the predecessor studies and not only in a selected subgroup of participants able to continue with long term treatment.
trans-unit.target: The randomisation scheme will be generated for all OBERON and TITANIA participants regardless of predecessor study completion, and not only in those participants that complete OBERON and TITANIA; this will ensure that the comparison of treatment groups in an integrated analysis will be valid from the point of randomisation in the predecessor studies and not only in a selected subgroup of participants able to continue with long term treatment.

trans-unit.source: Randomisation codes will be assigned strictly sequentially in each stratum as participants become eligible for randomisation. Randomisation will be stratified by predecessor study (OBERON versus TITANIA), smoking status (current versus former), maintenance inhaled therapy (dual versus triple), and region, with these strata determined as per randomisation to the predecessor study regardless of any subsequent changes.
trans-unit.target: Randomisation codes will be assigned strictly sequentially in each stratum as participants become eligible for randomisation. Randomisation will be stratified by predecessor study (OBERON versus TITANIA), smoking status (current versus former), maintenance inhaled therapy (dual versus triple), and region, with these strata determined as per randomisation to the predecessor study regardless of any subsequent changes.

trans-unit.source: Participants who fail to meet the inclusion/exclusion criteria should not, under any circumstances, be randomised, or receive IP. There can be no exceptions to this rule. If a participant withdraws from the study after enrolment, then his or her enrolment/randomisation code cannot be reused. Withdrawn participants will not be replaced.
trans-unit.target: Participants who fail to meet the inclusion/exclusion criteria should not, under any circumstances, be randomised, or receive IP. There can be no exceptions to this rule. If a participant withdraws from the study after enrolment, then his or her enrolment/randomisation code cannot be reused. Withdrawn participants will not be replaced.

trans-unit.source: This is a double-blind study in which the investigational site staff and participants remain blinded during the clinical study. To ensure study blinding, yellow blinding labels will be affixed onto both tozorakimab and placebo syringe barrels during APFS manufacture. All packaging and labelling of IP will be done in such way as to ensure blinding for all participants, Sponsor, and investigational site staff.
trans-unit.target: This is a double-blind study in which the investigational site staff and participants remain blinded during the clinical study. To ensure study blinding, yellow blinding labels will be affixed onto both tozorakimab and placebo syringe barrels during APFS manufacture. All packaging and labelling of IP will be done in such way as to ensure blinding for all participants, Sponsor, and investigational site staff.

trans-unit.source: Investigators, blinded Sponsor, and investigational site staff and participants will remain blinded to each participant’s assigned IP throughout the course of the study, but will know which version of the CSP the participant was recruited under, and therefore if randomisation to Q8W arm is possible.
trans-unit.target: Investigators, blinded Sponsor, and investigational site staff and participants will remain blinded to each participant’s assigned IP throughout the course of the study, but will know which version of the CSP the participant was recruited under, and therefore if randomisation to Q8W arm is possible.

trans-unit.source: Tozorakimab could reduce eosinophil counts in blood over time. As a precaution, eosinophil, basophil, and monocyte data, with the exception of HP values, from the haematology laboratory tests will not be communicated to blinded Sponsor or site personnel during the intervention and follow-up periods. There is no need to apply blinding to local laboratory tests taken as part of treatment for acute illness (eg, actual/suspected COPD exacerbation).
trans-unit.target: Tozorakimab could reduce eosinophil counts in blood over time. As a precaution, eosinophil, basophil, and monocyte data, with the exception of HP values, from the haematology laboratory tests will not be communicated to blinded Sponsor or site personnel during the intervention and follow-up periods. There is no need to apply blinding to local laboratory tests taken as part of treatment for acute illness (eg, actual/suspected COPD exacerbation).

trans-unit.source: Procedures to maintain the blind are described in the Study Integrity Plan.
trans-unit.target: Procedures to maintain the blind are described in the Study Integrity Plan.

trans-unit.source: A Sponsor site monitor will perform IP accountability. In the event that the treatment allocation for a participant becomes known to the Investigator or other study staff involved in the management of study participants or needs to be known to treat an individual participant for an AE, the Sponsor must be notified immediately by the Investigator and, if possible, before unblinding.
trans-unit.target: A Sponsor site monitor will perform IP accountability. In the event that the treatment allocation for a participant becomes known to the Investigator or other study staff involved in the management of study participants or needs to be known to treat an individual participant for an AE, the Sponsor must be notified immediately by the Investigator and, if possible, before unblinding.

trans-unit.source: The following personnel will have access to the randomisation list during the study, prior to database lock:
trans-unit.target: The following personnel will have access to the randomisation list during the study, prior to database lock:

trans-unit.source: Those carrying out the packaging and labelling of IP.
trans-unit.target: Those carrying out the packaging and labelling of IP.

trans-unit.source: Bioanalytical PK and ADA laboratories responsible for analysing the samples.
trans-unit.target: Bioanalytical PK and ADA laboratories responsible for analysing the samples.

trans-unit.source: The information in the randomisation list will be kept from other personnel involved in the conduct of the study in a secure location until the primary analysis of the study. No other member of the extended study team at the Sponsor, or any CRO handling data, will have access to the randomisation scheme during this time.
trans-unit.target: The information in the randomisation list will be kept from other personnel involved in the conduct of the study in a secure location until the primary analysis of the study. No other member of the extended study team at the Sponsor, or any CRO handling data, will have access to the randomisation scheme during this time.

trans-unit.source: Although the study will initially be completely double-blind (ie, blind for participants, Investigators/site staff, and AstraZeneca /designated CRO), at the conclusion of each of the predecessor studies (OBERON or TITANIA), the treatment allocation within the predecessor studies will become known to the AstraZeneca staff and/or designated CRO involved in reporting of the predecessor studies. The PROSPERO study team in direct contact with sites or with influence over management of participants or recording of endpoints will remain blinded and be separate from this team and access to systems or documents containing treatment allocations in OBERON/TITANIA will be controlled at least until after the unblinding of PROSPERO to conduct the primary analysis. The blind will be maintained for the Investigator, investigational site staff, and for the participants at least until the primary reporting of the PROSPERO study is completed. Procedures to maintain this blind will be described in the Study Integrity Plan.
trans-unit.target: Although the study will initially be completely double-blind (ie, blind for participants, Investigators/site staff, and AstraZeneca /designated CRO), at the conclusion of each of the predecessor studies (OBERON or TITANIA), the treatment allocation within the predecessor studies will become known to the AstraZeneca staff and/or designated CRO involved in reporting of the predecessor studies. The PROSPERO study team in direct contact with sites or with influence over management of participants or recording of endpoints will remain blinded and be separate from this team and access to systems or documents containing treatment allocations in OBERON/TITANIA will be controlled at least until after the unblinding of PROSPERO to conduct the primary analysis. The blind will be maintained for the Investigator, investigational site staff, and for the participants at least until the primary reporting of the PROSPERO study is completed. Procedures to maintain this blind will be described in the Study Integrity Plan.

trans-unit.source: Information indicating the treatment randomisation for each randomised participant, will be available to the Investigator(s) from the RTSM. Routines for this will be described in the RTSM user manual that will be provided to each site. The randomisation code should not be broken except in medical emergencies when the appropriate management of the participant requires knowledge of the treatment randomisation. The Investigator will document and report the action to the Sponsor, without revealing the participant’s treatment randomisation to the Sponsor staff. The Sponsor retains the right to break the code for SAEs that are unexpected and are suspected to be causally related to IP and that potentially require expedited reporting to regulatory authorities. Randomisation codes will not be broken for the planned analyses of data until all decisions on the evaluability of the data from each individual participant have been made and documented.
trans-unit.target: Information indicating the treatment randomisation for each randomised participant, will be available to the Investigator(s) from the RTSM. Routines for this will be described in the RTSM user manual that will be provided to each site. The randomisation code should not be broken except in medical emergencies when the appropriate management of the participant requires knowledge of the treatment randomisation. The Investigator will document and report the action to the Sponsor, without revealing the participant’s treatment randomisation to the Sponsor staff. The Sponsor retains the right to break the code for SAEs that are unexpected and are suspected to be causally related to IP and that potentially require expedited reporting to regulatory authorities. Randomisation codes will not be broken for the planned analyses of data until all decisions on the evaluability of the data from each individual participant have been made and documented.

trans-unit.source: When participants are dosed at an onsite visit, they will receive IP under medical supervision. The date and time of dose administered in the clinic will be recorded in the source documents and recorded in the eCRF. The outer carton should be kept onsite to assist with kit assignment checks, if permitted by site procedures.
trans-unit.target: When participants are dosed at an onsite visit, they will receive IP under medical supervision. The date and time of dose administered in the clinic will be recorded in the source documents and recorded in the eCRF. The outer carton should be kept onsite to assist with kit assignment checks, if permitted by site procedures.

trans-unit.source: Refer to Section 6.2.2.3 and the Study Instructions for Self-Administration of Investigational Product by the Participant and/or their Caregiver for procedures related to participant/caregiver self-administration.
trans-unit.target: Refer to Section 6.2.2.3 and the Study Instructions for Self-Administration of Investigational Product by the Participant and/or their Caregiver for procedures related to participant/caregiver self-administration.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator/authorised delegate will collect and record any medication taken from the time of enrolment until the final study visit. All background COPD medications (eg, ICS/LABA/LAMA, antibiotics) and treatment administered for COPD exacerbations, all other medications taken for any reason, and any concomitant medication(s), including herbal preparations, taken during the study will be recorded in source documentation and in eCRF, along with:
trans-unit.target: The Investigator/authorised delegate will collect and record any medication taken from the time of enrolment until the final study visit. All background COPD medications (eg, ICS/LABA/LAMA, antibiotics) and treatment administered for COPD exacerbations, all other medications taken for any reason, and any concomitant medication(s), including herbal preparations, taken during the study will be recorded in source documentation and in eCRF, along with:

trans-unit.source: Dates of administration including start and end dates.
trans-unit.target: Dates of administration including start and end dates.

trans-unit.source: Dosage information including dose, frequency, and route of administration.
trans-unit.target: Dosage information including dose, frequency, and route of administration.

trans-unit.source: The medical monitor should be contacted if there are any questions regarding concomitant or prior therapy.
trans-unit.target: The medical monitor should be contacted if there are any questions regarding concomitant or prior therapy.

trans-unit.source: Participants are prohibited from receiving certain concomitant medications (Section 6.5.4) unless clinically indicated as determined by a physician.
trans-unit.target: Participants are prohibited from receiving certain concomitant medications (Section 6.5.4) unless clinically indicated as determined by a physician.

trans-unit.source: Medications or therapies that are not prohibited and neither compromise participant safety nor affect study data, as judged by the Investigator, will be permitted and recorded in the source documentation and in the appropriate sections of the eCRF.
trans-unit.target: Medications or therapies that are not prohibited and neither compromise participant safety nor affect study data, as judged by the Investigator, will be permitted and recorded in the source documentation and in the appropriate sections of the eCRF.

trans-unit.source: Refer to Appendix J for auxiliary medicinal products as applicable to EU CTR.
trans-unit.target: Refer to Appendix J for auxiliary medicinal products as applicable to EU CTR.

trans-unit.source: Short-acting β2-agonists (SABAs, eg, salbutamol, albuterol, terbutaline, levalbuterol), SAMA, SABA/SAMA combination or alternative rescue medication, as per local SoC, may be used during the study in the event of worsening of COPD symptoms. Note medication restrictions prior to spirometry visits (Section 6.5.3).
trans-unit.target: Short-acting β2-agonists (SABAs, eg, salbutamol, albuterol, terbutaline, levalbuterol), SAMA, SABA/SAMA combination or alternative rescue medication, as per local SoC, may be used during the study in the event of worsening of COPD symptoms. Note medication restrictions prior to spirometry visits (Section 6.5.3).

trans-unit.source: All participants are required to be treated with stable optimised maintenance inhaled therapy (ICS/LABA/LAMA triple therapy, or dual therapy if triple is not considered appropriate) during the study. Both dual and triple therapy may be in the form of separate inhalers or fixed dose combination inhalers. Individual component changes or switches between devices are allowed as long as the participant remains on the same equivalent doses. If antibiotics or PDE4 inhibitors were added to the background therapy and regimen, the doses should be stable throughout the study. Xanthines are allowed as maintenance therapy provided that the dose is either equivalent to theophylline ≤ 400 mg per day or serum drug level within the therapeutic range (recommend blood test to confirm < 12 mg/dL). Changes to the participant’s maintenance therapies are strongly discouraged during the study, unless judged medically necessary by the Investigator. The justification and rationale for treatment changes should be documented in the source notes.
trans-unit.target: All participants are required to be treated with stable optimised maintenance inhaled therapy (ICS/LABA/LAMA triple therapy, or dual therapy if triple is not considered appropriate) during the study. Both dual and triple therapy may be in the form of separate inhalers or fixed dose combination inhalers. Individual component changes or switches between devices are allowed as long as the participant remains on the same equivalent doses. If antibiotics or PDE4 inhibitors were added to the background therapy and regimen, the doses should be stable throughout the study. Xanthines are allowed as maintenance therapy provided that the dose is either equivalent to theophylline ≤ 400 mg per day or serum drug level within the therapeutic range (recommend blood test to confirm < 12 mg/dL). Changes to the participant’s maintenance therapies are strongly discouraged during the study, unless judged medically necessary by the Investigator. The justification and rationale for treatment changes should be documented in the source notes.

trans-unit.source: Note: Meaning of “stable” or “unchanged” COPD maintenance therapy in this protocol allows for individual component changes or switches between devices provided that the participant remains on the same class therapies at equivalent doses (for examples of ICS equivalent doses, refer to Appendix I).
trans-unit.target: Note: Meaning of “stable” or “unchanged” COPD maintenance therapy in this protocol allows for individual component changes or switches between devices provided that the participant remains on the same class therapies at equivalent doses (for examples of ICS equivalent doses, refer to Appendix I).

trans-unit.source: Recommended COPD inhaled maintenance therapy compliance during the treatment period is
trans-unit.target: Recommended COPD inhaled maintenance therapy compliance during the treatment period is

trans-unit.source: ≥ 70%, as defined by medication start/stop dates, and the participant response to “in the past month, how many days a week have you taken your maintenance medication as scheduled?” at each visit, recorded in the source documentation. It is recommended that if compliance levels fall below 70%, the site/Investigator should evaluate the reasons for low compliance and to provide re-education to the participant in regard to the importance of compliance in taking their COPD inhaled maintenance therapy. It is strongly recommended that when compliance levels fall below 50%, the site contacts the Sponsor study physician in order to discuss the potential safety implication for the participant and future decisions.
trans-unit.target: ≥ 70%, as defined by medication start/stop dates, and the participant response to “in the past month, how many days a week have you taken your maintenance medication as scheduled?” at each visit, recorded in the source documentation. It is recommended that if compliance levels fall below 70%, the site/Investigator should evaluate the reasons for low compliance and to provide re-education to the participant in regard to the importance of compliance in taking their COPD inhaled maintenance therapy. It is strongly recommended that when compliance levels fall below 50%, the site contacts the Sponsor study physician in order to discuss the potential safety implication for the participant and future decisions.

trans-unit.source: All participants should be vaccinated against pneumococcus and influenza in accordance with local guidelines, unless vaccination is contraindicated or rejected by the participant, or is not available. Pneumococcal and influenza vaccination can be administered at any time through the study (except ± 7 days of IP administration) at the discretion of the Investigator.
trans-unit.target: All participants should be vaccinated against pneumococcus and influenza in accordance with local guidelines, unless vaccination is contraindicated or rejected by the participant, or is not available. Pneumococcal and influenza vaccination can be administered at any time through the study (except ± 7 days of IP administration) at the discretion of the Investigator.

trans-unit.source: Spirometry assessments will be performed at the study site at scheduled visits (Section 1.3). There are restrictions regarding the participant’s COPD medications prior to the spirometry assessments.
trans-unit.target: Spirometry assessments will be performed at the study site at scheduled visits (Section 1.3). There are restrictions regarding the participant’s COPD medications prior to the spirometry assessments.

trans-unit.source: Participants will be asked to withhold their usual maintenance inhaled therapy (ICS/LABA/LAMA triple therapy, or dual therapy if triple is not considered appropriate) on the mornings of the scheduled pre-BD spirometry visits, preferably for at least 12 hours prior to the spirometry assessment (24 hours prior to the assessment for once-daily medications). In addition, SABA and SAMA should not be used within 6 hours prior to the spirometry assessments. The participant’s usual COPD medications may be administered following completion of the pre-BD spirometry. The IP will be administered at a scheduled visit after the spirometry assessments.
trans-unit.target: Participants will be asked to withhold their usual maintenance inhaled therapy (ICS/LABA/LAMA triple therapy, or dual therapy if triple is not considered appropriate) on the mornings of the scheduled pre-BD spirometry visits, preferably for at least 12 hours prior to the spirometry assessment (24 hours prior to the assessment for once-daily medications). In addition, SABA and SAMA should not be used within 6 hours prior to the spirometry assessments. The participant’s usual COPD medications may be administered following completion of the pre-BD spirometry. The IP will be administered at a scheduled visit after the spirometry assessments.

trans-unit.source: If the participant has taken their usual maintenance inhaled therapy (ICS/LABA/LAMA triple therapy, or dual therapy if triple is not considered appropriate) on the morning of the scheduled spirometry visit, the Investigator/authorised delegate should remind the participant of the importance of withholding their usual morning maintenance inhaled therapy and reschedule the spirometry assessment for another day, within the allowed window. If rescheduling is absolutely not feasible for the participant, spirometry may be performed with a notation in source documentation and TPV system indicating that spirometry was performed after the usual optimised maintenance inhaled therapies were administered.
trans-unit.target: If the participant has taken their usual maintenance inhaled therapy (ICS/LABA/LAMA triple therapy, or dual therapy if triple is not considered appropriate) on the morning of the scheduled spirometry visit, the Investigator/authorised delegate should remind the participant of the importance of withholding their usual morning maintenance inhaled therapy and reschedule the spirometry assessment for another day, within the allowed window. If rescheduling is absolutely not feasible for the participant, spirometry may be performed with a notation in source documentation and TPV system indicating that spirometry was performed after the usual optimised maintenance inhaled therapies were administered.

trans-unit.source: If the participant has taken rescue SABA or SAMA within 6 hours of the planned spirometry visit, they should ideally remain at the site until such time that the 6 hours withholding time has been reached or return on another day within the visit window. If neither option is feasible for the participant, spirometry may be performed with a notation indicating that the lung volume measurements and/or spirometry were conducted within 6 hours of SABA/SAMA use.
trans-unit.target: If the participant has taken rescue SABA or SAMA within 6 hours of the planned spirometry visit, they should ideally remain at the site until such time that the 6 hours withholding time has been reached or return on another day within the visit window. If neither option is feasible for the participant, spirometry may be performed with a notation indicating that the lung volume measurements and/or spirometry were conducted within 6 hours of SABA/SAMA use.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor accepts that vaccination against COVID-19, when and where it is available and if participants are eligible according to applicable guidelines, is in the participants’ best interest. Consequently, vaccination during the study is, in general, permitted and any delays in vaccination due to study participation should be minimised.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor accepts that vaccination against COVID-19, when and where it is available and if participants are eligible according to applicable guidelines, is in the participants’ best interest. Consequently, vaccination during the study is, in general, permitted and any delays in vaccination due to study participation should be minimised.

trans-unit.source: To ensure the interpretability of safety data, receipt of COVID-19 vaccine (either first or any subsequent dose) is prohibited within 7 days before or after any dose of IP.
trans-unit.target: To ensure the interpretability of safety data, receipt of COVID-19 vaccine (either first or any subsequent dose) is prohibited within 7 days before or after any dose of IP.

trans-unit.source: For all study participants, on-study COVID-19 vaccination including data regarding the date, dose, brand name, ordinal dose number (eg, first, second, etc), and site of administration (if applicable) (eg, Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 Vaccine first dose left deltoid) should be recorded in the participant’s medical records and as a concomitant medication in eCRF. The site of administration should be collected if a participant receives a COVID-19 vaccination during study participation and an injection site reaction is observed after the vaccination.
trans-unit.target: For all study participants, on-study COVID-19 vaccination including data regarding the date, dose, brand name, ordinal dose number (eg, first, second, etc), and site of administration (if applicable) (eg, Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 Vaccine first dose left deltoid) should be recorded in the participant’s medical records and as a concomitant medication in eCRF. The site of administration should be collected if a participant receives a COVID-19 vaccination during study participation and an injection site reaction is observed after the vaccination.

trans-unit.source: Any AEs suspected to be due to the vaccination should be captured in the AE form including the causality assessment related to COVID-19 vaccine.
trans-unit.target: Any AEs suspected to be due to the vaccination should be captured in the AE form including the causality assessment related to COVID-19 vaccine.

trans-unit.source: This protocol does not allow for alteration from the currently outlined dosing schedule.
trans-unit.target: This protocol does not allow for alteration from the currently outlined dosing schedule.

trans-unit.source: Intervention After the End of the Study
trans-unit.target: Intervention After the End of the Study

trans-unit.source: After completing the study, participants should be given locally available SoC at the discretion of the Investigator/treating physician.
trans-unit.target: After completing the study, participants should be given locally available SoC at the discretion of the Investigator/treating physician.

trans-unit.source: DISCONTINUATION OF INVESTIGATIONAL PRODUCT AND PARTICIPANT DISCONTINUATION/WITHDRAWAL
trans-unit.target: DISCONTINUATION OF INVESTIGATIONAL PRODUCT AND PARTICIPANT DISCONTINUATION/WITHDRAWAL

trans-unit.source: Discontinuation from IP does NOT automatically lead to a complete withdrawal from the study. Participants discontinuing from IP are strongly encouraged to continue in the study until the end of treatment period as described in Section 7.1.1.
trans-unit.target: Discontinuation from IP does NOT automatically lead to a complete withdrawal from the study. Participants discontinuing from IP are strongly encouraged to continue in the study until the end of treatment period as described in Section 7.1.1.

trans-unit.source: Before a decision to discontinue a participant from IP is instituted, the Investigator should carefully consider whether continuation on IP or discontinuation of IP will be in the best interest of the participant and whether the issue can be mitigated by postponing or skipping the dose. It is recommended that the Sponsor study physician be consulted before the IP discontinuation transaction takes place. In case of safety concerns, IP administration must be immediately withheld until the final decision is made (refer to Section 6.1.2).
trans-unit.target: Before a decision to discontinue a participant from IP is instituted, the Investigator should carefully consider whether continuation on IP or discontinuation of IP will be in the best interest of the participant and whether the issue can be mitigated by postponing or skipping the dose. It is recommended that the Sponsor study physician be consulted before the IP discontinuation transaction takes place. In case of safety concerns, IP administration must be immediately withheld until the final decision is made (refer to Section 6.1.2).

trans-unit.source: Participants may be discontinued from IP in the following situations:
trans-unit.target: Participants may be discontinued from IP in the following situations:

trans-unit.source: Risk to participant as judged by the Investigator or the Sponsor.
trans-unit.target: Risk to participant as judged by the Investigator or the Sponsor.

trans-unit.source: Anaphylactic reaction to IP requiring epinephrine administration.
trans-unit.target: Anaphylactic reaction to IP requiring epinephrine administration.

trans-unit.source: Eligibility requirement found not to be fulfilled that in the opinion of the Investigator or Sponsor may put the participant at risk. If a participant does not meet all the eligibility criteria but is randomised in error, the Investigator should inform the Sponsor study physician immediately to discuss potential safety concerns and the best interests of the participant, and decide on the participant’s further disposition.
trans-unit.target: Eligibility requirement found not to be fulfilled that in the opinion of the Investigator or Sponsor may put the participant at risk. If a participant does not meet all the eligibility criteria but is randomised in error, the Investigator should inform the Sponsor study physician immediately to discuss potential safety concerns and the best interests of the participant, and decide on the participant’s further disposition.

trans-unit.source: The time elapsed since last dose of IP exceeds 12 weeks.
trans-unit.target: The time elapsed since last dose of IP exceeds 12 weeks.

trans-unit.source: Refer to the SoA (Section 1.3) for data to be collected at the time of intervention discontinuation and follow-up and for any further evaluations that need to be completed.
trans-unit.target: Refer to the SoA (Section 1.3) for data to be collected at the time of intervention discontinuation and follow-up and for any further evaluations that need to be completed.

trans-unit.source: The reason for premature discontinuation of IP should be documented in source documentation and recorded in the eCRF. Conditions that require administration of IP to be temporarily withheld are described in Section 6.1.2.
trans-unit.target: The reason for premature discontinuation of IP should be documented in source documentation and recorded in the eCRF. Conditions that require administration of IP to be temporarily withheld are described in Section 6.1.2.

trans-unit.source: Procedures for Discontinuation of Investigational Product
trans-unit.target: Procedures for Discontinuation of Investigational Product

trans-unit.source: The Investigator should contact the participant if a decision to stop IP is taken. A participant who decides to discontinue IP will always be asked about the reason(s) and the presence of any AEs. The date of last IP dose, the date of IP discontinuation decision, and the reason(s) for discontinuation should be documented in source documentation and recorded in the eCRF.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator should contact the participant if a decision to stop IP is taken. A participant who decides to discontinue IP will always be asked about the reason(s) and the presence of any AEs. The date of last IP dose, the date of IP discontinuation decision, and the reason(s) for discontinuation should be documented in source documentation and recorded in the eCRF.

trans-unit.source: Discontinuation of IP will be registered in the RTSM.
trans-unit.target: Discontinuation of IP will be registered in the RTSM.

trans-unit.source: All participants discontinued from IP will continue in the study follow-up until the study completion.
trans-unit.target: All participants discontinued from IP will continue in the study follow-up until the study completion.

trans-unit.source: Participants who prematurely discontinue IP should return to the study site and complete the E/D visit procedures 12 weeks after the last dose of IP (Section 1.3), or as soon as feasible after 12 weeks passed from the last dose of IP. In cases when the participant is unable or not willing to return to the site for the E/D visits at 12 weeks after the last IP dose, E/D visit assessments can be performed earlier than this, at a time point that is feasible for the participant. It is strongly recommended that all scheduled study visits and procedures are continued until the E/D Visit, if possible. If the participant is unable to attend a scheduled clinic visit, they should be contacted via phone to collect the following information pertaining to the respective visit:
trans-unit.target: Participants who prematurely discontinue IP should return to the study site and complete the E/D visit procedures 12 weeks after the last dose of IP (Section 1.3), or as soon as feasible after 12 weeks passed from the last dose of IP. In cases when the participant is unable or not willing to return to the site for the E/D visits at 12 weeks after the last IP dose, E/D visit assessments can be performed earlier than this, at a time point that is feasible for the participant. It is strongly recommended that all scheduled study visits and procedures are continued until the E/D Visit, if possible. If the participant is unable to attend a scheduled clinic visit, they should be contacted via phone to collect the following information pertaining to the respective visit:

trans-unit.source: After the E/D visit, the participant will continue in the study follow-up until Week 28 or Week 52 (unless the E/D visit occurs after the scheduled Visit 8 or Visit 14, respectively). To reduce participant burden, Follow-up Visits for participants who have discontinued IP prematurely will be performed remotely every 12 weeks until Week 28 or Week 52, respectively, (±1 week) after the E/D visit to collect death, severe COPD exacerbation, and SAE over the phone. Ongoing AEs from the E/D visit will continue to be followed. Such Follow-up Visits may or may not fall within a scheduled visit window. No other assessments, such as blood test, spirometry, or imaging will be required after the E/D visit for discontinued participants. Follow-up may also include contact with a relative or treating physician and collection of information from medical records.
trans-unit.target: After the E/D visit, the participant will continue in the study follow-up until Week 28 or Week 52 (unless the E/D visit occurs after the scheduled Visit 8 or Visit 14, respectively). To reduce participant burden, Follow-up Visits for participants who have discontinued IP prematurely will be performed remotely every 12 weeks until Week 28 or Week 52, respectively, (±1 week) after the E/D visit to collect death, severe COPD exacerbation, and SAE over the phone. Ongoing AEs from the E/D visit will continue to be followed. Such Follow-up Visits may or may not fall within a scheduled visit window. No other assessments, such as blood test, spirometry, or imaging will be required after the E/D visit for discontinued participants. Follow-up may also include contact with a relative or treating physician and collection of information from medical records.

trans-unit.source: Participants attending the E/D visit at the time or after scheduled Visit 8 or Visit 14 (Week 28 or Week 52) exit the study. At the time of primary reporting, all participants in the treatment period will be asked to continue to the E/D visit 12 weeks after the final dose of IP and exit the study. Participants will not attend scheduled study visits after the last dose of IP until the E/D Visit and there will be no phone follow-up after the E/D visit.
trans-unit.target: Participants attending the E/D visit at the time or after scheduled Visit 8 or Visit 14 (Week 28 or Week 52) exit the study. At the time of primary reporting, all participants in the treatment period will be asked to continue to the E/D visit 12 weeks after the final dose of IP and exit the study. Participants will not attend scheduled study visits after the last dose of IP until the E/D Visit and there will be no phone follow-up after the E/D visit.

trans-unit.source: A participant may withdraw from the study at any time at his/her own request, or may be withdrawn at any time at the discretion of the Investigator for safety, behavioural, compliance, or administrative reasons. This is expected to be uncommon.
trans-unit.target: A participant may withdraw from the study at any time at his/her own request, or may be withdrawn at any time at the discretion of the Investigator for safety, behavioural, compliance, or administrative reasons. This is expected to be uncommon.

trans-unit.source: A participant who considers withdrawing from the study must be informed by the Investigator about modified follow-up options (eg, telephone contact, a contact with a relative or treating physician, or information from medical records).
trans-unit.target: A participant who considers withdrawing from the study must be informed by the Investigator about modified follow-up options (eg, telephone contact, a contact with a relative or treating physician, or information from medical records).

trans-unit.source: At the time of withdrawal from the study, if possible, an E/D visit should be conducted, as described in Section 1.3. Refer to Section 1.3 for data to be collected at the time of study withdrawal and follow-up and for any further evaluations that need to be completed. The participant will discontinue the IP and be withdrawn from the study at that time.
trans-unit.target: At the time of withdrawal from the study, if possible, an E/D visit should be conducted, as described in Section 1.3. Refer to Section 1.3 for data to be collected at the time of study withdrawal and follow-up and for any further evaluations that need to be completed. The participant will discontinue the IP and be withdrawn from the study at that time.

trans-unit.source: If the participant withdraws consent for disclosure of future information, the Sponsor may retain and continue to use any data collected before such a withdrawal of consent.
trans-unit.target: If the participant withdraws consent for disclosure of future information, the Sponsor may retain and continue to use any data collected before such a withdrawal of consent.

trans-unit.source: If a participant withdraws from the study, it should be confirmed if he/she still agrees for existing samples to be used in line with the original consent. If he/she requests withdrawal
trans-unit.target: If a participant withdraws from the study, it should be confirmed if he/she still agrees for existing samples to be used in line with the original consent. If he/she requests withdrawal

trans-unit.source: of consent for use of samples, destruction of any samples taken and not tested should be carried out in line with what was stated in the informed consent and local regulation. The Investigator must document the decision on use of existing samples in the site study records and inform the Global Study Team.
trans-unit.target: of consent for use of samples, destruction of any samples taken and not tested should be carried out in line with what was stated in the informed consent and local regulation. The Investigator must document the decision on use of existing samples in the site study records and inform the Global Study Team.

trans-unit.source: A participant who withdraws consent will always be asked about the reason(s) and the presence of any AEs. The Investigator will follow-up with a participant as medically indicated. The Sponsor or its delegate will request Investigators to collect information on participants’ vital status (dead or alive; date of death when applicable) at the end of the study from publicly available sources, in accordance with local regulations. Knowledge of the vital status at study end in all participants is crucial for the integrity of the study.
trans-unit.target: A participant who withdraws consent will always be asked about the reason(s) and the presence of any AEs. The Investigator will follow-up with a participant as medically indicated. The Sponsor or its delegate will request Investigators to collect information on participants’ vital status (dead or alive; date of death when applicable) at the end of the study from publicly available sources, in accordance with local regulations. Knowledge of the vital status at study end in all participants is crucial for the integrity of the study.

trans-unit.source: It is the Investigator’s responsibility to fully evaluate the participant upon discontinuation or withdrawal to ensure that the participant receives appropriate treatment according to his/her clinical status/condition.
trans-unit.target: It is the Investigator’s responsibility to fully evaluate the participant upon discontinuation or withdrawal to ensure that the participant receives appropriate treatment according to his/her clinical status/condition.

trans-unit.source: Withdrawal of Informed Consent for Donated Biological Samples
trans-unit.target: Withdrawal of Informed Consent for Donated Biological Samples

trans-unit.source: If a participant withdraws consent to the use of donated biological samples, the samples will be disposed of/destroyed, and the action documented. The collection of the biological samples is an integral part of the study where mandatory sampling conform with the clinical safety laboratory test requirements.
trans-unit.target: If a participant withdraws consent to the use of donated biological samples, the samples will be disposed of/destroyed, and the action documented. The collection of the biological samples is an integral part of the study where mandatory sampling conform with the clinical safety laboratory test requirements.

trans-unit.source: A participant who does not allow for further collection or analysis of the mandatory biologic samples critical for clinical safety laboratory tests will need to be discontinued from IP.
trans-unit.target: A participant who does not allow for further collection or analysis of the mandatory biologic samples critical for clinical safety laboratory tests will need to be discontinued from IP.

trans-unit.source: Additional actions to be taken in the event of withdrawal of consent for donated biological samples are described in Appendix C 2.
trans-unit.target: Additional actions to be taken in the event of withdrawal of consent for donated biological samples are described in Appendix C 2.

trans-unit.source: A participant will be considered lost to follow-up if he or she repeatedly fails to return for scheduled visits and is unable to be contacted by the study site.
trans-unit.target: A participant will be considered lost to follow-up if he or she repeatedly fails to return for scheduled visits and is unable to be contacted by the study site.

trans-unit.source: To prevent participants being lost to follow-up, their contact details, including next of kin or other emergency contacts, should be collected initially and updated regularly by the site staff or representative (if allowed by national regulation). The Investigator should educate the participant on the importance of contact with the Investigator throughout the study.
trans-unit.target: To prevent participants being lost to follow-up, their contact details, including next of kin or other emergency contacts, should be collected initially and updated regularly by the site staff or representative (if allowed by national regulation). The Investigator should educate the participant on the importance of contact with the Investigator throughout the study.

trans-unit.source: The following actions must be taken if a participant is identified as a potential lost to follow-up:
trans-unit.target: The following actions must be taken if a participant is identified as a potential lost to follow-up:

trans-unit.source: The site must make an effort to contact the participant to reschedule the missed visit as soon as possible and counsel the participant on the importance of maintaining the assigned visit schedule.
trans-unit.target: The site must make an effort to contact the participant to reschedule the missed visit as soon as possible and counsel the participant on the importance of maintaining the assigned visit schedule.

trans-unit.source: Repeated attempts must be made to contact the participant or next of kin/emergency contact via telephone, fax, email, certified letter, and/or through participant locator agencies (if allowed by national regulation).
trans-unit.target: Repeated attempts must be made to contact the participant or next of kin/emergency contact via telephone, fax, email, certified letter, and/or through participant locator agencies (if allowed by national regulation).

trans-unit.source: Repeated attempts must be made to locate and obtain pertinent medical information for the participant via available hospital records, participant’s primary physician, and a publicly available death registry to obtain a current survival status (if allowed by national regulation).
trans-unit.target: Repeated attempts must be made to locate and obtain pertinent medical information for the participant via available hospital records, participant’s primary physician, and a publicly available death registry to obtain a current survival status (if allowed by national regulation).

trans-unit.source: Where permissible by local law, the ICF will include language to grant the option to employ outside companies to assist in obtaining updated contact information or ascertainment of vital status of lost participants using publicly available sources.
trans-unit.target: Where permissible by local law, the ICF will include language to grant the option to employ outside companies to assist in obtaining updated contact information or ascertainment of vital status of lost participants using publicly available sources.

trans-unit.source: Efforts to reach the participant should continue until the end of the study.
trans-unit.target: Efforts to reach the participant should continue until the end of the study.

trans-unit.source: A participant will be classified as lost to follow-up only if he/she has failed to return for the required study visits and his/her vital status remains unknown at the end of the study despite all above listed efforts.
trans-unit.target: A participant will be classified as lost to follow-up only if he/she has failed to return for the required study visits and his/her vital status remains unknown at the end of the study despite all above listed efforts.

trans-unit.source: Discontinuation of specific sites or of the study as a whole are handled as part of Appendix A.
trans-unit.target: Discontinuation of specific sites or of the study as a whole are handled as part of Appendix A.

trans-unit.source: Study procedures and their timing are summarised in the SoA (Section 1.3). Protocol waivers or exemptions are not allowed.
trans-unit.target: Study procedures and their timing are summarised in the SoA (Section 1.3). Protocol waivers or exemptions are not allowed.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator will ensure that data are recorded on the eCRF. The EDC system will be used for data collection and query handling.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator will ensure that data are recorded on the eCRF. The EDC system will be used for data collection and query handling.

trans-unit.source: Before randomisation and first dosing in PROSPERO, all relevant data including, but not limited to, AEs, SAEs, CM, HRU, and COPD exacerbations will be collected as part of the predecessor study and should be recorded and up to date in the predecessor study.
trans-unit.target: Before randomisation and first dosing in PROSPERO, all relevant data including, but not limited to, AEs, SAEs, CM, HRU, and COPD exacerbations will be collected as part of the predecessor study and should be recorded and up to date in the predecessor study.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator ensures the accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of the data recorded and of the provision of answers to data queries according to the Clinical Study Agreement. The Investigator will sign the completed eCRF. A copy of the completed eCRF will be archived at the study site.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator ensures the accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of the data recorded and of the provision of answers to data queries according to the Clinical Study Agreement. The Investigator will sign the completed eCRF. A copy of the completed eCRF will be archived at the study site.

trans-unit.source: Safety concerns should be discussed with the Sponsor immediately upon occurrence or awareness to determine if the participant should continue or discontinue IP.
trans-unit.target: Safety concerns should be discussed with the Sponsor immediately upon occurrence or awareness to determine if the participant should continue or discontinue IP.
