trans-unit.source: Adherence to the study design requirements, including those specified in the SoA (Section 1.3), is essential and required for study conduct.
trans-unit.target: Adherence to the study design requirements, including those specified in the SoA (Section 1.3), is essential and required for study conduct.

trans-unit.source: All enrolment evaluations must be completed and reviewed to confirm that potential participants meet all eligibility criteria prior to randomisation transaction. The Investigator will maintain a log to record details of all participants that have undergone enrolment procedures and to confirm eligibility or record reasons for enrolment failure, as applicable.
trans-unit.target: All enrolment evaluations must be completed and reviewed to confirm that potential participants meet all eligibility criteria prior to randomisation transaction. The Investigator will maintain a log to record details of all participants that have undergone enrolment procedures and to confirm eligibility or record reasons for enrolment failure, as applicable.

trans-unit.source: Procedures conducted as part of the participant’s routine clinical management (eg, echocardiography, radiological assessments) and obtained before signing of the ICF may be utilised for enrolment purposes provided the procedures met the protocol-specified criteria and were performed within the timeframe defined in the SoA.
trans-unit.target: Procedures conducted as part of the participant’s routine clinical management (eg, echocardiography, radiological assessments) and obtained before signing of the ICF may be utilised for enrolment purposes provided the procedures met the protocol-specified criteria and were performed within the timeframe defined in the SoA.

trans-unit.source: Repeat or unscheduled samples may be taken for safety reasons or for technical issues with the samples.
trans-unit.target: Repeat or unscheduled samples may be taken for safety reasons or for technical issues with the samples.

trans-unit.source: For the purpose of the protocol, a COPD exacerbation will be defined as a worsening in the participant’s usual COPD symptoms that is beyond normal day-to-day variation, is acute in onset, lasts 2 or more days (or less if the worsening is so rapid and profound that the treating physician judges that intensification of treatment cannot be delayed), and may warrant a change in regular medication and leads to any of the following:
trans-unit.target: For the purpose of the protocol, a COPD exacerbation will be defined as a worsening in the participant’s usual COPD symptoms that is beyond normal day-to-day variation, is acute in onset, lasts 2 or more days (or less if the worsening is so rapid and profound that the treating physician judges that intensification of treatment cannot be delayed), and may warrant a change in regular medication and leads to any of the following:

trans-unit.source: Use of systemic corticosteroids for at least 3 days; a single depot injectable (IM) dose of corticosteroids will be considered equivalent to a 3-day course of systemic corticosteroids.
trans-unit.target: Use of systemic corticosteroids for at least 3 days; a single depot injectable (IM) dose of corticosteroids will be considered equivalent to a 3-day course of systemic corticosteroids.

trans-unit.source: ER or ED visit < 24 hours requiring intensive treatment.
trans-unit.target: ER or ED visit < 24 hours requiring intensive treatment.

trans-unit.source: An inpatient hospitalisation due to COPD (defined as an inpatient admission ≥ 24 hours in the hospital, an observation area, the ED, or other equivalent healthcare facility depending on the country and healthcare system).
trans-unit.target: An inpatient hospitalisation due to COPD (defined as an inpatient admission ≥ 24 hours in the hospital, an observation area, the ED, or other equivalent healthcare facility depending on the country and healthcare system).

trans-unit.source: An exacerbation will be considered moderate if it requires treatment with systemic corticosteroids and/or antibiotics or resulted in an ER/ED visit < 24 hours requiring intensive treatment, and does not result in hospitalisation or death. An exacerbation will be considered severe if it results in hospitalisation or death due to COPD.
trans-unit.target: An exacerbation will be considered moderate if it requires treatment with systemic corticosteroids and/or antibiotics or resulted in an ER/ED visit < 24 hours requiring intensive treatment, and does not result in hospitalisation or death. An exacerbation will be considered severe if it results in hospitalisation or death due to COPD.

trans-unit.source: Participants will be encouraged to contact the study site and/or their primary care physician in case of COPD symptom worsening. The participants will be asked to inform the Investigator of any COPD-related events treated outside of the study site.
trans-unit.target: Participants will be encouraged to contact the study site and/or their primary care physician in case of COPD symptom worsening. The participants will be asked to inform the Investigator of any COPD-related events treated outside of the study site.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator should record all pertinent findings and symptoms associated with the exacerbation event (eg, dyspnoea, sputum volume, sputum purulence, cough, wheezing, and other COPD-related symptoms and/or findings) and their duration in source documents and in the eCRF COPD exacerbation module.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator should record all pertinent findings and symptoms associated with the exacerbation event (eg, dyspnoea, sputum volume, sputum purulence, cough, wheezing, and other COPD-related symptoms and/or findings) and their duration in source documents and in the eCRF COPD exacerbation module.

trans-unit.source: A vast majority of COPD exacerbations are associated with worsening of the symptoms as described above. Clinical presentations may, however, vary among participants. If it is not possible to capture the symptoms as described above (eg, participant intubated upon arrival to the ED), the Investigator must document the justification for defining the event as an exacerbation and record it in the source documentation and in the eCRF.
trans-unit.target: A vast majority of COPD exacerbations are associated with worsening of the symptoms as described above. Clinical presentations may, however, vary among participants. If it is not possible to capture the symptoms as described above (eg, participant intubated upon arrival to the ED), the Investigator must document the justification for defining the event as an exacerbation and record it in the source documentation and in the eCRF.

trans-unit.source: Death due to COPD will always be captured as severe COPD exacerbation irrespectively of associated symptom worsening.
trans-unit.target: Death due to COPD will always be captured as severe COPD exacerbation irrespectively of associated symptom worsening.

trans-unit.source: During a COPD exacerbation, it is important for participants to be treated as deemed appropriate by the treating HCP. However, all supplemental medication used to treat the COPD exacerbation should be discontinued as soon as it is considered safe by the Investigator and participants should return to their pre-exacerbation medication regimen as soon as practical.
trans-unit.target: During a COPD exacerbation, it is important for participants to be treated as deemed appropriate by the treating HCP. However, all supplemental medication used to treat the COPD exacerbation should be discontinued as soon as it is considered safe by the Investigator and participants should return to their pre-exacerbation medication regimen as soon as practical.

trans-unit.source: It is recommended that medications to treat a COPD exacerbation are not used for more than 14 days. Recent data suggest that treatment with systemic corticosteroids for shorter periods of time results in similar outcomes with less systemic steroid exposure (Leuppi et al 2013).
trans-unit.target: It is recommended that medications to treat a COPD exacerbation are not used for more than 14 days. Recent data suggest that treatment with systemic corticosteroids for shorter periods of time results in similar outcomes with less systemic steroid exposure (Leuppi et al 2013).

trans-unit.source: The start of an exacerbation is defined as the start date of systemic corticosteroids or antibiotic treatment or hospital admission, whichever occurs earlier, and the end date is defined as the last day of systemic corticosteroids or antibiotic treatment or hospital discharge, whichever occurs later. A single depot injectable dose of corticosteroids will be considered equivalent to a 3-day course of systemic corticosteroids. The corresponding stop date for this treatment will consequently be determined as the date of administration plus 2 days. A minimum 7-day interval is required between the end of treatment (last dose of systemic corticosteroid or antibiotics or the last day of hospitalisation) of one exacerbation to the treatment start of the next exacerbation.
trans-unit.target: The start of an exacerbation is defined as the start date of systemic corticosteroids or antibiotic treatment or hospital admission, whichever occurs earlier, and the end date is defined as the last day of systemic corticosteroids or antibiotic treatment or hospital discharge, whichever occurs later. A single depot injectable dose of corticosteroids will be considered equivalent to a 3-day course of systemic corticosteroids. The corresponding stop date for this treatment will consequently be determined as the date of administration plus 2 days. A minimum 7-day interval is required between the end of treatment (last dose of systemic corticosteroid or antibiotics or the last day of hospitalisation) of one exacerbation to the treatment start of the next exacerbation.

trans-unit.source: The participant may remain in the study after an exacerbation and continue to receive IP if the Investigator judges that it is medically appropriate for the participant to do so.
trans-unit.target: The participant may remain in the study after an exacerbation and continue to receive IP if the Investigator judges that it is medically appropriate for the participant to do so.

trans-unit.source: Study site evaluations for a COPD worsening may occur as a part of an ordinary onsite visit, via telephone contact, or as an unscheduled visit, if deemed necessary by the Investigator. A copy of the medical record should be obtained for exacerbations evaluated and treated at non- study sites (eg, by the primary care HCP or at an ED/hospital) and details entered into the eCRF exacerbation module in a timely fashion. Changes in concomitant medication due to an exacerbation must be recorded in the source documentation and in the appropriate module of the eCRF.
trans-unit.target: Study site evaluations for a COPD worsening may occur as a part of an ordinary onsite visit, via telephone contact, or as an unscheduled visit, if deemed necessary by the Investigator. A copy of the medical record should be obtained for exacerbations evaluated and treated at non- study sites (eg, by the primary care HCP or at an ED/hospital) and details entered into the eCRF exacerbation module in a timely fashion. Changes in concomitant medication due to an exacerbation must be recorded in the source documentation and in the appropriate module of the eCRF.

trans-unit.source: The investigator site is responsible for documenting the contacts (or other actions) following participant contact in the eCRF.
trans-unit.target: The investigator site is responsible for documenting the contacts (or other actions) following participant contact in the eCRF.

trans-unit.source: At study visits, the participant will be asked a single question, “in the past month, how many days a week have you taken your maintenance medication as scheduled?” The response must be recorded in the source documentation and in the appropriate module of the eCRF.
trans-unit.target: At study visits, the participant will be asked a single question, “in the past month, how many days a week have you taken your maintenance medication as scheduled?” The response must be recorded in the source documentation and in the appropriate module of the eCRF.

trans-unit.source: Lung function (FEV1 and FVC) will be measured by spirometry using equipment provided by a central vendor. Spirometry will be performed by the Investigator or authorised delegate according to American Thoracic Society (ATS)/European Respiratory Society (ERS) guidelines (Graham et al 2019).
trans-unit.target: Lung function (FEV1 and FVC) will be measured by spirometry using equipment provided by a central vendor. Spirometry will be performed by the Investigator or authorised delegate according to American Thoracic Society (ATS)/European Respiratory Society (ERS) guidelines (Graham et al 2019).

trans-unit.source: The vendor providing central spirometry is responsible for assuring that the spirometer meets ATS/ERS recommendations and that the site personnel who will be performing the testing are properly certified. Spirometry calibration and data quality checks (including monitoring of unexpectedly high variability of results) will be detailed in a separate spirometry procedures manual and in the monitoring plan. Participants who exhibit unexpectedly high variability of FEV1 will be evaluated by the Investigator for aetiology of variability, necessary follow-up actions, and assessment of continued participant eligibility. The Investigator will discuss unexpectedly high variability with the Sponsor study physician.
trans-unit.target: The vendor providing central spirometry is responsible for assuring that the spirometer meets ATS/ERS recommendations and that the site personnel who will be performing the testing are properly certified. Spirometry calibration and data quality checks (including monitoring of unexpectedly high variability of results) will be detailed in a separate spirometry procedures manual and in the monitoring plan. Participants who exhibit unexpectedly high variability of FEV1 will be evaluated by the Investigator for aetiology of variability, necessary follow-up actions, and assessment of continued participant eligibility. The Investigator will discuss unexpectedly high variability with the Sponsor study physician.

trans-unit.source: Participants should observe the relevant medication withhold periods and other restrictions beforehand (Section 5.3.3, Section 5.3.4, Section 5.3.5, and Section 6.5.3). If any of the restrictions are not met and the session cannot be sufficiently delayed on the day, the onsite visit and dosing should be rescheduled within the allowed visit window. All lung function measurements should take place prior to dosing, if dosing is also scheduled at the same visit.
trans-unit.target: Participants should observe the relevant medication withhold periods and other restrictions beforehand (Section 5.3.3, Section 5.3.4, Section 5.3.5, and Section 6.5.3). If any of the restrictions are not met and the session cannot be sufficiently delayed on the day, the onsite visit and dosing should be rescheduled within the allowed visit window. All lung function measurements should take place prior to dosing, if dosing is also scheduled at the same visit.

trans-unit.source: Usual infection control measures should be followed including use of filters, per the instructions of the central vendor. Any additional infection control measures in accordance with COVID-19 related local and national guidelines should also be followed.
trans-unit.target: Usual infection control measures should be followed including use of filters, per the instructions of the central vendor. Any additional infection control measures in accordance with COVID-19 related local and national guidelines should also be followed.

trans-unit.source: Time of Day for Scheduled Onsite Visit Spirometry
trans-unit.target: Time of Day for Scheduled Onsite Visit Spirometry

trans-unit.source: Spirometry testing should be performed at specified visits according to the SoA (Section 1.3). It is recommended that all post-enrolment spirometry assessments should be performed within
trans-unit.target: Spirometry testing should be performed at specified visits according to the SoA (Section 1.3). It is recommended that all post-enrolment spirometry assessments should be performed within

trans-unit.source: ± 1.5 hours of the time that the enrolment spirometry in the predecessor study was performed, if possible. For example, if the enrolment spirometry in the predecessor study was started at 8:00 AM, then all subsequent spirometry testing in PROSPERO is recommended to be initiated between 6:30 AM and 9:30 AM.
trans-unit.target: ± 1.5 hours of the time that the enrolment spirometry in the predecessor study was performed, if possible. For example, if the enrolment spirometry in the predecessor study was started at 8:00 AM, then all subsequent spirometry testing in PROSPERO is recommended to be initiated between 6:30 AM and 9:30 AM.

trans-unit.source: Detailed procedure for performing spirometry will be described in an instruction manual. Details regarding assessment of the quality of spirometry and the Best Test Review process will also be detailed in the manual and Monitoring Plan.
trans-unit.target: Detailed procedure for performing spirometry will be described in an instruction manual. Details regarding assessment of the quality of spirometry and the Best Test Review process will also be detailed in the manual and Monitoring Plan.

trans-unit.source: The Global Lung Function Initiative equations will be used to determine the predicted normal value (PNV) and are pre-programmed into the spirometer (Quanjer et al 2012).
trans-unit.target: The Global Lung Function Initiative equations will be used to determine the predicted normal value (PNV) and are pre-programmed into the spirometer (Quanjer et al 2012).

trans-unit.source: Forced expiratory volume in one second expressed as percent of the PNV, will be calculated as follows:
trans-unit.target: Forced expiratory volume in one second expressed as percent of the PNV, will be calculated as follows:

trans-unit.source: FEF25-75% will be calculated using a similar method to FEV1.
trans-unit.target: FEF25-75% will be calculated using a similar method to FEV1.

trans-unit.source: Post-BD spirometry will be performed at specified visits as detailed in the SoA (Section 1.3). If a patient cannot tolerate post-BD spirometry, or their condition otherwise contraindicated a post-BD spirometry assessment, it can be skipped.
trans-unit.target: Post-BD spirometry will be performed at specified visits as detailed in the SoA (Section 1.3). If a patient cannot tolerate post-BD spirometry, or their condition otherwise contraindicated a post-BD spirometry assessment, it can be skipped.

trans-unit.source: Endpoint maximal BD will be induced using albuterol (90 μg metered dose) or salbutamol (100 μg metered dose) with or without a spacer device up to a maximum of 4 inhalations, which are recommended to be taken within 30 minutes ± 15 minutes of the final pre-BD spirometry measurement. Post-BD spirometry will be performed 15 to 30 minutes later. If a participant cannot tolerate 4 puffs of albuterol or salbutamol, a lower number of inhalations may be considered at the clinical judgement of the Investigator.
trans-unit.target: Endpoint maximal BD will be induced using albuterol (90 μg metered dose) or salbutamol (100 μg metered dose) with or without a spacer device up to a maximum of 4 inhalations, which are recommended to be taken within 30 minutes ± 15 minutes of the final pre-BD spirometry measurement. Post-BD spirometry will be performed 15 to 30 minutes later. If a participant cannot tolerate 4 puffs of albuterol or salbutamol, a lower number of inhalations may be considered at the clinical judgement of the Investigator.

trans-unit.source: Order of Administration for the COPD Maintenance Inhaled Therapy and IP Relative to the Scheduled Pre- and Post-BD Spirometry
trans-unit.target: Order of Administration for the COPD Maintenance Inhaled Therapy and IP Relative to the Scheduled Pre- and Post-BD Spirometry

trans-unit.source: The participant’s usual COPD morning maintenance inhaled therapy and rescue medicine must not be given until spirometry assessments are complete. Dosing of IP should also be performed after spirometry is complete.
trans-unit.target: The participant’s usual COPD morning maintenance inhaled therapy and rescue medicine must not be given until spirometry assessments are complete. Dosing of IP should also be performed after spirometry is complete.

trans-unit.source: If the participant cannot tolerate a spirometry assessment during the visit, it can be rescheduled for a different date, preferably within the visit window. There is no need to withhold IP in this case; the IP can be administered during the visit as scheduled.
trans-unit.target: If the participant cannot tolerate a spirometry assessment during the visit, it can be rescheduled for a different date, preferably within the visit window. There is no need to withhold IP in this case; the IP can be administered during the visit as scheduled.

trans-unit.source: A signed and dated copy of the pre- and post-BD printout must be kept at study centre for source data verification. The printout must be marked with the study code, enrolment code, date and time of measurement, and visit number. If a printout cannot be printed, the mean value of the measurements will be recorded in the participant’s charts.
trans-unit.target: A signed and dated copy of the pre- and post-BD printout must be kept at study centre for source data verification. The printout must be marked with the study code, enrolment code, date and time of measurement, and visit number. If a printout cannot be printed, the mean value of the measurements will be recorded in the participant’s charts.

trans-unit.source: If feasible, a low radiation dose inspiratory and expiratory CT scans should be performed according to a standardised protocol at the end of treatment period as detailed in the SoA (Section 1.3), for participants with a qualified baseline CT in the predecessor study (as confirmed by the imaging vendor).
trans-unit.target: If feasible, a low radiation dose inspiratory and expiratory CT scans should be performed according to a standardised protocol at the end of treatment period as detailed in the SoA (Section 1.3), for participants with a qualified baseline CT in the predecessor study (as confirmed by the imaging vendor).

trans-unit.source: For participants who discontinue IP early, it is highly recommended that a CT is performed at the E/D visit instead of at the end of treatment period. However, it should not be performed if less than 24 weeks since PROSPERO randomisation.
trans-unit.target: For participants who discontinue IP early, it is highly recommended that a CT is performed at the E/D visit instead of at the end of treatment period. However, it should not be performed if less than 24 weeks since PROSPERO randomisation.

trans-unit.source: The analysis of the CT images performed by the imaging vendor will enable quantification of mucous plugging, emphysema, and airway wall thickening. The follow-up scan performed in this study will be used to determine the long-term effect of treatment on change from baseline in the CT parameters.
trans-unit.target: The analysis of the CT images performed by the imaging vendor will enable quantification of mucous plugging, emphysema, and airway wall thickening. The follow-up scan performed in this study will be used to determine the long-term effect of treatment on change from baseline in the CT parameters.

trans-unit.source: Ideally, new CT scans should be performed in a standardised way that will facilitate a more detailed assessment by the imaging vendor of:
trans-unit.target: Ideally, new CT scans should be performed in a standardised way that will facilitate a more detailed assessment by the imaging vendor of:

trans-unit.source: Other quantitative assessments of airway morphometry and lung density
trans-unit.target: Other quantitative assessments of airway morphometry and lung density

trans-unit.source: A radiology manual, as well as further training by the imaging vendor, will describe the process of obtaining the CT scans and how to submit these to the imaging vendor. An optimised CT protocol will also be provided to the sites that includes all required scan parameters (same as in the predecessor study).
trans-unit.target: A radiology manual, as well as further training by the imaging vendor, will describe the process of obtaining the CT scans and how to submit these to the imaging vendor. An optimised CT protocol will also be provided to the sites that includes all required scan parameters (same as in the predecessor study).

trans-unit.source: All CT scans should, if possible be submitted to the central imaging vendor for central review.
trans-unit.target: All CT scans should, if possible be submitted to the central imaging vendor for central review.

trans-unit.source: Planned time points for all safety assessments are provided in the SoA (Section 1.3).
trans-unit.target: Planned time points for all safety assessments are provided in the SoA (Section 1.3).

trans-unit.source: Physical Examinations, Weight, and Supplemental Oxygen Status
trans-unit.target: Physical Examinations, Weight, and Supplemental Oxygen Status

trans-unit.source: Complete and abbreviated physical examinations will be performed by a licensed HCP (eg, physician, physician’s assistant, or licensed nurse practitioner). A complete physical
trans-unit.target: Complete and abbreviated physical examinations will be performed by a licensed HCP (eg, physician, physician’s assistant, or licensed nurse practitioner). A complete physical

trans-unit.source: examination will include, but will not be limited to, assessment of general appearance, head, ears, eyes, nose, throat, neck, skin, as well as cardiovascular, respiratory, abdominal, and nervous systems.
trans-unit.target: examination will include, but will not be limited to, assessment of general appearance, head, ears, eyes, nose, throat, neck, skin, as well as cardiovascular, respiratory, abdominal, and nervous systems.

trans-unit.source: An abbreviated physical examination will include, at a minimum, assessments of cardiovascular, respiratory, skin and nervous systems.
trans-unit.target: An abbreviated physical examination will include, at a minimum, assessments of cardiovascular, respiratory, skin and nervous systems.

trans-unit.source: Before randomisation and first dosing in PROSPERO, each clinically significant abnormal finding from physical examinations will be recorded as an AE in the predecessor study, and each treatment-emergent abnormality, including injection site reactions identified by assessing SC injection sites, will be recorded as an AE.
trans-unit.target: Before randomisation and first dosing in PROSPERO, each clinically significant abnormal finding from physical examinations will be recorded as an AE in the predecessor study, and each treatment-emergent abnormality, including injection site reactions identified by assessing SC injection sites, will be recorded as an AE.

trans-unit.source: Physical examinations, weight, and supplemental O2 status will be recorded at timepoints specified in the SoA (Section 1.3).
trans-unit.target: Physical examinations, weight, and supplemental O2 status will be recorded at timepoints specified in the SoA (Section 1.3).

trans-unit.source: Smoking status will be assessed at visit by collecting the participant’s response to a single yes/no question from study personnel: ‘What is your smoking status as of today, do you currently smoke?’.
trans-unit.target: Smoking status will be assessed at visit by collecting the participant’s response to a single yes/no question from study personnel: ‘What is your smoking status as of today, do you currently smoke?’.

trans-unit.source: Use of e-cigarettes or vaping of any products (eg, nicotine, THC) is discouraged during the study. Use of heated tobacco products (eg, IQOS) is discouraged in the former smoker cohort; it should be captured in source documentation and eCRF.
trans-unit.target: Use of e-cigarettes or vaping of any products (eg, nicotine, THC) is discouraged during the study. Use of heated tobacco products (eg, IQOS) is discouraged in the former smoker cohort; it should be captured in source documentation and eCRF.

trans-unit.source: Vital signs will be assessed pre-dose as described in the SoA (Section 1.3). It is recommended that vital signs are assessed before any interventional study procedures (blood test collection, spirometry, IP administration), and prior to administration of COPD maintenance inhaled
trans-unit.target: Vital signs will be assessed pre-dose as described in the SoA (Section 1.3). It is recommended that vital signs are assessed before any interventional study procedures (blood test collection, spirometry, IP administration), and prior to administration of COPD maintenance inhaled

trans-unit.source: therapy, if possible. The participant should be in a resting position for at least 5 minutes prior to the collection of vital signs, as follows:
trans-unit.target: therapy, if possible. The participant should be in a resting position for at least 5 minutes prior to the collection of vital signs, as follows:

trans-unit.source: Body temperature (measured in Celsius). Oral measurement is preferred, but other methods are acceptable as per local standards of care.
trans-unit.target: Body temperature (measured in Celsius). Oral measurement is preferred, but other methods are acceptable as per local standards of care.

trans-unit.source: Oxygen saturation (SpO2) measured by pulse oximetry
trans-unit.target: Oxygen saturation (SpO2) measured by pulse oximetry

trans-unit.source: Single 12-lead ECGs will be performed at timelines as specified in the SoA (Section 1.3). The ECG assessment is recommended to be performed before other interventions (eg, blood sampling, spirometry, and administration of the COPD maintenance medications), but must be before administration of IP. The participant should be in a resting supine position for at least 10 minutes prior to the collection of ECGs.
trans-unit.target: Single 12-lead ECGs will be performed at timelines as specified in the SoA (Section 1.3). The ECG assessment is recommended to be performed before other interventions (eg, blood sampling, spirometry, and administration of the COPD maintenance medications), but must be before administration of IP. The participant should be in a resting supine position for at least 10 minutes prior to the collection of ECGs.

trans-unit.source: Electrocardiograms will be recorded using an ECG machine provided by a central vendor that automatically calculates the heart rate and measures PR, QRS, and QT intervals; only this equipment should be used for assessment throughout the participant’s participation in the study. Electrocardiogram device software will be used to assess ECG parameters. The ECG results will be transmitted to a central reader. The Investigator or authorised delegate will be responsible for the overall interpretation and determination of clinical significance of any potential ECG findings. Any findings will be recorded in the source documentation and eCRF.
trans-unit.target: Electrocardiograms will be recorded using an ECG machine provided by a central vendor that automatically calculates the heart rate and measures PR, QRS, and QT intervals; only this equipment should be used for assessment throughout the participant’s participation in the study. Electrocardiogram device software will be used to assess ECG parameters. The ECG results will be transmitted to a central reader. The Investigator or authorised delegate will be responsible for the overall interpretation and determination of clinical significance of any potential ECG findings. Any findings will be recorded in the source documentation and eCRF.

trans-unit.source: Electrocardiogram variables will be collected, as follows:
trans-unit.target: Electrocardiogram variables will be collected, as follows:

trans-unit.source: All participants who were eligible for echocardiography at baseline, and/or received echocardiography performed in a predecessor study or during this study will have a follow-up echocardiography at the end of the treatment period or E/D visit (see Section 1.3).
trans-unit.target: All participants who were eligible for echocardiography at baseline, and/or received echocardiography performed in a predecessor study or during this study will have a follow-up echocardiography at the end of the treatment period or E/D visit (see Section 1.3).

trans-unit.source: During the study, additional echocardiography assessments must be performed as soon as practical if new onset or worsening of heart failure is suspected, and may also be performed for other reasons as clinically indicated.
trans-unit.target: During the study, additional echocardiography assessments must be performed as soon as practical if new onset or worsening of heart failure is suspected, and may also be performed for other reasons as clinically indicated.

trans-unit.source: Echocardiography will be performed locally using local equipment. It is strongly recommended that any repeated assessments throughout the study for a given participant are performed on the same equipment by the same echocardiography specialist. LVEF and left ventricular volume will be assessed, and local echocardiography results should include clinical interpretation by the qualified specialist.
trans-unit.target: Echocardiography will be performed locally using local equipment. It is strongly recommended that any repeated assessments throughout the study for a given participant are performed on the same equipment by the same echocardiography specialist. LVEF and left ventricular volume will be assessed, and local echocardiography results should include clinical interpretation by the qualified specialist.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator/delegate will be responsible for the overall interpretation and determination of clinical significance of any potential echocardiography findings and their recording in source notes and eCRF.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator/delegate will be responsible for the overall interpretation and determination of clinical significance of any potential echocardiography findings and their recording in source notes and eCRF.

trans-unit.source: The nominal timing of blood draws is described in the SoA (Section 1.3). A Laboratory Manual that specifies the procedures for collection, processing, storage, and shipment of samples, as well as laboratory contact information, specific to this clinical research study will be provided to the sites. Clinical laboratory safety tests will be performed in a central clinical laboratory. Urine pregnancy tests, if applicable, will be performed at the site using a licensed test (dipstick). In case of a telemedicine visit, a urine pregnancy test will be performed elsewhere, as appropriate.
trans-unit.target: The nominal timing of blood draws is described in the SoA (Section 1.3). A Laboratory Manual that specifies the procedures for collection, processing, storage, and shipment of samples, as well as laboratory contact information, specific to this clinical research study will be provided to the sites. Clinical laboratory safety tests will be performed in a central clinical laboratory. Urine pregnancy tests, if applicable, will be performed at the site using a licensed test (dipstick). In case of a telemedicine visit, a urine pregnancy test will be performed elsewhere, as appropriate.

trans-unit.source: Clinically significant abnormal laboratory results should be repeated as soon as possible (preferably within 24 to 48 hours). NT-proBNP should be additionally performed when new onset heart failure is suspected, or as soon as possible thereafter. If clinically indicated, other laboratory tests, including troponin, may be performed locally per local SoC.
trans-unit.target: Clinically significant abnormal laboratory results should be repeated as soon as possible (preferably within 24 to 48 hours). NT-proBNP should be additionally performed when new onset heart failure is suspected, or as soon as possible thereafter. If clinically indicated, other laboratory tests, including troponin, may be performed locally per local SoC.

trans-unit.source: The following laboratory variables will be measured (Table 6). For haematology, refer to blinding procedures for eosinophil data in Section 6.3.2.
trans-unit.target: The following laboratory variables will be measured (Table 6). For haematology, refer to blinding procedures for eosinophil data in Section 6.3.2.

trans-unit.source: Microscopy including white blood cell/RBC and casts
trans-unit.target: Microscopy including white blood cell/RBC and casts

trans-unit.source: Tryptase (only to be taken in event of suspected anaphylaxis; Appendix G)
trans-unit.target: Tryptase (only to be taken in event of suspected anaphylaxis; Appendix G)

trans-unit.source: Note: For serum chemistry, tests for AST, ALT, ALP, and S-bilirubin must be conducted concurrently and assessed concurrently. In case a participant shows an AST or ALT ≥ 3× ULN together with TBL ≥ 2× ULN refer to Appendix D ‘Actions required in cases of increases in liver biochemistry and evaluation of Hy’s Law (HL)’, for further instructions.
trans-unit.target: Note: For serum chemistry, tests for AST, ALT, ALP, and S-bilirubin must be conducted concurrently and assessed concurrently. In case a participant shows an AST or ALT ≥ 3× ULN together with TBL ≥ 2× ULN refer to Appendix D ‘Actions required in cases of increases in liver biochemistry and evaluation of Hy’s Law (HL)’, for further instructions.

trans-unit.source: a Eosinophil, basophil, and monocyte counts to be redacted.
trans-unit.target: a Eosinophil, basophil, and monocyte counts to be redacted.

trans-unit.source: b If needed to confirm post-menopausal status in female participants aged < 50 years.
trans-unit.target: b If needed to confirm post-menopausal status in female participants aged < 50 years.

trans-unit.source: c Serum HCG test will be performed at Visit 1 for FOCBP. If a urine stick test is positive, then a serum HCG test is required for confirmation.
trans-unit.target: c Serum HCG test will be performed at Visit 1 for FOCBP. If a urine stick test is positive, then a serum HCG test is required for confirmation.

trans-unit.source: ALP, alkaline phosphatase; ALT, alanine aminotransferase; AST, aspartate aminotransferase; CRP, C-reactive protein; FOCBP, female(s) of child-bearing potential; FSH, follicle-stimulating hormone; GGT, gamma-glutamyl transferase; Hb, haemoglobin; HbA1c, haemoglobin A1c; HCG, human chorionic gonadotropin;
trans-unit.target: ALP, alkaline phosphatase; ALT, alanine aminotransferase; AST, aspartate aminotransferase; CRP, C-reactive protein; FOCBP, female(s) of child-bearing potential; FSH, follicle-stimulating hormone; GGT, gamma-glutamyl transferase; Hb, haemoglobin; HbA1c, haemoglobin A1c; HCG, human chorionic gonadotropin;

trans-unit.source: NT-ProBNP, N-terminal pro-brain natriuretic peptide; RBC, red blood cell(s); TBL, total bilirubin; ULN, upper limit of normal.
trans-unit.target: NT-ProBNP, N-terminal pro-brain natriuretic peptide; RBC, red blood cell(s); TBL, total bilirubin; ULN, upper limit of normal.

trans-unit.source: The following tests are applicable only to FOCBP participants and will be conducted as per the schedule provided in the SoA (Section 1.3):
trans-unit.target: The following tests are applicable only to FOCBP participants and will be conducted as per the schedule provided in the SoA (Section 1.3):

trans-unit.source: FSH: The test will be performed, for female participants, to confirm postmenopausal status in females < 50 years of age who have been amenorrhoeic for ≥ 12 months.
trans-unit.target: FSH: The test will be performed, for female participants, to confirm postmenopausal status in females < 50 years of age who have been amenorrhoeic for ≥ 12 months.

trans-unit.source: Serum beta-HCG: The test will be performed, for FOCBP (analysed at the central laboratory).
trans-unit.target: Serum beta-HCG: The test will be performed, for FOCBP (analysed at the central laboratory).

trans-unit.source: Urine HCG: The dipstick test will be performed for FOCBP. At each treatment visit, the test will be performed before IP administration. In case of a positive dipstick test, IP must be withheld and a confirmatory serum beta-HCG test must be performed.
trans-unit.target: Urine HCG: The dipstick test will be performed for FOCBP. At each treatment visit, the test will be performed before IP administration. In case of a positive dipstick test, IP must be withheld and a confirmatory serum beta-HCG test must be performed.

trans-unit.source: The following tests may be conducted locally if clinically indicated and as per local SoC:
trans-unit.target: The following tests may be conducted locally if clinically indicated and as per local SoC:

trans-unit.source: Serology: Hepatitis B (HBsAg and anti-HBc) and hepatitis C antibodies
trans-unit.target: Serology: Hepatitis B (HBsAg and anti-HBc) and hepatitis C antibodies

trans-unit.source: p24 antigen, HIV-1 and HIV-2 antibodies
trans-unit.target: p24 antigen, HIV-1 and HIV-2 antibodies

trans-unit.source: TB test: If applicable, TB test will be performed according to the local SoC as determined by local guidelines.
trans-unit.target: TB test: If applicable, TB test will be performed according to the local SoC as determined by local guidelines.

trans-unit.source: Adverse Events and Serious Adverse Events
trans-unit.target: Adverse Events and Serious Adverse Events

trans-unit.source: The Investigator is responsible for ensuring that all staff involved in the study are familiar with the content of this section.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator is responsible for ensuring that all staff involved in the study are familiar with the content of this section.

trans-unit.source: The definitions of an AE or SAE can be found in Appendix B.
trans-unit.target: The definitions of an AE or SAE can be found in Appendix B.

trans-unit.source: Adverse events will be reported by the participant (or, when appropriate, by a caregiver, surrogate, or the participant’s legally authorised representative).
trans-unit.target: Adverse events will be reported by the participant (or, when appropriate, by a caregiver, surrogate, or the participant’s legally authorised representative).

trans-unit.source: The Investigator and any designees are responsible for detecting, documenting, and recording events that meet the definition of an AE.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator and any designees are responsible for detecting, documenting, and recording events that meet the definition of an AE.

trans-unit.source: Time Period and Frequency for Collecting AE and SAE Information
trans-unit.target: Time Period and Frequency for Collecting AE and SAE Information

trans-unit.source: Adverse events will be collected from randomisation (V1) throughout the treatment period and including the follow-up period.
trans-unit.target: Adverse events will be collected from randomisation (V1) throughout the treatment period and including the follow-up period.

trans-unit.source: SAEs will be collected from signing the ICF throughout the duration of the study in the predecessor and/or PROSPERO study eCRF.
trans-unit.target: SAEs will be collected from signing the ICF throughout the duration of the study in the predecessor and/or PROSPERO study eCRF.

trans-unit.source: Before randomisation and first dosing in PROSPERO, AEs and SAEs will be collected as part of the predecessor study and should be recorded and up to date in the predecessor study eCRF.
trans-unit.target: Before randomisation and first dosing in PROSPERO, AEs and SAEs will be collected as part of the predecessor study and should be recorded and up to date in the predecessor study eCRF.

trans-unit.source: In case of AEs and SAEs not resolved in predecessor studies at time of randomisation to PROSPERO, they will be also recorded in the PROSPERO study eCRF.
trans-unit.target: In case of AEs and SAEs not resolved in predecessor studies at time of randomisation to PROSPERO, they will be also recorded in the PROSPERO study eCRF.

trans-unit.source: If the Investigator becomes aware of an SAE with a suspected causal relationship to the IP that occurs after the end of the clinical study in a participant treated by him or her, the Investigator shall, without undue delay, report the SAE to the Sponsor.
trans-unit.target: If the Investigator becomes aware of an SAE with a suspected causal relationship to the IP that occurs after the end of the clinical study in a participant treated by him or her, the Investigator shall, without undue delay, report the SAE to the Sponsor.

trans-unit.source: Any AEs that are unresolved at the participant’s last AE assessment in the study are followed up by the Investigator for as long as medically indicated, but without further recording in the eCRF. The Sponsor retains the right to request additional information for any participant with ongoing AE(s)/SAE(s) at the end of the study, if judged necessary.
trans-unit.target: Any AEs that are unresolved at the participant’s last AE assessment in the study are followed up by the Investigator for as long as medically indicated, but without further recording in the eCRF. The Sponsor retains the right to request additional information for any participant with ongoing AE(s)/SAE(s) at the end of the study, if judged necessary.

trans-unit.source: The following variables will be collected for each AE:
trans-unit.target: The following variables will be collected for each AE:

trans-unit.source: Adverse event (verbatim). Note: for COVID-related events, the Investigator should add ‘COVID’ as part of the verbatim term.
trans-unit.target: Adverse event (verbatim). Note: for COVID-related events, the Investigator should add ‘COVID’ as part of the verbatim term.

trans-unit.source: The date and time when the AE started and stopped
trans-unit.target: The date and time when the AE started and stopped

trans-unit.source: Whether the AE is serious or not
trans-unit.target: Whether the AE is serious or not

trans-unit.source: Investigator causality rating against the IP(s) (yes or no)
trans-unit.target: Investigator causality rating against the IP(s) (yes or no)

trans-unit.source: Action taken with regard to IP(s)
trans-unit.target: Action taken with regard to IP(s)

trans-unit.source: Adverse event caused participant’s withdrawal from study (yes or no)
trans-unit.target: Adverse event caused participant’s withdrawal from study (yes or no)

trans-unit.source: In addition, the following variables will be collected for SAEs:
trans-unit.target: In addition, the following variables will be collected for SAEs:

trans-unit.source: Date AE met criteria for SAE
trans-unit.target: Date AE met criteria for SAE

trans-unit.source: Date Investigator became aware of SAE
trans-unit.target: Date Investigator became aware of SAE

trans-unit.source: Adverse event is serious due to
trans-unit.target: Adverse event is serious due to

trans-unit.source: Causality assessment in relation to study procedure(s)
trans-unit.target: Causality assessment in relation to study procedure(s)

trans-unit.source: The Investigator should assess causal relationship between IP and each AE, and answer ‘yes’ or ‘no’ to the question ‘Do you consider that there is a reasonable possibility that the event may have been caused by the IP?’
trans-unit.target: The Investigator should assess causal relationship between IP and each AE, and answer ‘yes’ or ‘no’ to the question ‘Do you consider that there is a reasonable possibility that the event may have been caused by the IP?’

trans-unit.source: For SAEs, causal relationship should also be assessed for other medication and study procedures. Note that for SAEs that could be associated with any study procedure the causal relationship is implied as ‘yes’.
trans-unit.target: For SAEs, causal relationship should also be assessed for other medication and study procedures. Note that for SAEs that could be associated with any study procedure the causal relationship is implied as ‘yes’.

trans-unit.source: A guide to the interpretation of the causality question is found in Appendix B to the CSP.
trans-unit.target: A guide to the interpretation of the causality question is found in Appendix B to the CSP.

trans-unit.source: Adverse Events Based on Signs and Symptoms
trans-unit.target: Adverse Events Based on Signs and Symptoms

trans-unit.source: All AEs spontaneously reported by the participant or reported in response to the open question from the study site staff: ‘Have you had any health problems since the previous visit/you were last asked?’ or revealed by observation will be collected and recorded in the source documentation and eCRF. When collecting AEs, the recording of diagnoses is preferred (when possible) to recording a list of signs and symptoms. However, if a diagnosis is known and there are other signs or symptoms that are not generally part of the diagnosis, the diagnosis and each sign or symptom will be recorded separately.
trans-unit.target: All AEs spontaneously reported by the participant or reported in response to the open question from the study site staff: ‘Have you had any health problems since the previous visit/you were last asked?’ or revealed by observation will be collected and recorded in the source documentation and eCRF. When collecting AEs, the recording of diagnoses is preferred (when possible) to recording a list of signs and symptoms. However, if a diagnosis is known and there are other signs or symptoms that are not generally part of the diagnosis, the diagnosis and each sign or symptom will be recorded separately.

trans-unit.source: Adverse Events Based on Examinations and Tests
trans-unit.target: Adverse Events Based on Examinations and Tests

trans-unit.source: The results from the CSP-mandated laboratory tests and vital signs will be summarised in the CSR.
trans-unit.target: The results from the CSP-mandated laboratory tests and vital signs will be summarised in the CSR.

trans-unit.source: Deterioration as compared to baseline in protocol-mandated laboratory values, vital signs, or spirometry values should therefore only be reported as AEs if they fulfil any of the SAE criteria, are the reason for discontinuation of treatment with the IP, or are considered to be clinically relevant as judged by the Investigator (which may include, but is not limited to, consideration as to whether treatment or non-planned visits were required or other action was taken with the IP [eg, IP interruption]).
trans-unit.target: Deterioration as compared to baseline in protocol-mandated laboratory values, vital signs, or spirometry values should therefore only be reported as AEs if they fulfil any of the SAE criteria, are the reason for discontinuation of treatment with the IP, or are considered to be clinically relevant as judged by the Investigator (which may include, but is not limited to, consideration as to whether treatment or non-planned visits were required or other action was taken with the IP [eg, IP interruption]).

trans-unit.source: If deterioration in a laboratory value/vital sign is associated with clinical signs and symptoms, the sign or symptom will be reported as an AE and the associated laboratory result/vital sign will be considered as additional information. Wherever possible the reporting Investigator uses the clinical, rather than the laboratory term (eg, anaemia versus low haemoglobin value). In the absence of clinical signs or symptoms, clinically relevant deteriorations in non- mandated parameters should be reported as AE(s).
trans-unit.target: If deterioration in a laboratory value/vital sign is associated with clinical signs and symptoms, the sign or symptom will be reported as an AE and the associated laboratory result/vital sign will be considered as additional information. Wherever possible the reporting Investigator uses the clinical, rather than the laboratory term (eg, anaemia versus low haemoglobin value). In the absence of clinical signs or symptoms, clinically relevant deteriorations in non- mandated parameters should be reported as AE(s).

trans-unit.source: Deterioration of a laboratory value, which is unequivocally due to disease progression, should not be reported as an AE/SAE.
trans-unit.target: Deterioration of a laboratory value, which is unequivocally due to disease progression, should not be reported as an AE/SAE.

trans-unit.source: Any new or aggravated clinically relevant abnormal medical finding at a physical examination as compared with the baseline assessment will be reported as an AE unless unequivocally related to the disease under study.
trans-unit.target: Any new or aggravated clinically relevant abnormal medical finding at a physical examination as compared with the baseline assessment will be reported as an AE unless unequivocally related to the disease under study.

trans-unit.source: Adverse events of special interest are events of scientific and medical interest specific to understanding of tozorakimab and may require close monitoring and rapid communication by the Investigator to the Sponsor. An AESI may be serious or non-serious.
trans-unit.target: Adverse events of special interest are events of scientific and medical interest specific to understanding of tozorakimab and may require close monitoring and rapid communication by the Investigator to the Sponsor. An AESI may be serious or non-serious.

trans-unit.source: The following AESIs will be particularly monitored in this study:
trans-unit.target: The following AESIs will be particularly monitored in this study:

trans-unit.source: Serious hypersensitivity (including Type 1 to 4 hypersensitivity reactions)
trans-unit.target: Serious hypersensitivity (including Type 1 to 4 hypersensitivity reactions)

trans-unit.source: New onset or progression of heart failure.
trans-unit.target: New onset or progression of heart failure.

trans-unit.source: Serious infections: Any event that is defined by the seriousness criteria and falls into the Infections and Infestations System Organ Class.
trans-unit.target: Serious infections: Any event that is defined by the seriousness criteria and falls into the Infections and Infestations System Organ Class.

trans-unit.source: Opportunistic infections: Any event, both serious or non-serious, caused by microorganisms that are normally non-pathogenic or rarely pathogenic in individuals with normal immune function or cause an infection of a type or severity not seen in the normal host. Examples of common opportunistic infections include, but are not limited to: Cryptococcosis, cytomegalovirus infection, Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia, and tuberculosis.
trans-unit.target: Opportunistic infections: Any event, both serious or non-serious, caused by microorganisms that are normally non-pathogenic or rarely pathogenic in individuals with normal immune function or cause an infection of a type or severity not seen in the normal host. Examples of common opportunistic infections include, but are not limited to: Cryptococcosis, cytomegalovirus infection, Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia, and tuberculosis.

trans-unit.source: Cases where a participant shows elevations in liver biochemistry may require further evaluation and occurrences of AST or ALT ≥ 3× ULN together with TBL ≥ 2× ULN may need to be reported as SAEs. Please refer to Appendix D for further instruction on cases of increases in liver biochemistry and evaluation of HL.
trans-unit.target: Cases where a participant shows elevations in liver biochemistry may require further evaluation and occurrences of AST or ALT ≥ 3× ULN together with TBL ≥ 2× ULN may need to be reported as SAEs. Please refer to Appendix D for further instruction on cases of increases in liver biochemistry and evaluation of HL.

trans-unit.source: Disease progression can be considered as worsening of participant’s condition attributable to the disease for which the IP is being studied (increase in the COPD symptoms and/or increase in the severity of the disease). Symptoms of disease under study are those which might be expected to occur as a direct result of COPD (eg, dyspnoea, mucus production, cough etc.).
trans-unit.target: Disease progression can be considered as worsening of participant’s condition attributable to the disease for which the IP is being studied (increase in the COPD symptoms and/or increase in the severity of the disease). Symptoms of disease under study are those which might be expected to occur as a direct result of COPD (eg, dyspnoea, mucus production, cough etc.).

trans-unit.source: Events which are unequivocally due to disease under study and/or its progression should not be reported as an AE during the study unless they meet SAE criteria or lead to discontinuation of the IP.
trans-unit.target: Events which are unequivocally due to disease under study and/or its progression should not be reported as an AE during the study unless they meet SAE criteria or lead to discontinuation of the IP.

trans-unit.source: Worsening of COPD that meets the protocol definition for COPD exacerbation (Section 8.1.1) will be captured in the source notes and on a specific eCRF module. A COPD exacerbation
trans-unit.target: Worsening of COPD that meets the protocol definition for COPD exacerbation (Section 8.1.1) will be captured in the source notes and on a specific eCRF module. A COPD exacerbation

trans-unit.source: should not be reported as an AE unless it meets the definition of an SAE or leads to discontinuation of the IP.
trans-unit.target: should not be reported as an AE unless it meets the definition of an SAE or leads to discontinuation of the IP.

trans-unit.source: All SAEs must be reported, whether or not considered causally related to the IP, or to the study procedure(s). All SAEs will be recorded in source documentation and eCRF.
trans-unit.target: All SAEs must be reported, whether or not considered causally related to the IP, or to the study procedure(s). All SAEs will be recorded in source documentation and eCRF.

trans-unit.source: If any SAE occurs during the course of the study, Investigators or other site personnel will inform the appropriate Sponsor representatives within one day, ie, immediately but no later than 24 hours of when he or she becomes aware of it.
trans-unit.target: If any SAE occurs during the course of the study, Investigators or other site personnel will inform the appropriate Sponsor representatives within one day, ie, immediately but no later than 24 hours of when he or she becomes aware of it.

trans-unit.source: The designated Sponsor representative will work with the Investigator to ensure that all the necessary information is provided to the Sponsor Patient Safety data entry site within
trans-unit.target: The designated Sponsor representative will work with the Investigator to ensure that all the necessary information is provided to the Sponsor Patient Safety data entry site within

trans-unit.source: one calendar day of initial receipt for fatal and life-threatening events and within 5 calendar days of initial receipt for all other SAEs.
trans-unit.target: one calendar day of initial receipt for fatal and life-threatening events and within 5 calendar days of initial receipt for all other SAEs.

trans-unit.source: For fatal or life-threatening AEs where important or relevant information is missing, active follow-up will be undertaken immediately. Investigators or other site personnel will inform the Sponsor representatives of any follow-up information on a previously reported SAE within one calendar day, ie, immediately but no later than 24 hours of when he or she becomes aware of it.
trans-unit.target: For fatal or life-threatening AEs where important or relevant information is missing, active follow-up will be undertaken immediately. Investigators or other site personnel will inform the Sponsor representatives of any follow-up information on a previously reported SAE within one calendar day, ie, immediately but no later than 24 hours of when he or she becomes aware of it.

trans-unit.source: Once the Investigators or other site personnel indicate an AE is serious in the EDC system, an automated email alert is sent to the designated Sponsor representative.
trans-unit.target: Once the Investigators or other site personnel indicate an AE is serious in the EDC system, an automated email alert is sent to the designated Sponsor representative.

trans-unit.source: If the EDC system is not available, then the Investigator or other study site staff reports an SAE to the appropriate Sponsor representative by telephone.
trans-unit.target: If the EDC system is not available, then the Investigator or other study site staff reports an SAE to the appropriate Sponsor representative by telephone.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor representative will advise the Investigator/study site staff how to proceed.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor representative will advise the Investigator/study site staff how to proceed.

trans-unit.source: The reference document for definition of expectedness/listedness is the IB for the Sponsor drug.
trans-unit.target: The reference document for definition of expectedness/listedness is the IB for the Sponsor drug.

trans-unit.source: All pregnancies and outcomes of pregnancy should be reported to the Sponsor, except for if the pregnancy is discovered before the study participant has received any IP.
trans-unit.target: All pregnancies and outcomes of pregnancy should be reported to the Sponsor, except for if the pregnancy is discovered before the study participant has received any IP.

trans-unit.source: If a participant becomes pregnant during the course of the study, IP should be discontinued immediately.
trans-unit.target: If a participant becomes pregnant during the course of the study, IP should be discontinued immediately.

trans-unit.source: Pregnancy itself is not regarded as an AE unless there is a suspicion that the IP under study may have interfered with the effectiveness of a contraceptive medication. Congenital anomalies/birth defects and spontaneous miscarriages should be reported and handled as SAEs. Elective abortions without complications should not be handled as AEs. The outcome of all pregnancies (spontaneous miscarriage, elective termination, ectopic pregnancy, normal birth, or congenital anomaly/birth defect) should be followed up and documented even if the participant was discontinued from the study.
trans-unit.target: Pregnancy itself is not regarded as an AE unless there is a suspicion that the IP under study may have interfered with the effectiveness of a contraceptive medication. Congenital anomalies/birth defects and spontaneous miscarriages should be reported and handled as SAEs. Elective abortions without complications should not be handled as AEs. The outcome of all pregnancies (spontaneous miscarriage, elective termination, ectopic pregnancy, normal birth, or congenital anomaly/birth defect) should be followed up and documented even if the participant was discontinued from the study.

trans-unit.source: If any pregnancy occurs in the course of the study or within the 14-week post-last dose of IP follow-up period, then the Investigator or other site personnel must inform the appropriate Sponsor representatives within one day (ie, immediately but no later than 24 hours of when he or she becomes aware of it).
trans-unit.target: If any pregnancy occurs in the course of the study or within the 14-week post-last dose of IP follow-up period, then the Investigator or other site personnel must inform the appropriate Sponsor representatives within one day (ie, immediately but no later than 24 hours of when he or she becomes aware of it).

trans-unit.source: The designated Sponsor representative works with the Investigator to ensure that all relevant information is provided to the Sponsor Patient Safety data entry site within 1 or 5 calendar days for SAEs (Section 8.3.9) and within 30 days for all other pregnancies.
trans-unit.target: The designated Sponsor representative works with the Investigator to ensure that all relevant information is provided to the Sponsor Patient Safety data entry site within 1 or 5 calendar days for SAEs (Section 8.3.9) and within 30 days for all other pregnancies.

trans-unit.source: The same timelines apply when outcome information is available.
trans-unit.target: The same timelines apply when outcome information is available.

trans-unit.source: The Pregnancy Report (PREGREP) module in the eCRF is used to report the pregnancy and the paper-based PREGOUT form is used to report the outcome of the pregnancy.
trans-unit.target: The Pregnancy Report (PREGREP) module in the eCRF is used to report the pregnancy and the paper-based PREGOUT form is used to report the outcome of the pregnancy.

trans-unit.source: Non-sterilised male participants must use a male condom from enrolment through 14 weeks following the last dose of IP. It is strongly recommended for the female partner of a male participant to also use a highly effective method of contraception throughout this period.
trans-unit.target: Non-sterilised male participants must use a male condom from enrolment through 14 weeks following the last dose of IP. It is strongly recommended for the female partner of a male participant to also use a highly effective method of contraception throughout this period.

trans-unit.source: Non-sterilised male participants should also refrain from biologically fathering a child or donating sperm during the same period. Pregnancy of the participant’s partners is not considered to be an AE. However, the outcome of all pregnancies (spontaneous miscarriage, elective termination, ectopic pregnancy, normal birth, or congenital anomaly), occurring from the date of the first dose until a specified time period after the last dose and as indicated by previous studies (preclinical and clinical) should, if possible, be followed up and documented in the Pregnancy Report Form. Consent from the partner must be obtained before the Pregnancy Report Form is completed.
trans-unit.target: Non-sterilised male participants should also refrain from biologically fathering a child or donating sperm during the same period. Pregnancy of the participant’s partners is not considered to be an AE. However, the outcome of all pregnancies (spontaneous miscarriage, elective termination, ectopic pregnancy, normal birth, or congenital anomaly), occurring from the date of the first dose until a specified time period after the last dose and as indicated by previous studies (preclinical and clinical) should, if possible, be followed up and documented in the Pregnancy Report Form. Consent from the partner must be obtained before the Pregnancy Report Form is completed.

trans-unit.source: Medication Error, Drug Abuse, and Drug Misuse
trans-unit.target: Medication Error, Drug Abuse, and Drug Misuse

trans-unit.source: If an event of medication error, drug abuse, or drug misuse occurs during the course of the study, then the Investigator or other site personnel informs the appropriate Sponsor representatives within one calendar day, ie, immediately but no later than 24 hours of when they become aware of it.
trans-unit.target: If an event of medication error, drug abuse, or drug misuse occurs during the course of the study, then the Investigator or other site personnel informs the appropriate Sponsor representatives within one calendar day, ie, immediately but no later than 24 hours of when they become aware of it.

trans-unit.source: The designated Sponsor representative works with the Investigator to ensure that all relevant information is completed within one (initial fatal/life-threatening or follow-up fatal/life- threatening) or 5 (other serious initial and follow-up) calendar days if there is an SAE associated with the event of medication error, drug abuse, or misuse (see Section 8.3.9) and within 30 days for all other events.
trans-unit.target: The designated Sponsor representative works with the Investigator to ensure that all relevant information is completed within one (initial fatal/life-threatening or follow-up fatal/life- threatening) or 5 (other serious initial and follow-up) calendar days if there is an SAE associated with the event of medication error, drug abuse, or misuse (see Section 8.3.9) and within 30 days for all other events.

trans-unit.source: For the purposes of this clinical study a medication error is an unintended failure or mistake in the treatment process for an IMP or AstraZeneca NIMP that either causes harm to the participant or has the potential to cause harm to the participant.
trans-unit.target: For the purposes of this clinical study a medication error is an unintended failure or mistake in the treatment process for an IMP or AstraZeneca NIMP that either causes harm to the participant or has the potential to cause harm to the participant.

trans-unit.source: The full definition and examples of a medication error can be found in Appendix B 4.
trans-unit.target: The full definition and examples of a medication error can be found in Appendix B 4.

trans-unit.source: Drug abuse is the persistent or sporadic intentional, non-therapeutic excessive use of IMP or AstraZeneca NIMP for a perceived reward or desired non-therapeutic effect.
trans-unit.target: Drug abuse is the persistent or sporadic intentional, non-therapeutic excessive use of IMP or AstraZeneca NIMP for a perceived reward or desired non-therapeutic effect.

trans-unit.source: The full definition and examples of drug abuse can be found in Appendix B 4.
trans-unit.target: The full definition and examples of drug abuse can be found in Appendix B 4.

trans-unit.source: Drug misuse is the intentional and inappropriate use (by a study participant) of IMP or AstraZeneca NIMP for medicinal purposes outside of the authorised product information, or for unauthorised IMPs or AstraZeneca NIMPs, outside of the intended use as specified in the protocol and includes deliberate administration of the product by the wrong route.
trans-unit.target: Drug misuse is the intentional and inappropriate use (by a study participant) of IMP or AstraZeneca NIMP for medicinal purposes outside of the authorised product information, or for unauthorised IMPs or AstraZeneca NIMPs, outside of the intended use as specified in the protocol and includes deliberate administration of the product by the wrong route.

trans-unit.source: The full definition and examples of drug misuse can be found in Appendix B 4.
trans-unit.target: The full definition and examples of drug misuse can be found in Appendix B 4.

trans-unit.source: For this study, any dose of tozorakimab greater than planned dose in the study will be considered an overdose.
trans-unit.target: For this study, any dose of tozorakimab greater than planned dose in the study will be considered an overdose.

trans-unit.source: An overdose with associated AEs is recorded as the AE diagnosis/symptoms on the relevant AE modules in the eCRF and on the Overdose eCRF module.
trans-unit.target: An overdose with associated AEs is recorded as the AE diagnosis/symptoms on the relevant AE modules in the eCRF and on the Overdose eCRF module.

trans-unit.source: An overdose without associated symptoms is only reported on the Overdose eCRF module.
trans-unit.target: An overdose without associated symptoms is only reported on the Overdose eCRF module.

trans-unit.source: 24 hours of when he or she becomes aware of it.

trans-unit.target: The designated Sponsor representative works with the Investigator to ensure that all relevant information is provided to the Sponsor Patient Safety data entry site within one or 5 calendar

trans-unit.source: days for overdoses associated with an SAE (see Section 8.3.9) and within 30 days for all other overdoses.

trans-unit.source: Device Constituent and Third Party Medical Device Deficiencies
trans-unit.target: Device Constituent and Third Party Medical Device Deficiencies

trans-unit.source: Combination products with a device constituent are being provided for use in this study. In order to fulfil regulatory reporting obligations worldwide, the Investigator is responsible for the detection and documentation of events meeting the definitions of a deficiency that occur during the study with the device constituent of tozorakimab combination product.
trans-unit.target: Combination products with a device constituent are being provided for use in this study. In order to fulfil regulatory reporting obligations worldwide, the Investigator is responsible for the detection and documentation of events meeting the definitions of a deficiency that occur during the study with the device constituent of tozorakimab combination product.

trans-unit.source: The definition of a deficiency in the device constituent of a combination product is an inadequacy of a device with respect to its identity, quality, durability, reliability, safety, or performance. Device constituent deficiencies include malfunctions, use errors, and inadequate information supplied by the manufacturer.
trans-unit.target: The definition of a deficiency in the device constituent of a combination product is an inadequacy of a device with respect to its identity, quality, durability, reliability, safety, or performance. Device constituent deficiencies include malfunctions, use errors, and inadequate information supplied by the manufacturer.

trans-unit.source: Device constituent deficiencies from this study will be collected and monitored to ensure the safety of participants and to improve the safety and performance of the device.
trans-unit.target: Device constituent deficiencies from this study will be collected and monitored to ensure the safety of participants and to improve the safety and performance of the device.

trans-unit.source: Device constituent deficiencies will not be presented in the CSR, but where required by local regulations, deficiencies will be summarised in the relevant period report.
trans-unit.target: Device constituent deficiencies will not be presented in the CSR, but where required by local regulations, deficiencies will be summarised in the relevant period report.

trans-unit.source: The Product Complaint Intake Form will be used to collect the deficiency. Additionally, where an SAE has occurred in addition to a malfunction, the Sponsor Clinical Study Medical Device/Device Constituent Report Form will be used.
trans-unit.target: The Product Complaint Intake Form will be used to collect the deficiency. Additionally, where an SAE has occurred in addition to a malfunction, the Sponsor Clinical Study Medical Device/Device Constituent Report Form will be used.

trans-unit.source: In addition, third-party spirometers and ECG machines provided by a central vendor are being provided for use in this study. Instructions for medical device use are provided in device- specific Instructions For Use. All deficiencies relating to these third-party medical devices, will be reported to the manufacturer, who will be responsible for fulfilling their regulatory obligations. The site will directly report deficiencies using the manufacturer’s contact details in the Instructions for Use.
trans-unit.target: In addition, third-party spirometers and ECG machines provided by a central vendor are being provided for use in this study. Instructions for medical device use are provided in device- specific Instructions For Use. All deficiencies relating to these third-party medical devices, will be reported to the manufacturer, who will be responsible for fulfilling their regulatory obligations. The site will directly report deficiencies using the manufacturer’s contact details in the Instructions for Use.

trans-unit.source: Time Period for Detecting Device Constituent Deficiencies
trans-unit.target: Time Period for Detecting Device Constituent Deficiencies

trans-unit.source: Any incident or malfunction related to the IPs will be detected, documented, and reported during all periods of the study in which the IP is used.
trans-unit.target: Any incident or malfunction related to the IPs will be detected, documented, and reported during all periods of the study in which the IP is used.

trans-unit.source: If the Investigator learns of any deficiency at any time after a participant has been discharged from the study, and such incident is considered reasonably related to a device constituent provided for the study, the Investigator will promptly notify the Sponsor.
trans-unit.target: If the Investigator learns of any deficiency at any time after a participant has been discharged from the study, and such incident is considered reasonably related to a device constituent provided for the study, the Investigator will promptly notify the Sponsor.

trans-unit.source: New or updated information will be recorded on the originally completed form with all changes signed and dated by the Investigator.
trans-unit.target: New or updated information will be recorded on the originally completed form with all changes signed and dated by the Investigator.

trans-unit.source: Follow-up applies to all participants, including those who discontinue study intervention.
trans-unit.target: Follow-up applies to all participants, including those who discontinue study intervention.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator is responsible for ensuring that follow-up includes any supplemental investigations as indicated to elucidate the nature and/or causality of the deficiency.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator is responsible for ensuring that follow-up includes any supplemental investigations as indicated to elucidate the nature and/or causality of the deficiency.

trans-unit.source: New or updated information will be recorded on the originally completed form with all changes signed and dated by the Investigator.
