trans-unit.source: Prompt Reporting of a Device Constituent Deficiency to the Sponsor
trans-unit.target: Prompt Reporting of a Device Constituent Deficiency to the Sponsor

trans-unit.source: All device deficiencies will be reported to the Sponsor within 24 hours after the Investigator determines that the event meets the protocol definition of a device constituent deficiency.
trans-unit.target: All device deficiencies will be reported to the Sponsor within 24 hours after the Investigator determines that the event meets the protocol definition of a device constituent deficiency.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor Clinical Study Medical Device/Device Constituent Report Form and/or Product Complaint Intake Form will be sent to the Sponsor by email.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor Clinical Study Medical Device/Device Constituent Report Form and/or Product Complaint Intake Form will be sent to the Sponsor by email.

trans-unit.source: Where an SAE has occurred in addition to the malfunction, the SAE will be recorded in the eCRF as detailed in Section 8.3.9.
trans-unit.target: Where an SAE has occurred in addition to the malfunction, the SAE will be recorded in the eCRF as detailed in Section 8.3.9.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor will be the contact for the receipt of device deficiency reports.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor will be the contact for the receipt of device deficiency reports.

trans-unit.source: An IDMC will be established to oversee the safety of the study participants, ensuring that the studies are being conducted with the highest scientific and ethical standards. The IDMC will periodically review unblinded safety summary tables and listings to evaluate participant safety and make appropriate recommendations, including at the time of interim analysis (refer also to Section 9.5 and Section 9.6). The IDMC will function independently of all other individuals associated with the conduct of the studies, including the Sponsor. The committee will operate in accordance with an IDMC Charter.
trans-unit.target: An IDMC will be established to oversee the safety of the study participants, ensuring that the studies are being conducted with the highest scientific and ethical standards. The IDMC will periodically review unblinded safety summary tables and listings to evaluate participant safety and make appropriate recommendations, including at the time of interim analysis (refer also to Section 9.5 and Section 9.6). The IDMC will function independently of all other individuals associated with the conduct of the studies, including the Sponsor. The committee will operate in accordance with an IDMC Charter.

trans-unit.source: An Independent Adjudication Committee will be constituted to provide an independent, external, systematic, and unbiased assessment of heart failure events (worsening and new onset), MACE, and all AEs with fatal outcome.
trans-unit.target: An Independent Adjudication Committee will be constituted to provide an independent, external, systematic, and unbiased assessment of heart failure events (worsening and new onset), MACE, and all AEs with fatal outcome.

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the collection and handling of biological samples will be provided in the study-specific laboratory manual. Samples should be stored in a secure storage space with adequate measures to protect confidentiality. Research on biological samples, including any possible future research, is subject to national regulations and will only be conducted in a particular country if approved in that country. For further details on Handling of Human Biological Samples, refer to Appendix C.
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the collection and handling of biological samples will be provided in the study-specific laboratory manual. Samples should be stored in a secure storage space with adequate measures to protect confidentiality. Research on biological samples, including any possible future research, is subject to national regulations and will only be conducted in a particular country if approved in that country. For further details on Handling of Human Biological Samples, refer to Appendix C.

trans-unit.source: Samples will be stored for a maximum of 15 years from the date of the issue of the CSR in line with consent and local requirements, after which they will be destroyed/repatriated.
trans-unit.target: Samples will be stored for a maximum of 15 years from the date of the issue of the CSR in line with consent and local requirements, after which they will be destroyed/repatriated.

trans-unit.source: Pharmacokinetic samples will be disposed of after the Bioanalytical Report finalisation or 6 months after issuance of the draft Bioanalytical Report (whichever is earlier).
trans-unit.target: Pharmacokinetic samples will be disposed of after the Bioanalytical Report finalisation or 6 months after issuance of the draft Bioanalytical Report (whichever is earlier).

trans-unit.source: Pharmacokinetic samples may be disposed of or anonymised by pooling. Additional analyses may be conducted on the anonymised, pooled PK samples to further evaluate and validate the analytical method. Any results from such analyses may be reported separately from the CSR.
trans-unit.target: Pharmacokinetic samples may be disposed of or anonymised by pooling. Additional analyses may be conducted on the anonymised, pooled PK samples to further evaluate and validate the analytical method. Any results from such analyses may be reported separately from the CSR.

trans-unit.source: Remaining ADA sample aliquots will be retained by the Sponsor or its designee for a maximum of 15 years following issue of the CSR. Additional use includes, but is not limited to, further characterisation of any ADAs, confirmation and/or requalification of the assay, as well as additional assay development work. The results from future analysis will not be reported in the CSR.
trans-unit.target: Remaining ADA sample aliquots will be retained by the Sponsor or its designee for a maximum of 15 years following issue of the CSR. Additional use includes, but is not limited to, further characterisation of any ADAs, confirmation and/or requalification of the assay, as well as additional assay development work. The results from future analysis will not be reported in the CSR.

trans-unit.source: Serum samples will be collected for measurement of serum concentrations of IP as specified in the SoA (Section 1.3).
trans-unit.target: Serum samples will be collected for measurement of serum concentrations of IP as specified in the SoA (Section 1.3).

trans-unit.source: Samples may be collected at additional time points during the study if warranted and agreed upon between the Investigator and the Sponsor (eg, for safety reasons). The timing of sampling may be altered during the course of the study based on newly available data (eg, to obtain data closer to the time of peak or trough matrix concentrations) to ensure appropriate monitoring.
trans-unit.target: Samples may be collected at additional time points during the study if warranted and agreed upon between the Investigator and the Sponsor (eg, for safety reasons). The timing of sampling may be altered during the course of the study based on newly available data (eg, to obtain data closer to the time of peak or trough matrix concentrations) to ensure appropriate monitoring.

trans-unit.source: Serum samples will be used to analyse the PK of tozorakimab. Samples collected for analyses of tozorakimab serum concentration may also be used to evaluate safety or efficacy aspects related to concerns arising during or after the study.
trans-unit.target: Serum samples will be used to analyse the PK of tozorakimab. Samples collected for analyses of tozorakimab serum concentration may also be used to evaluate safety or efficacy aspects related to concerns arising during or after the study.

trans-unit.source: Samples will be collected, labelled, stored, and shipped as detailed in the Laboratory Manual.
trans-unit.target: Samples will be collected, labelled, stored, and shipped as detailed in the Laboratory Manual.

trans-unit.source: Samples for determination of IP concentration in serum will be assayed by bioanalytical test sites operated by or on behalf of the Sponsor, using an appropriately validated bioanalytical method. Full details of the analytical method used will be described in a separate bioanalytical report.
trans-unit.target: Samples for determination of IP concentration in serum will be assayed by bioanalytical test sites operated by or on behalf of the Sponsor, using an appropriately validated bioanalytical method. Full details of the analytical method used will be described in a separate bioanalytical report.

trans-unit.source: Samples from participants who received placebo may be analysed at minimum selected time points to confirm no dosing with tozorakimab took place.
trans-unit.target: Samples from participants who received placebo may be analysed at minimum selected time points to confirm no dosing with tozorakimab took place.

trans-unit.source: IP concentration information that may unblind the study will not be reported to investigative sites or blinded personnel until the study has been unblinded.
trans-unit.target: IP concentration information that may unblind the study will not be reported to investigative sites or blinded personnel until the study has been unblinded.

trans-unit.source: Incurred sample reproducibility analysis, if any, will be performed alongside the bioanalysis of the test samples. The results from the evaluation, if performed, will be reported in a separate Bioanalytical Report.
trans-unit.target: Incurred sample reproducibility analysis, if any, will be performed alongside the bioanalysis of the test samples. The results from the evaluation, if performed, will be reported in a separate Bioanalytical Report.

trans-unit.source: Blood samples for determination of ADA in serum will be assayed by bioanalytical test sites operated by or on behalf of the Sponsor, using an appropriately validated bioanalytical method. Tiered analyses will be performed to include screening, confirmatory, and titre assay components. All ADA positive samples from the study will be tested for nAb using a validated bioanalytical method. Full details of the methods used will be described in a separate report.
trans-unit.target: Blood samples for determination of ADA in serum will be assayed by bioanalytical test sites operated by or on behalf of the Sponsor, using an appropriately validated bioanalytical method. Tiered analyses will be performed to include screening, confirmatory, and titre assay components. All ADA positive samples from the study will be tested for nAb using a validated bioanalytical method. Full details of the methods used will be described in a separate report.

trans-unit.source: ADA samples may also be further tested for characterisation of ADA response, including possible assessment of neutralising antibody.
trans-unit.target: ADA samples may also be further tested for characterisation of ADA response, including possible assessment of neutralising antibody.

trans-unit.source: Samples will be collected, labelled, stored, and shipped as detailed in the Laboratory Manual.
trans-unit.target: Samples will be collected, labelled, stored, and shipped as detailed in the Laboratory Manual.

trans-unit.source: There are no established PD measures for tozorakimab and no PD analyses are intended to be performed in this study.
trans-unit.target: There are no established PD measures for tozorakimab and no PD analyses are intended to be performed in this study.

trans-unit.target: Overall and COPD-related HRU data will be collected:

trans-unit.source: Annualised rate of hospitalisations and ER/ED visits combined.
trans-unit.target: Annualised rate of hospitalisations and ER/ED visits combined.

trans-unit.source: Number of days in hospital, measured by total number of days in hospital, general ward, intensive care unit, and critical care unit.
trans-unit.target: Number of days in hospital, measured by total number of days in hospital, general ward, intensive care unit, and critical care unit.

trans-unit.source: Note that cases of hospitalisation also must be reported as an SAE (Section 8.3.9).
trans-unit.target: Note that cases of hospitalisation also must be reported as an SAE (Section 8.3.9).

trans-unit.source: This study acts as an LTE following an initial 52-week treatment period in predecessor studies OBERON and TITANIA. Analyses will include data from participants enrolled in the predecessor studies whenever applicable in order to minimise the impact of bias caused by dropout and in order to allow inferences to be made from the time of first randomisation and dosing. The primary reporting of the study may take place once both predecessor studies have been unblinded and reported without waiting for all participants to complete the treatment period in this study. The number of severe exacerbations observed in the study by this point will be monitored to estimate statistical power before the decision to unblind is confirmed.
trans-unit.target: This study acts as an LTE following an initial 52-week treatment period in predecessor studies OBERON and TITANIA. Analyses will include data from participants enrolled in the predecessor studies whenever applicable in order to minimise the impact of bias caused by dropout and in order to allow inferences to be made from the time of first randomisation and dosing. The primary reporting of the study may take place once both predecessor studies have been unblinded and reported without waiting for all participants to complete the treatment period in this study. The number of severe exacerbations observed in the study by this point will be monitored to estimate statistical power before the decision to unblind is confirmed.

trans-unit.source: The primary efficacy endpoint is the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations over the treatment period incorporating both the predecessor studies and PROSPERO. Here the treatment period will vary between participants (28 or 52 weeks) dependent upon the CSP version under which they were recruited. The primary endpoint will be assessed first in the Primary Population of former smokers, followed by the Overall Population of former or current smokers.
trans-unit.target: The primary efficacy endpoint is the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations over the treatment period incorporating both the predecessor studies and PROSPERO. Here the treatment period will vary between participants (28 or 52 weeks) dependent upon the CSP version under which they were recruited. The primary endpoint will be assessed first in the Primary Population of former smokers, followed by the Overall Population of former or current smokers.

trans-unit.source: The tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm is used in the predecessor studies only for characterisation of the dose-response curve, and not for confirmatory hypothesis testing and, as of CSP Version 2.0, no new participants will enter PROSPERO receiving this regimen. As such, confirmatory hypothesis tests will only be conducted on the comparison of tozorakimab
trans-unit.target: The tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm is used in the predecessor studies only for characterisation of the dose-response curve, and not for confirmatory hypothesis testing and, as of CSP Version 2.0, no new participants will enter PROSPERO receiving this regimen. As such, confirmatory hypothesis tests will only be conducted on the comparison of tozorakimab

trans-unit.source: The null hypothesis for tozorakimab is that the annualised severe COPD exacerbation rate on tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W is equal to the severe COPD exacerbation rate on placebo, or that the rate ratio equals 1. The alternative hypothesis is that the severe COPD exacerbation rate on tozorakimab is not equal to the exacerbation rate on placebo, ie:
trans-unit.target: The null hypothesis for tozorakimab is that the annualised severe COPD exacerbation rate on tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W is equal to the severe COPD exacerbation rate on placebo, or that the rate ratio equals 1. The alternative hypothesis is that the severe COPD exacerbation rate on tozorakimab is not equal to the exacerbation rate on placebo, ie:

trans-unit.source: H0: Rate ratio of tozorakimab 300mg Q4W SC versus placebo = 1 H1: Rate ratio of tozorakimab 300mg Q4W SC versus placebo ≠ 1
trans-unit.target: H0: Rate ratio of tozorakimab 300mg Q4W SC versus placebo = 1 H1: Rate ratio of tozorakimab 300mg Q4W SC versus placebo ≠ 1

trans-unit.source: Hypothesis testing for the primary analysis, annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations, will be performed at the 2-sided 5% significance level. If the p-value is less than 0.05 and the treatment effect favours tozorakimab, H0 will be rejected.
trans-unit.target: Hypothesis testing for the primary analysis, annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations, will be performed at the 2-sided 5% significance level. If the p-value is less than 0.05 and the treatment effect favours tozorakimab, H0 will be rejected.

trans-unit.source: A multiple testing procedure will be applied to control type I error rate at the 2-sided significance level of 5% for the primary and key secondary endpoints; details are provided in
trans-unit.target: A multiple testing procedure will be applied to control type I error rate at the 2-sided significance level of 5% for the primary and key secondary endpoints; details are provided in

trans-unit.source: Section 9.1.2. Significance of hypothesis tests will be determined according to the significance levels defined by this testing procedure.
trans-unit.target: Section 9.1.2. Significance of hypothesis tests will be determined according to the significance levels defined by this testing procedure.

trans-unit.source: Analyses of the treatment policy estimand will be conducted in the FAS, in which all observed data in participants who were dosed will be used regardless of whether participants remain on randomised treatment, aside from data after planned re-allocation of IP. This will be the primary estimand of interest.
trans-unit.target: Analyses of the treatment policy estimand will be conducted in the FAS, in which all observed data in participants who were dosed will be used regardless of whether participants remain on randomised treatment, aside from data after planned re-allocation of IP. This will be the primary estimand of interest.

trans-unit.source: Analysis of the while-on-treatment estimand will be conducted using the on-treatment period applied to the FAS, where only data obtained prior to participants experiencing intercurrent events of discontinuation from randomised treatment and/or initiation of prohibited biologic medication will be used. For those discontinuing randomised treatment, this assumes that efficacy observed on treatment is reflective of what would have occurred had the participant not discontinued treatment. The while-on-treatment estimand will be provided as a supporting analysis for the primary and key secondary endpoints and time to death and will be the main estimand for the remaining secondary endpoints and exploratory endpoints for which assessments are only conducted up until the E/D visit.
trans-unit.target: Analysis of the while-on-treatment estimand will be conducted using the on-treatment period applied to the FAS, where only data obtained prior to participants experiencing intercurrent events of discontinuation from randomised treatment and/or initiation of prohibited biologic medication will be used. For those discontinuing randomised treatment, this assumes that efficacy observed on treatment is reflective of what would have occurred had the participant not discontinued treatment. The while-on-treatment estimand will be provided as a supporting analysis for the primary and key secondary endpoints and time to death and will be the main estimand for the remaining secondary endpoints and exploratory endpoints for which assessments are only conducted up until the E/D visit.

trans-unit.source: To account for multiplicity in testing for the primary endpoint (annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations) and key secondary endpoint (time to first severe COPD exacerbation) for tozorakimab in both the Primary Population of former smokers and the Overall Population of former or current smokers, a testing strategy will be used to strongly control the overall type I error rate at the 2-sided 5% level. The testing strategy is depicted in Figure 3.
trans-unit.target: To account for multiplicity in testing for the primary endpoint (annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations) and key secondary endpoint (time to first severe COPD exacerbation) for tozorakimab in both the Primary Population of former smokers and the Overall Population of former or current smokers, a testing strategy will be used to strongly control the overall type I error rate at the 2-sided 5% level. The testing strategy is depicted in Figure 3.

trans-unit.source: Figure 3 Type I Error Control for Comparison of 300 mg Q4W Versus Placebo

trans-unit.source: of these tests conducted at a 5% 2-sided significance level. Otherwise, no null hypotheses will be rejected for any other endpoint in either population.
trans-unit.target: of these tests conducted at a 5% 2-sided significance level. Otherwise, no null hypotheses will be rejected for any other endpoint in either population.

trans-unit.source: Hierarchical testing will continue at the given significance level until a null hypothesis cannot be rejected; at this time, further testing will stop, and no subsequent null hypotheses in the testing hierarchy will be rejected for that dose.

trans-unit.target: These hypothesis tests for key secondary endpoints will proceed in each study as follows:

trans-unit.source: The primary endpoint for the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations will be tested at the 5% 2-sided significance level in the overall All Comer Population (former or current smokers).
trans-unit.target: The primary endpoint for the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations will be tested at the 5% 2-sided significance level in the overall All Comer Population (former or current smokers).

trans-unit.source: If this null hypothesis is rejected, then the key secondary endpoint of time to first severe COPD exacerbation will be tested in the population of former smokers.
trans-unit.target: If this null hypothesis is rejected, then the key secondary endpoint of time to first severe COPD exacerbation will be tested in the population of former smokers.

trans-unit.source: If this null hypothesis is rejected, then the key secondary endpoint of time to first severe COPD exacerbation will be tested in the Overall Population of former or current smokers (all-comers).
trans-unit.target: If this null hypothesis is rejected, then the key secondary endpoint of time to first severe COPD exacerbation will be tested in the Overall Population of former or current smokers (all-comers).

trans-unit.source: If this null hypothesis is rejected, then the key secondary endpoint of annualised rate of COPD exacerbation requiring hospitalisation and/or ER/ED visits will be tested in the population of former smokers.
trans-unit.target: If this null hypothesis is rejected, then the key secondary endpoint of annualised rate of COPD exacerbation requiring hospitalisation and/or ER/ED visits will be tested in the population of former smokers.

trans-unit.source: If this null hypothesis is rejected, then the key secondary endpoint of annualised rate of COPD exacerbation requiring hospitalisation and/or ER/ED visits will be tested in the Overall Population of former or current smokers (all-comers).
trans-unit.target: If this null hypothesis is rejected, then the key secondary endpoint of annualised rate of COPD exacerbation requiring hospitalisation and/or ER/ED visits will be tested in the Overall Population of former or current smokers (all-comers).

trans-unit.source: The number of participants entering PROSPERO is determined by the number of participants who complete the treatment period in the predecessor studies (OBERON and TITANIA) and have not been prematurely discontinued from IP. As such the sample size entering PROSPERO is anticipated to be up to 2120 participants, although this is dependent upon the number of participants recruited to the tozorakimab Q8W arm and the proportion of participants who complete the predecessor study and consent to enter this extension so the actual number may be either higher or lower.

trans-unit.source: All participants randomised and dosed in the predecessor studies OBERON and TITANIA will be incorporated into analyses to support this CSR, even if they did not enter the PROSPERO study. This allows valid randomised comparisons to be made from the point of randomisation and first dose onwards. The populations are defined as indicated in Table 7 and treatment groups to be compared within these populations in Table 8.

trans-unit.source: All participants randomised and receiving at least one dose of IP in OBERON or TITANIA, irrespective of their continued participation into PROSPERO. Participants will be analysed according to their randomised treatment, irrespective of whether or not they have prematurely discontinued, according to the ITT principle. Participants who withdraw consent to participate in the study, who did not enter PROSPERO, or who were re-randomised from placebo to tozorakimab treatment in PROSPERO will be included up to the date of their study termination or re-randomisation.

Participants who withdraw consent to participate in the study, who did not enter PROSPERO, or who were re-randomised from placebo to tozorakimab treatment in PROSPERO will be included up to the date of their study termination or re-randomisation.

trans-unit.source: All participants who have received at least one dose of IP in OBERON or TITANIA, irrespective of their continued participation into PROSPERO. Erroneously treated participants (eg, those randomised to Treatment A but actually given Treatment B) are accounted for in the treatment group of the treatment they actually received. A participant who has on one or several occasions received active IP is classified as active.

trans-unit.source: In addition to the populations defined above, subsets of each population (SAF-LTE or FAS- LTE) will be defined, which includes only participants who enrolled and who have received at least 1 dose of IP in the PROSPERO LTE study. As these are based on completers of the predecessor study, they may be subject to selection bias, where less severe participants may be more likely to complete the predecessor study and enrol into the LTE study. These populations will be used for supportive analyses only for making comparisons of the first year of data in the predecessor studies to the second year of data in PROSPERO in a similar set of participants. The number of participants discontinuing IP and patterns of discontinuations will be considered when interpreting the data. Reporting will be performed over particular periods

trans-unit.source: The SAP will be finalised prior to the first IDMC meeting, BSSR, or interim analysis, (whichever occurs first) involving data from this study and will describe the participant populations to be included in the analyses, and procedures for accounting for missing, unused, and spurious data along with a more technical and detailed description of the statistical analyses described in this section. Any deviations from this plan will be reported in the CSR.
trans-unit.target: The SAP will be finalised prior to the first IDMC meeting, BSSR, or interim analysis, (whichever occurs first) involving data from this study and will describe the participant populations to be included in the analyses, and procedures for accounting for missing, unused, and spurious data along with a more technical and detailed description of the statistical analyses described in this section. Any deviations from this plan will be reported in the CSR.

trans-unit.source: This section is a summary of the planned statistical analyses of the most important endpoints including primary and key secondary endpoints.
trans-unit.target: This section is a summary of the planned statistical analyses of the most important endpoints including primary and key secondary endpoints.

trans-unit.source: Analyses will be performed by Sponsor or its representatives.
trans-unit.target: Analyses will be performed by Sponsor or its representatives.

trans-unit.source: The primary endpoint is the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations. The primary analysis will be conducted in the former smoker population of the FAS population pooling data from the predecessor studies and PROSPERO study, comparing the Randomised tozorakimab 300 mg SC Q4W, and Randomised placebo treatment groups as described in Section 9.3. Participants randomised to placebo in the predecessor studies but re-randomised to tozorakimab in the PROSPERO study will only have data from the predecessor studies used in the primary analysis, with data for the remaining period imputed as if the participants had remained on placebo, given that this data is missing by design and determined by randomisation. This will provide an estimate of the treatment policy estimand in the scenario where all participants had remained on their initially randomised treatment for the duration of the study.
trans-unit.target: The primary endpoint is the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations. The primary analysis will be conducted in the former smoker population of the FAS population pooling data from the predecessor studies and PROSPERO study, comparing the Randomised tozorakimab 300 mg SC Q4W, and Randomised placebo treatment groups as described in Section 9.3. Participants randomised to placebo in the predecessor studies but re-randomised to tozorakimab in the PROSPERO study will only have data from the predecessor studies used in the primary analysis, with data for the remaining period imputed as if the participants had remained on placebo, given that this data is missing by design and determined by randomisation. This will provide an estimate of the treatment policy estimand in the scenario where all participants had remained on their initially randomised treatment for the duration of the study.

trans-unit.source: A supportive on-treatment analysis of severe exacerbation endpoints will also be produced.
trans-unit.target: A supportive on-treatment analysis of severe exacerbation endpoints will also be produced.

trans-unit.source: The primary endpoint is the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations. This will be assessed for tozorakimab versus placebo primarily in the population of former smokers. Analyses will be conducted in the FAS, using all available data, where all observed data will be used regardless of treatment or study discontinuation, background treatment switching, or change in smoking status, aside from data after planned re-allocation of IP. The use of this population will provide an estimate of the treatment policy estimand.
trans-unit.target: The primary endpoint is the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations. This will be assessed for tozorakimab versus placebo primarily in the population of former smokers. Analyses will be conducted in the FAS, using all available data, where all observed data will be used regardless of treatment or study discontinuation, background treatment switching, or change in smoking status, aside from data after planned re-allocation of IP. The use of this population will provide an estimate of the treatment policy estimand.

trans-unit.source: The rate of severe COPD exacerbations in the tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W group will be compared to the exacerbation rate in the placebo group using a negative binomial model. The response variable in the model will be the number of severe COPD exacerbations over the duration of the treatment period after applying dropout based multiple imputation. The model will include treatment, predecessor study, region, maintenance inhaled therapy, and baseline COPD exacerbation history as categorical covariates, and baseline post-BD percent predicted FEV1 and log blood eosinophil count as continuous covariates. The log of the follow-up time will be used as an offset variable in the model with this taken or imputed up to the end of the planned treatment period.
trans-unit.target: The rate of severe COPD exacerbations in the tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W group will be compared to the exacerbation rate in the placebo group using a negative binomial model. The response variable in the model will be the number of severe COPD exacerbations over the duration of the treatment period after applying dropout based multiple imputation. The model will include treatment, predecessor study, region, maintenance inhaled therapy, and baseline COPD exacerbation history as categorical covariates, and baseline post-BD percent predicted FEV1 and log blood eosinophil count as continuous covariates. The log of the follow-up time will be used as an offset variable in the model with this taken or imputed up to the end of the planned treatment period.

trans-unit.source: The estimated treatment effect (ie, the rate ratio of tozorakimab versus placebo), corresponding 95% CI, and 2-sided p-value for the rate ratio will be presented. In addition, the exacerbation rate in each treatment group will be presented.
trans-unit.target: The estimated treatment effect (ie, the rate ratio of tozorakimab versus placebo), corresponding 95% CI, and 2-sided p-value for the rate ratio will be presented. In addition, the exacerbation rate in each treatment group will be presented.

trans-unit.source: Any COPD exacerbation that occurs within 7 days of the last dose of systemic corticosteroids or antibiotics or the last day of hospitalisation for a prior COPD exacerbation will be combined and counted as a single exacerbation event and as the maximum severity between the individual events. Further details on combining exacerbation events will be provided in the SAP. Similar criteria will be applied to all exacerbation endpoints.
trans-unit.target: Any COPD exacerbation that occurs within 7 days of the last dose of systemic corticosteroids or antibiotics or the last day of hospitalisation for a prior COPD exacerbation will be combined and counted as a single exacerbation event and as the maximum severity between the individual events. Further details on combining exacerbation events will be provided in the SAP. Similar criteria will be applied to all exacerbation endpoints.

trans-unit.source: Participants who remain in PROSPERO at the time of data cutoff will have data utilised up to this point. For participants lost to follow-up, or prematurely withdrawn from the study, data from the date of last contact to the date of data cutoff or end of planned treatment period (28 or 52 weeks, respectively) will be imputed. Similarly, participants re-randomised from placebo to active therapy will have data imputed from the end of the predecessor study to the date of data cutoff or end of planned treatment period. Only data prior to participants being switched off the 300 mg Q8W arm will be utilised.
trans-unit.target: Participants who remain in PROSPERO at the time of data cutoff will have data utilised up to this point. For participants lost to follow-up, or prematurely withdrawn from the study, data from the date of last contact to the date of data cutoff or end of planned treatment period (28 or 52 weeks, respectively) will be imputed. Similarly, participants re-randomised from placebo to active therapy will have data imputed from the end of the predecessor study to the date of data cutoff or end of planned treatment period. Only data prior to participants being switched off the 300 mg Q8W arm will be utilised.

trans-unit.source: In the primary analysis multiple imputation will be carried out where event counts post study withdrawal will be imputed conditional upon the observed number of events prior to the withdrawal (Gottlow et al 2015, Wan et al 2015). The missing data for participants who withdraw early will be imputed based on dropout reason-based multiple imputation (DRMI) where event counts after withdrawals unrelated to treatment are imputed under missing at random assumptions and those related to treatment are imputed under missing not at random assumptions. Event counts post withdrawal for participants in the tozorakimab treatment arm who dropped out for a treatment-related reason are imputed conditional upon the negative binomial rate parameter for the placebo control arm given baseline covariates. For all other participants the rate conditional upon their randomised treatment will be used. Reasons for dropout will be categorised based upon blinded data. Imputation will be applied to each of multiple bootstrap datasets before conducting the primary analysis model and estimates based upon each dataset will then be combined using Rubin’s rules (Fleming 2011, Ratitch et al 2013).
trans-unit.target: In the primary analysis multiple imputation will be carried out where event counts post study withdrawal will be imputed conditional upon the observed number of events prior to the withdrawal (Gottlow et al 2015, Wan et al 2015). The missing data for participants who withdraw early will be imputed based on dropout reason-based multiple imputation (DRMI) where event counts after withdrawals unrelated to treatment are imputed under missing at random assumptions and those related to treatment are imputed under missing not at random assumptions. Event counts post withdrawal for participants in the tozorakimab treatment arm who dropped out for a treatment-related reason are imputed conditional upon the negative binomial rate parameter for the placebo control arm given baseline covariates. For all other participants the rate conditional upon their randomised treatment will be used. Reasons for dropout will be categorised based upon blinded data. Imputation will be applied to each of multiple bootstrap datasets before conducting the primary analysis model and estimates based upon each dataset will then be combined using Rubin’s rules (Fleming 2011, Ratitch et al 2013).

trans-unit.source: Analysis for all secondary endpoints will be conducted in the FAS in the Primary Population of former smokers and in the Overall Population of former or current smokers.
trans-unit.target: Analysis for all secondary endpoints will be conducted in the FAS in the Primary Population of former smokers and in the Overall Population of former or current smokers.

trans-unit.source: Annualised Rate of Severe COPD Exacerbations
trans-unit.target: Annualised Rate of Severe COPD Exacerbations

trans-unit.source: The annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations will be analysed as a key secondary endpoint in the All Comer Population of former or current smokers, using similar methods as in the primary analysis (Section 9.4.2.1), with the addition of a covariate for smoking status.
trans-unit.target: The annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations will be analysed as a key secondary endpoint in the All Comer Population of former or current smokers, using similar methods as in the primary analysis (Section 9.4.2.1), with the addition of a covariate for smoking status.

trans-unit.source: Time to First Severe COPD Exacerbation
trans-unit.target: Time to First Severe COPD Exacerbation

trans-unit.source: A key secondary endpoint is the time to first severe COPD exacerbation over the treatment period. Analyses will be conducted in the former smoker population, followed by the Overall Population of former or current smokers.
trans-unit.target: A key secondary endpoint is the time to first severe COPD exacerbation over the treatment period. Analyses will be conducted in the former smoker population, followed by the Overall Population of former or current smokers.

trans-unit.source: The time to first severe COPD exacerbation is defined as:
trans-unit.target: The time to first severe COPD exacerbation is defined as:

trans-unit.source: Start date of first severe COPD exacerbation – Date of randomisation + 1
trans-unit.target: Start date of first severe COPD exacerbation – Date of randomisation + 1

trans-unit.source: The hazard ratio of severe COPD exacerbations in tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W group relative to the placebo group will be derived using a Cox-proportional hazards model. The response variable in the model will be the time to first severe COPD exacerbation. The model will include covariates of treatment group, predecessor study, region, maintenance inhaled therapy, and baseline COPD exacerbation history as categorical covariates, and baseline post-BD percent predicted FEV1 and log blood eosinophil count as continuous covariates. Smoking status will be included as a covariate in analyses in the Overall Population of former or current smokers.
trans-unit.target: The hazard ratio of severe COPD exacerbations in tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W group relative to the placebo group will be derived using a Cox-proportional hazards model. The response variable in the model will be the time to first severe COPD exacerbation. The model will include covariates of treatment group, predecessor study, region, maintenance inhaled therapy, and baseline COPD exacerbation history as categorical covariates, and baseline post-BD percent predicted FEV1 and log blood eosinophil count as continuous covariates. Smoking status will be included as a covariate in analyses in the Overall Population of former or current smokers.

trans-unit.source: Estimated adjusted hazard ratios relative to the placebo comparator will be displayed along with the associated 2-sided 95% CI and p-values.
trans-unit.target: Estimated adjusted hazard ratios relative to the placebo comparator will be displayed along with the associated 2-sided 95% CI and p-values.

trans-unit.source: Participants who remain in PROSPERO at the time of data cutoff and do not experience a severe COPD exacerbation will be censored at the date of data cutoff. For participants who did not experience a COPD exacerbation before being lost to follow-up, or prematurely withdrawn from the study, data from the date of last contact to the date of data cutoff or end of planned treatment period (28 or 52 weeks, respectively) will be imputed before censoring is applied. Similarly, participants re-randomised from placebo to active therapy will have data imputed from the end of the predecessor study to the date of data cutoff or end of planned treatment period. Participants switched off the 300 mg Q8W arm will be censored at the point of switching dose regimen in the primary efficacy analyses.
trans-unit.target: Participants who remain in PROSPERO at the time of data cutoff and do not experience a severe COPD exacerbation will be censored at the date of data cutoff. For participants who did not experience a COPD exacerbation before being lost to follow-up, or prematurely withdrawn from the study, data from the date of last contact to the date of data cutoff or end of planned treatment period (28 or 52 weeks, respectively) will be imputed before censoring is applied. Similarly, participants re-randomised from placebo to active therapy will have data imputed from the end of the predecessor study to the date of data cutoff or end of planned treatment period. Participants switched off the 300 mg Q8W arm will be censored at the point of switching dose regimen in the primary efficacy analyses.

trans-unit.source: Annualised Rate of COPD Exacerbation Requiring Hospitalisation and/or ER/ED Visits
trans-unit.target: Annualised Rate of COPD Exacerbation Requiring Hospitalisation and/or ER/ED Visits

trans-unit.source: The annualised rate of COPD exacerbations requiring hospitalisation and/or ER/ED visits will be analysed as a key secondary endpoint in the former smoker population, followed by the Overall Population of former or current smokers, using similar methods as in the primary analysis.
trans-unit.target: The annualised rate of COPD exacerbations requiring hospitalisation and/or ER/ED visits will be analysed as a key secondary endpoint in the former smoker population, followed by the Overall Population of former or current smokers, using similar methods as in the primary analysis.

trans-unit.source: Time to First Moderate to Severe COPD Exacerbation
trans-unit.target: Time to First Moderate to Severe COPD Exacerbation

trans-unit.source: The time to first moderate to severe COPD exacerbation will be analysed as a secondary efficacy endpoint in former smokers. Participants are not asked to fill in a daily symptom diary in this study and so once participants discontinue randomised treatment the identification of moderate COPD exacerbations may not be systematic. Therefore, only the while-on-treatment estimand will be addressed for endpoints relating to moderate and severe COPD exacerbations using the on-treatment period.
trans-unit.target: The time to first moderate to severe COPD exacerbation will be analysed as a secondary efficacy endpoint in former smokers. Participants are not asked to fill in a daily symptom diary in this study and so once participants discontinue randomised treatment the identification of moderate COPD exacerbations may not be systematic. Therefore, only the while-on-treatment estimand will be addressed for endpoints relating to moderate and severe COPD exacerbations using the on-treatment period.

trans-unit.source: A Cox proportional hazard model will be fitted with similar covariates and methods to the while-on-treatment supportive analysis of the primary endpoint as described in
trans-unit.target: A Cox proportional hazard model will be fitted with similar covariates and methods to the while-on-treatment supportive analysis of the primary endpoint as described in

trans-unit.source: Section 9.4.2.1. Hazard ratios, 95% CI, and p-values will be reported as well as the proportion of participants with an event. The time to first moderate or severe COPD exacerbation among former or current smokers will be analysed in a similar manner.
trans-unit.target: Section 9.4.2.1. Hazard ratios, 95% CI, and p-values will be reported as well as the proportion of participants with an event. The time to first moderate or severe COPD exacerbation among former or current smokers will be analysed in a similar manner.

trans-unit.source: Annualised Rate of Moderate-to-Severe COPD Exacerbations
trans-unit.target: Annualised Rate of Moderate-to-Severe COPD Exacerbations

trans-unit.source: The secondary endpoint of annualised rate of moderate to severe COPD exacerbations during the on-treatment period will be assessed for each dose of tozorakimab versus placebo in former smokers in a similar manner to while-on-treatment supportive analysis of the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations as described above. A similar analysis in former or current smokers will be conducted. Smoking status will be included as a covariate in analyses in the all-comer population (ie, former or current smokers).
trans-unit.target: The secondary endpoint of annualised rate of moderate to severe COPD exacerbations during the on-treatment period will be assessed for each dose of tozorakimab versus placebo in former smokers in a similar manner to while-on-treatment supportive analysis of the annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations as described above. A similar analysis in former or current smokers will be conducted. Smoking status will be included as a covariate in analyses in the all-comer population (ie, former or current smokers).

trans-unit.source: Time to Death (All-cause Mortality and Respiratory-related Mortality)
trans-unit.target: Time to Death (All-cause Mortality and Respiratory-related Mortality)

trans-unit.source: The incidence of death, all cause and respiratory related, will be summarised in the FAS. If there are sufficient number of events, a Cox proportional hazards model may be used to compare time to event for these mortality endpoints between treatment groups. The number and percentage of participants who die from any cause will be presented by treatment group.
trans-unit.target: The incidence of death, all cause and respiratory related, will be summarised in the FAS. If there are sufficient number of events, a Cox proportional hazards model may be used to compare time to event for these mortality endpoints between treatment groups. The number and percentage of participants who die from any cause will be presented by treatment group.

trans-unit.source: Serum concentrations of tozorakimab will be summarised in the PK analysis set using descriptive statistics at each visit applying the continuation treatment groups comparing the data from participants who were first dosed with tozorakimab in predecessor studies separately from those participants who newly received active treatment in PROSPERO. Details of the PK evaluation or any additional PK or PK/PD analyses will be outlined in the SAP.

trans-unit.source: Immunogenicity information will be analysed for tozorakimab and placebo treatments using the information for both predecessor and PROSPERO studies.
trans-unit.target: Immunogenicity information will be analysed for tozorakimab and placebo treatments using the information for both predecessor and PROSPERO studies.

trans-unit.source: The ADA and corresponding titre will be summarised at each visit by treatment group. Additionally, the ADA status across studies will also be summarised using continuation treatment groups and ADA categories. The potential effects of ADA status and ADA titre on PK, efficacy, and safety will be evaluated.

trans-unit.source: Change from Baseline in Pre-dose/Pre-BD FEV1
trans-unit.target: Change from Baseline in Pre-dose/Pre-BD FEV1

trans-unit.source: Change from baseline in pre-dose/pre-BD FEV1 will be analysed between tozorakimab and placebo using a repeated measures linear model in the on-treatment period of the FAS. The dependent variable will be the change from baseline in pre-dose/pre-BD FEV1 total score at post-baseline visits up to the Follow-up Visit. Treatment, visit, treatment-by-visit interaction, predecessor study, region, maintenance inhaled therapy, and the number of exacerbations in the previous year will be fitted as categorical covariates and baseline FEV1 and post-BD percent FEV1 predicted, and log baseline blood eosinophil count as will be fitted as continuous covariates. An unstructured variance-covariance matrix will be used to model correlation per participant. If this model fails to converge, an alternative structure will be employed as pre-specified in the SAP.
trans-unit.target: Change from baseline in pre-dose/pre-BD FEV1 will be analysed between tozorakimab and placebo using a repeated measures linear model in the on-treatment period of the FAS. The dependent variable will be the change from baseline in pre-dose/pre-BD FEV1 total score at post-baseline visits up to the Follow-up Visit. Treatment, visit, treatment-by-visit interaction, predecessor study, region, maintenance inhaled therapy, and the number of exacerbations in the previous year will be fitted as categorical covariates and baseline FEV1 and post-BD percent FEV1 predicted, and log baseline blood eosinophil count as will be fitted as continuous covariates. An unstructured variance-covariance matrix will be used to model correlation per participant. If this model fails to converge, an alternative structure will be employed as pre-specified in the SAP.

trans-unit.source: Contrasts will be used to produce treatment effect estimates at each visit. This will be reported alongside the 2-sided 95% CI and p-value.
trans-unit.target: Contrasts will be used to produce treatment effect estimates at each visit. This will be reported alongside the 2-sided 95% CI and p-value.

trans-unit.target: Rate of Decline in Trough and Post-BD FEV1

trans-unit.source: The rate of decline in trough and post-BD FEV1 will be analysed between tozorakimab and placebo using the on-treatment period of the FAS. Pre-dose trough FEV1 will be analysed with a linear mixed model with random participant slopes of FEV1 versus time and random participant intercepts. The time is calculated as weeks between the date of the first dose of study treatment and the date of the visit:
trans-unit.target: The rate of decline in trough and post-BD FEV1 will be analysed between tozorakimab and placebo using the on-treatment period of the FAS. Pre-dose trough FEV1 will be analysed with a linear mixed model with random participant slopes of FEV1 versus time and random participant intercepts. The time is calculated as weeks between the date of the first dose of study treatment and the date of the visit:

trans-unit.source: (Date of Visit – Date of First Dose +1) / 7
trans-unit.target: (Date of Visit – Date of First Dose +1) / 7

trans-unit.source: Slopes from Week 4 of the predecessor studies will be considered for the analysis. An unstructured correlation matrix will be employed for these 2 random effects. The model will fit a distinct fixed mean for each treatment and a distinct fixed slope (of FEV1 versus time) for each treatment. The model will include the interaction between treatment (as a categorical covariate) and time (as a continuous covariate). Other covariates in the model will be percentage baseline FEV1 and baseline blood eosinophil count as continuous covariates and treatment, predecessor study, region, maintenance inhaled therapy, and the number of exacerbations in the previous year as categorical covariates. The rate of decline (the negative of the slope) will be estimated and compared between treatments for the treatment policy estimand. Similar models will be fitted for the former smoker and Overall Population of

Other covariates in the model will be percentage baseline FEV1 and baseline blood eosinophil count as continuous covariates and treatment, predecessor study, region, maintenance inhaled therapy, and the number of exacerbations in the previous year as categorical covariates. In addition, potentially clinically significant values will be identified and summarised.

trans-unit.source: An IDMC will oversee a futility analysis in the predecessor studies to assess whether the studies can be stopped for futility and may oversee an interim analysis in this study and the predecessor studies based upon the primary endpoints to assess whether the studies can be reported early for overwhelming efficacy. The IDMC will also periodically review unblinded safety summary tables and listings to evaluate participant safety and make appropriate recommendations, including at the time of interim analysis (Section 9.6). The committee will operate in accordance with an IDMC charter.

The committee will operate in accordance with an IDMC charter.

trans-unit.target: Implications of Interim Analysis for Futility in OBERON and TITANIA

trans-unit.source: An interim analysis for efficacy may also be conducted and an alpha adjustment will be made only if in the Primary Population of former smokers analysis, the following point is reached:

trans-unit.source: At least 80% information fraction for the rate of moderate or severe exacerbations is reached in each predecessor study, and
trans-unit.target: At least 80% information fraction for the rate of moderate or severe exacerbations is reached in each predecessor study, and

trans-unit.target: At least 70% information fraction for the rate of severe exacerbations are observed in a pooled analysis of this study and the 2 predecessor studies, and

trans-unit.source: This is reached by at least 6 months prior to the point where every participant has been treated for one year in the predecessor studies.

trans-unit.source: Decision guidance will be provided to the IDMC. The interim analysis could lead to early reporting of PROSPERO if the interim analysis for efficacy is met. The study would still be continuing with sites and patients remaining blinded to collect further longer-term safety information.

The study would still be continuing with sites and patients remaining blinded to collect further longer-term safety information.

trans-unit.target: 0.025 one-sided, or equivalently 2-sided 0.05 for the tozorakimab Q4W comparison with

trans-unit.source: This study will be conducted in accordance with the protocol and with the following:

trans-unit.source: Consensus ethical principles derived from international guidelines including the Declaration of Helsinki as amended at 64th WMA General Assembly, Fortaleza, Brazil, October 2013 and Council for International Organisations of Medical Sciences (CIOMS) International Ethical Guidelines.
trans-unit.target: Consensus ethical principles derived from international guidelines including the Declaration of Helsinki as amended at 64th WMA General Assembly, Fortaleza, Brazil, October 2013 and Council for International Organisations of Medical Sciences (CIOMS) International Ethical Guidelines.

trans-unit.source: The protocol, revised protocol, ICF, IB, and other relevant documents (eg, advertisements) must be submitted to an IRB/IEC by the Investigator and reviewed and approved by the IRB/IEC before the study is initiated.
trans-unit.target: The protocol, revised protocol, ICF, IB, and other relevant documents (eg, advertisements) must be submitted to an IRB/IEC by the Investigator and reviewed and approved by the IRB/IEC before the study is initiated.

trans-unit.source: Any revised protocol will require IRB/IEC and applicable Regulatory Authority approval before implementation of changes made to the study design, except for changes necessary to eliminate an immediate hazard to study participants.
trans-unit.target: Any revised protocol will require IRB/IEC and applicable Regulatory Authority approval before implementation of changes made to the study design, except for changes necessary to eliminate an immediate hazard to study participants.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor will be responsible for obtaining the required authorisations to conduct the study from the concerned Regulatory Authority. This responsibility may be delegated to a CRO, but the accountability remains with the Sponsor.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor will be responsible for obtaining the required authorisations to conduct the study from the concerned Regulatory Authority. This responsibility may be delegated to a CRO, but the accountability remains with the Sponsor.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator will be responsible for providing oversight of the conduct of the study at the site and adherence to requirements of 21 CFR 312.120, ICH guidelines, the IRB/IEC, European Regulation 536/2014 for clinical studies (if applicable), European Medical Device Regulation 2017/745 for clinical device research (if applicable), and all other applicable local regulations.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator will be responsible for providing oversight of the conduct of the study at the site and adherence to requirements of 21 CFR 312.120, ICH guidelines, the IRB/IEC, European Regulation 536/2014 for clinical studies (if applicable), European Medical Device Regulation 2017/745 for clinical device research (if applicable), and all other applicable local regulations.

trans-unit.source: Prompt notification by the Investigator to the Sponsor of an SAE is essential so that legal obligations and ethical responsibilities towards the safety of participants and the safety of a study intervention under clinical investigation are met.
trans-unit.target: Prompt notification by the Investigator to the Sponsor of an SAE is essential so that legal obligations and ethical responsibilities towards the safety of participants and the safety of a study intervention under clinical investigation are met.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor has a legal responsibility to notify both the local regulatory authority and other regulatory agencies about the safety of a study intervention under clinical investigation. The Sponsor will comply with country-specific regulatory requirements relating to safety reporting to the regulatory authority, IRB/IEC, and Investigators.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor has a legal responsibility to notify both the local regulatory authority and other regulatory agencies about the safety of a study intervention under clinical investigation. The Sponsor will comply with country-specific regulatory requirements relating to safety reporting to the regulatory authority, IRB/IEC, and Investigators.

trans-unit.source: In the European Union, the Sponsor will comply with safety reporting requirements and procedures as described in the European Clinical Trials Regulation (EU) No 536/2014. All Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions (SUSARs) to investigational medicinal product will be reported to the EudraVigilance database within the required regulatory timelines
trans-unit.target: In the European Union, the Sponsor will comply with safety reporting requirements and procedures as described in the European Clinical Trials Regulation (EU) No 536/2014. All Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions (SUSARs) to investigational medicinal product will be reported to the EudraVigilance database within the required regulatory timelines

trans-unit.source: For all studies except those utilising medical devices, Investigator safety reports must be prepared for SUSARs according to local regulatory requirements and Sponsor policy and forwarded to Investigators as necessary.
trans-unit.target: For all studies except those utilising medical devices, Investigator safety reports must be prepared for SUSARs according to local regulatory requirements and Sponsor policy and forwarded to Investigators as necessary.

trans-unit.source: Adherence to European Medical Device Regulation 2017/745 for clinical device research (if applicable), and all other applicable local regulations.
trans-unit.target: Adherence to European Medical Device Regulation 2017/745 for clinical device research (if applicable), and all other applicable local regulations.

trans-unit.source: An Investigator who receives an investigator safety report describing an SAE or other specific safety information (eg, summary or listing of SAEs) from the Sponsor will review and then file it along with the IB and will notify the IRB/IEC, if appropriate according to local requirements.
trans-unit.target: An Investigator who receives an investigator safety report describing an SAE or other specific safety information (eg, summary or listing of SAEs) from the Sponsor will review and then file it along with the IB and will notify the IRB/IEC, if appropriate according to local requirements.

trans-unit.source: Regulatory Reporting Requirements for Serious Breaches, Including Personal Data Breaches
trans-unit.target: Regulatory Reporting Requirements for Serious Breaches, Including Personal Data Breaches

trans-unit.source: Prompt notification by the Investigator to the Sponsor of any (potential) serious breach of the protocol or regulations (including if a data breach compromises the integrity, confidentiality, or availability of the personal data of participants) is essential so that legal and ethical obligations are met.
trans-unit.target: Prompt notification by the Investigator to the Sponsor of any (potential) serious breach of the protocol or regulations (including if a data breach compromises the integrity, confidentiality, or availability of the personal data of participants) is essential so that legal and ethical obligations are met.

trans-unit.source: A “serious breach” means a breach likely to affect to a significant degree the safety and rights of a participant or the reliability and robustness of the data generated in the clinical study.
trans-unit.target: A “serious breach” means a breach likely to affect to a significant degree the safety and rights of a participant or the reliability and robustness of the data generated in the clinical study.

trans-unit.source: If any (potential) serious breach occurs in the course of the study, Investigators or other site personnel will inform the appropriate Sponsor representatives immediately after he or she becomes aware of it.
trans-unit.target: If any (potential) serious breach occurs in the course of the study, Investigators or other site personnel will inform the appropriate Sponsor representatives immediately after he or she becomes aware of it.

trans-unit.source: In certain regions/countries, the Sponsor has a legal responsibility to notify both the local regulatory authority and other regulatory agencies about such breaches.
trans-unit.target: In certain regions/countries, the Sponsor has a legal responsibility to notify both the local regulatory authority and other regulatory agencies about such breaches.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor will comply with country-specific regulatory requirements relating to serious breach reporting to the regulatory authority (including data protection authorities and, if applicable, affected participants in case of a personal data breach), IRB/IEC, and Investigators. If EU CTR 536/2014 applies, the Sponsor is required to enter details of serious breaches into the European Medicines Agency CTIS. It is important to note that redacted versions of serious breach reports will be available to the public via CTIS.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor will comply with country-specific regulatory requirements relating to serious breach reporting to the regulatory authority (including data protection authorities and, if applicable, affected participants in case of a personal data breach), IRB/IEC, and Investigators. If EU CTR 536/2014 applies, the Sponsor is required to enter details of serious breaches into the European Medicines Agency CTIS. It is important to note that redacted versions of serious breach reports will be available to the public via CTIS.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator should have a process in place to ensure that:
trans-unit.target: The Investigator should have a process in place to ensure that:

trans-unit.source: The site staff or service providers delegated by the Investigator/institution are able to identify the occurrence of a (potential) serious breach., including personal data breaches.
trans-unit.target: The site staff or service providers delegated by the Investigator/institution are able to identify the occurrence of a (potential) serious breach., including personal data breaches.

trans-unit.source: A (potential) serious breach is promptly reported to the Sponsor or delegated party, through the contacts (email address or telephone number) provided by the Sponsor.
trans-unit.target: A (potential) serious breach is promptly reported to the Sponsor or delegated party, through the contacts (email address or telephone number) provided by the Sponsor.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor and the site have taken all necessary steps to avoid personal data breaches and have undertaken measures to prevent such breaches from occurring in the first place and to mitigate the impact of occurred data breaches (eg, applying encryption, maintaining and keeping systems and IT security measures up-to-date, regular reviews and testing, regular training of employees, and developed security policies and standards).
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor and the site have taken all necessary steps to avoid personal data breaches and have undertaken measures to prevent such breaches from occurring in the first place and to mitigate the impact of occurred data breaches (eg, applying encryption, maintaining and keeping systems and IT security measures up-to-date, regular reviews and testing, regular training of employees, and developed security policies and standards).

trans-unit.source: Both the Sponsor and the site have developed an internal data breach reporting and investigation process and internal protocols with guidance on how to respond swiftly and diligently to the occurrence of a personal data breach.
trans-unit.target: Both the Sponsor and the site have developed an internal data breach reporting and investigation process and internal protocols with guidance on how to respond swiftly and diligently to the occurrence of a personal data breach.

trans-unit.source: In compliance with applicable laws, the Data Controller for the processing activity where the personal data breach occurred (the Sponsor or respectively the site), will notify the data protection authorities without undue delay within the legal terms provided for such notification and within the prescribed form and content.
trans-unit.target: In compliance with applicable laws, the Data Controller for the processing activity where the personal data breach occurred (the Sponsor or respectively the site), will notify the data protection authorities without undue delay within the legal terms provided for such notification and within the prescribed form and content.

trans-unit.source: If the personal data breach needs to be notified to participants, the notification to participants is done by the site for the data breaches that occurred within the processing activities for which the site is the Data Controller. For data breaches that occurred within the processing activities of the Sponsor as the Data Controller, the notification is done in collaboration with the site and is performed by the site and/or Principal Investigator, acting on behalf of the Sponsor, so that the Sponsor has no access to the identifying personal information of the participants. The site and/or Principal Investigator shall conduct the notification by contacting the participants using the information that they gave for communication purposes in clinical research.
trans-unit.target: If the personal data breach needs to be notified to participants, the notification to participants is done by the site for the data breaches that occurred within the processing activities for which the site is the Data Controller. For data breaches that occurred within the processing activities of the Sponsor as the Data Controller, the notification is done in collaboration with the site and is performed by the site and/or Principal Investigator, acting on behalf of the Sponsor, so that the Sponsor has no access to the identifying personal information of the participants. The site and/or Principal Investigator shall conduct the notification by contacting the participants using the information that they gave for communication purposes in clinical research.

trans-unit.source: If a personal data breach occurs in a processor’s systems, engaged by the Sponsor, the processor under contractual obligations with the Sponsor promptly and in due course after discovering the breach notifies the Sponsor and provides full cooperation with the investigation. In these cases, to the extent the Sponsor is the Data Controller for the processing activity where the breach occurred, it will be responsible for the notification to data protection authorities and, if applicable, to participants. If the personal data breach needs to be notified to the participants, the notification to participants is done in collaboration with the site and is performed by the site and/or Principal Investigator, acting on behalf of the Sponsor, so that the Sponsor has no access to the identifying personal information of the participants.
trans-unit.target: If a personal data breach occurs in a processor’s systems, engaged by the Sponsor, the processor under contractual obligations with the Sponsor promptly and in due course after discovering the breach notifies the Sponsor and provides full cooperation with the investigation. In these cases, to the extent the Sponsor is the Data Controller for the processing activity where the breach occurred, it will be responsible for the notification to data protection authorities and, if applicable, to participants. If the personal data breach needs to be notified to the participants, the notification to participants is done in collaboration with the site and is performed by the site and/or Principal Investigator, acting on behalf of the Sponsor, so that the Sponsor has no access to the identifying personal information of the participants.

trans-unit.source: Where a personal data breach is suffered by the Study Monitor, the latter will provide the Sponsor with all of the information needed for notification of the breach, without disclosing data that allows the Sponsor directly or indirectly to identify the participants. The notification will be done by the Sponsor solely with the information provided by the Study Monitor and in no event with access to information that could entail a risk of re- identification of the participants. If the data breach must be notified to the data participants, the notification will be done directly by the Study Monitor in collaboration with the site and/or Principal Investigator, acting on behalf of the Sponsor, so that the Sponsor has no access to the identifying personal information of the participants. The contract between the Sponsor and the Study Monitor shall expressly specify these conditions.
trans-unit.target: Where a personal data breach is suffered by the Study Monitor, the latter will provide the Sponsor with all of the information needed for notification of the breach, without disclosing data that allows the Sponsor directly or indirectly to identify the participants. The notification will be done by the Sponsor solely with the information provided by the Study Monitor and in no event with access to information that could entail a risk of re- identification of the participants. If the data breach must be notified to the data participants, the notification will be done directly by the Study Monitor in collaboration with the site and/or Principal Investigator, acting on behalf of the Sponsor, so that the Sponsor has no access to the identifying personal information of the participants. The contract between the Sponsor and the Study Monitor shall expressly specify these conditions.

trans-unit.source: The contract between the site and the Sponsor for performing the clinical research includes the provisions and rules regarding who is responsible for coordinating and directing the actions in relation to the breaches and performing the mandatory notifications to authorities and participants, where applicable.
trans-unit.target: The contract between the site and the Sponsor for performing the clinical research includes the provisions and rules regarding who is responsible for coordinating and directing the actions in relation to the breaches and performing the mandatory notifications to authorities and participants, where applicable.

trans-unit.source: Investigators and sub-Investigators will provide the Sponsor with sufficient, accurate financial information as requested to allow the Sponsor to submit complete and accurate financial certification or disclosure statements to the appropriate regulatory authorities. Investigators are responsible for providing information on financial interests during the course of the study and for 1 year after completion of the study.
trans-unit.target: Investigators and sub-Investigators will provide the Sponsor with sufficient, accurate financial information as requested to allow the Sponsor to submit complete and accurate financial certification or disclosure statements to the appropriate regulatory authorities. Investigators are responsible for providing information on financial interests during the course of the study and for 1 year after completion of the study.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator or his/her representative will explain the nature of the study to the participant and answer all questions regarding the study.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator or his/her representative will explain the nature of the study to the participant and answer all questions regarding the study.

trans-unit.source: Participants must be informed that their participation is voluntary and they are free to refuse to participate and may withdraw their consent at any time and for any reason during the study. Participants will be required to sign a statement of informed consent that meets the requirements of 21 CFR 50, local regulations, ICH guidelines, HIPAA requirements, where applicable, and the IRB/IEC or study centre.
trans-unit.target: Participants must be informed that their participation is voluntary and they are free to refuse to participate and may withdraw their consent at any time and for any reason during the study. Participants will be required to sign a statement of informed consent that meets the requirements of 21 CFR 50, local regulations, ICH guidelines, HIPAA requirements, where applicable, and the IRB/IEC or study centre.

trans-unit.source: The medical record must include a statement that written informed consent was obtained before the participant was enrolled in the study and the date the written consent was obtained. The authorised person obtaining the informed consent must also sign the ICF.
trans-unit.target: The medical record must include a statement that written informed consent was obtained before the participant was enrolled in the study and the date the written consent was obtained. The authorised person obtaining the informed consent must also sign the ICF.

trans-unit.source: Participants must be re-consented to the most current version of the ICF(s) during their participation in the study.
trans-unit.target: Participants must be re-consented to the most current version of the ICF(s) during their participation in the study.

trans-unit.source: A copy of the ICF(s) must be provided to the participant.
trans-unit.target: A copy of the ICF(s) must be provided to the participant.

trans-unit.source: Participants who re-undergo enrolment procedures are required to sign a new ICF.
trans-unit.target: Participants who re-undergo enrolment procedures are required to sign a new ICF.

trans-unit.source: The ICF will contain a separate section that addresses and documents the collection and use of any mandatory human biological samples. The Investigator or authorised designee will explain to each participant the objectives of the analysis to be performed on the samples.
trans-unit.target: The ICF will contain a separate section that addresses and documents the collection and use of any mandatory human biological samples. The Investigator or authorised designee will explain to each participant the objectives of the analysis to be performed on the samples.

trans-unit.source: Participants will be assigned a unique identifier by Trusted Third Party contracted by the Sponsor or by the Principal Investigator. Any participant records or datasets that are transferred to the Sponsor will contain the identifier only; participant names or any information which would make the participant identifiable will not be transferred.
trans-unit.target: Participants will be assigned a unique identifier by Trusted Third Party contracted by the Sponsor or by the Principal Investigator. Any participant records or datasets that are transferred to the Sponsor will contain the identifier only; participant names or any information which would make the participant identifiable will not be transferred.

trans-unit.source: The participant must be informed that his/her personal study-related and coded (pseudonymised) data will be used by the Sponsor in accordance with local data protection law. The level of disclosure and use of their data must also be explained to the participant in the informed consent.
