trans-unit.target: The participant must be informed that his/her personal study-related and coded (pseudonymised) data will be used by the Sponsor in accordance with local data protection law. The level of disclosure and use of their data must also be explained to the participant in the informed consent.

trans-unit.source: The participant must be informed that his/her medical records may be examined by Clinical Quality Assurance auditors or other authorised personnel appointed by the Sponsor, by appropriate IRB/IEC members, and by inspectors from regulatory authorities.
trans-unit.target: The participant must be informed that his/her medical records may be examined by Clinical Quality Assurance auditors or other authorised personnel appointed by the Sponsor, by appropriate IRB/IEC members, and by inspectors from regulatory authorities.

trans-unit.source: General Data Protection Regulation defines pseudonymisation as the processing of personal data in such a way that the personal data can no longer be attributed to a specific individual without the use of additional information, provided that such additional information is kept separately and protected by technical and organisational measures to
trans-unit.target: General Data Protection Regulation defines pseudonymisation as the processing of personal data in such a way that the personal data can no longer be attributed to a specific individual without the use of additional information, provided that such additional information is kept separately and protected by technical and organisational measures to

trans-unit.source: ensure that the personal data are not attributed to an identified or identifiable natural person.
trans-unit.target: ensure that the personal data are not attributed to an identified or identifiable natural person.

trans-unit.source: Appropriate safeguards will be implemented to protect coded data during and after the study that include:
trans-unit.target: Appropriate safeguards will be implemented to protect coded data during and after the study that include:

trans-unit.source: Access to the coded data will be limited to specific individuals subject to confidentiality obligations (including the obligation to not attempt to re-identify individuals/decode the clinical data).
trans-unit.target: Access to the coded data will be limited to specific individuals subject to confidentiality obligations (including the obligation to not attempt to re-identify individuals/decode the clinical data).

trans-unit.source: The coded data will be protected with security measures to avoid data alteration, loss, and unauthorised accesses and further de-identification techniques may be applied.
trans-unit.target: The coded data will be protected with security measures to avoid data alteration, loss, and unauthorised accesses and further de-identification techniques may be applied.

trans-unit.source: A data protection impact assessment, where required, will apply to identify and mitigate privacy risks, if any, associated with each scientific research.
trans-unit.target: A data protection impact assessment, where required, will apply to identify and mitigate privacy risks, if any, associated with each scientific research.

trans-unit.source: The coded data will not be shared for direct marketing purposes or other purposes that are not legal duties or are not considered scientific research according to the applicable data protection legislation. In particular, it will not be used to make decisions about future services available to the participant, such as insurance.
trans-unit.target: The coded data will not be shared for direct marketing purposes or other purposes that are not legal duties or are not considered scientific research according to the applicable data protection legislation. In particular, it will not be used to make decisions about future services available to the participant, such as insurance.

trans-unit.source: In addition to having the participants’ data and biosamples coded, the data is also protected by high-standard technical security means, such as strong access control and encryption.
trans-unit.target: In addition to having the participants’ data and biosamples coded, the data is also protected by high-standard technical security means, such as strong access control and encryption.

trans-unit.source: Participants are also protected legally by the following means if the level of disclosure of the coded data includes sharing of the latter with other third parties as the participants will be explained in the ICF:
trans-unit.target: Participants are also protected legally by the following means if the level of disclosure of the coded data includes sharing of the latter with other third parties as the participants will be explained in the ICF:

trans-unit.source: Within the Sponsor group, the participants’ coded data are protected by Binding Corporate Rules. More information about the Sponsor’s BCR are provided here: www.astrazenecabindingcorporaterules.com.
trans-unit.target: Within the Sponsor group, the participants’ coded data are protected by Binding Corporate Rules. More information about the Sponsor’s BCR are provided here: www.astrazenecabindingcorporaterules.com.

trans-unit.source: In all other cases, the participants’ coded data is protected by contractual arrangements, Codes of Conduct, or certifications that set the rules for personal information protection to those available in European countries or other alternatives set forth in the law, as well as any supplementary technical and organisational measures that may results out of conducted transfer impact assessments.
trans-unit.target: In all other cases, the participants’ coded data is protected by contractual arrangements, Codes of Conduct, or certifications that set the rules for personal information protection to those available in European countries or other alternatives set forth in the law, as well as any supplementary technical and organisational measures that may results out of conducted transfer impact assessments.

trans-unit.source: For details on the IDMC, refer to the IDMC charter. For details on the Independent Adjudication Committee, refer to the charter.
trans-unit.target: For details on the IDMC, refer to the IDMC charter. For details on the Independent Adjudication Committee, refer to the charter.

trans-unit.source: A 6 Dissemination of Clinical Study Data
trans-unit.target: A 6 Dissemination of Clinical Study Data

trans-unit.source: Any results both technical and lay summaries for this trial will be submitted to EU CTIS within a year from the global End of Trial Date in all participating countries, due to scientific reasons, as otherwise statistical analysis is not relevant.
trans-unit.target: Any results both technical and lay summaries for this trial will be submitted to EU CTIS within a year from the global End of Trial Date in all participating countries, due to scientific reasons, as otherwise statistical analysis is not relevant.

trans-unit.source: A description of this clinical study will be available on http://astrazenecagrouptrials.pharmacm.com and http://www.clinicaltrials.gov as will the summary of the study results when they are available. The clinical study and/or summary of study results may also be available on other websites according to the regulations of the countries in which the study is conducted.
trans-unit.target: A description of this clinical study will be available on http://astrazenecagrouptrials.pharmacm.com and http://www.clinicaltrials.gov as will the summary of the study results when they are available. The clinical study and/or summary of study results may also be available on other websites according to the regulations of the countries in which the study is conducted.

trans-unit.source: All participant data relating to the study will be recorded on eCRF unless transmitted to the Sponsor or designee electronically (eg, laboratory data). The Investigator is responsible for verifying that data entries are accurate and correct by electronically signing the eCRF.
trans-unit.target: All participant data relating to the study will be recorded on eCRF unless transmitted to the Sponsor or designee electronically (eg, laboratory data). The Investigator is responsible for verifying that data entries are accurate and correct by electronically signing the eCRF.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator must maintain accurate documentation (source data) that supports the information entered in the eCRF.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator must maintain accurate documentation (source data) that supports the information entered in the eCRF.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator must permit study-related monitoring, audits, IRB/IEC review, and regulatory agency inspections and provide direct access to source data documents.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator must permit study-related monitoring, audits, IRB/IEC review, and regulatory agency inspections and provide direct access to source data documents.

trans-unit.source: Quality tolerance limits will be predefined in the IQRMP to identify systematic issues that can impact participant safety and/or reliability of study results. These predefined parameters will be monitored during the study, and important deviations from the quality tolerance limits and remedial actions taken will be summarised in the CSR.
trans-unit.target: Quality tolerance limits will be predefined in the IQRMP to identify systematic issues that can impact participant safety and/or reliability of study results. These predefined parameters will be monitored during the study, and important deviations from the quality tolerance limits and remedial actions taken will be summarised in the CSR.

trans-unit.source: Monitoring details describing strategy, including definition of study-critical data items and processes (eg, risk-based initiatives in operations and quality such as Risk Management and Mitigation Strategies and Analytical Risk-based Monitoring), methods, responsibilities and requirements, including handling of noncompliance issues and monitoring techniques (central, remote, or onsite monitoring) are included in the monitoring plan.
trans-unit.target: Monitoring details describing strategy, including definition of study-critical data items and processes (eg, risk-based initiatives in operations and quality such as Risk Management and Mitigation Strategies and Analytical Risk-based Monitoring), methods, responsibilities and requirements, including handling of noncompliance issues and monitoring techniques (central, remote, or onsite monitoring) are included in the monitoring plan.

trans-unit.source: AstraZeneca or designee is responsible for medical oversight throughout the conduct of the study which includes clinical reviews of study data in accordance with the currently approved protocol. Monitoring details describing clinical reviews of study data from a medical perspective are included in more detail in the monitoring plan.
trans-unit.target: AstraZeneca or designee is responsible for medical oversight throughout the conduct of the study which includes clinical reviews of study data in accordance with the currently approved protocol. Monitoring details describing clinical reviews of study data from a medical perspective are included in more detail in the monitoring plan.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor or designee is responsible for the data management of this study including quality checking of the data.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor or designee is responsible for the data management of this study including quality checking of the data.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor assumes accountability for actions delegated to other individuals (eg, Contract Research Organisations).
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor assumes accountability for actions delegated to other individuals (eg, Contract Research Organisations).

trans-unit.source: Study monitors will perform ongoing source data verification as per the Monitoring Plan to confirm that data entered into the eCRF by authorised site personnel are accurate, complete, and verifiable from source documents; that the safety and rights of participants are being protected; and that the study is being conducted in accordance with the currently approved protocol and any other study agreements, ICH GCP, and all applicable regulatory requirements.
trans-unit.target: Study monitors will perform ongoing source data verification as per the Monitoring Plan to confirm that data entered into the eCRF by authorised site personnel are accurate, complete, and verifiable from source documents; that the safety and rights of participants are being protected; and that the study is being conducted in accordance with the currently approved protocol and any other study agreements, ICH GCP, and all applicable regulatory requirements.

trans-unit.source: Records and documents, including signed ICFs, pertaining to the conduct of this study must be retained by the Investigator for a minimum of 25 years after study archiving or as required by local regulations, according to the AstraZeneca GRAD schedule. No records may be destroyed during the retention period without the written approval of the Sponsor. No records may be transferred to another location or party without written notification to the Sponsor.
trans-unit.target: Records and documents, including signed ICFs, pertaining to the conduct of this study must be retained by the Investigator for a minimum of 25 years after study archiving or as required by local regulations, according to the AstraZeneca GRAD schedule. No records may be destroyed during the retention period without the written approval of the Sponsor. No records may be transferred to another location or party without written notification to the Sponsor.

trans-unit.source: Source documents provide evidence for the existence of the participant and substantiate the integrity of the data collected. Source documents are filed at the Investigator’s site.
trans-unit.target: Source documents provide evidence for the existence of the participant and substantiate the integrity of the data collected. Source documents are filed at the Investigator’s site.

trans-unit.source: Data entered in the eCRF that are transcribed from source documents must be consistent with the source documents or the discrepancies must be explained. The Investigator may need to request previous medical records or transfer records, depending on the study. Also, current medical records must be available.
trans-unit.target: Data entered in the eCRF that are transcribed from source documents must be consistent with the source documents or the discrepancies must be explained. The Investigator may need to request previous medical records or transfer records, depending on the study. Also, current medical records must be available.

trans-unit.source: Definition of what constitutes source data can be found in the monitoring plan.
trans-unit.target: Definition of what constitutes source data can be found in the monitoring plan.

trans-unit.source: A 9 Study and Site Start and Closure
trans-unit.target: A 9 Study and Site Start and Closure

trans-unit.source: The study start date is the date on which the clinical study will be open for recruitment of participants.
trans-unit.target: The study start date is the date on which the clinical study will be open for recruitment of participants.

trans-unit.source: The first act of recruitment is the first site open and will be the study start date.
trans-unit.target: The first act of recruitment is the first site open and will be the study start date.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor designee reserves the right to close the study site or terminate the study at any time for any reason at the sole discretion of the Sponsor. Study sites will be closed upon study completion. A study site is considered closed when all required documents and study supplies have been collected and a study-site closure visit has been performed.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor designee reserves the right to close the study site or terminate the study at any time for any reason at the sole discretion of the Sponsor. Study sites will be closed upon study completion. A study site is considered closed when all required documents and study supplies have been collected and a study-site closure visit has been performed.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator may initiate study-site closure at any time, provided there is reasonable cause and sufficient notice is given in advance of the intended termination.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator may initiate study-site closure at any time, provided there is reasonable cause and sufficient notice is given in advance of the intended termination.

trans-unit.source: Reasons for the early closure of a study site by the Sponsor or Investigator may include but are not limited to:
trans-unit.target: Reasons for the early closure of a study site by the Sponsor or Investigator may include but are not limited to:

trans-unit.source: Failure of the Investigator to comply with the protocol, the requirements of the IRB/IEC or local health authorities, the Sponsor’s procedures, or GCP guidelines
trans-unit.target: Failure of the Investigator to comply with the protocol, the requirements of the IRB/IEC or local health authorities, the Sponsor’s procedures, or GCP guidelines

trans-unit.source: Inadequate recruitment of participants by the Investigator
trans-unit.target: Inadequate recruitment of participants by the Investigator

trans-unit.source: If the study is prematurely terminated or suspended, the Sponsor shall promptly inform the Investigators, the IECs/IRBs, the regulatory authorities, and any contract research organisation(s) used in the study of the reason for termination or suspension, as specified by the applicable regulatory requirements. The Investigator shall promptly inform the participant and should assure appropriate participant therapy and/or follow-up.
trans-unit.target: If the study is prematurely terminated or suspended, the Sponsor shall promptly inform the Investigators, the IECs/IRBs, the regulatory authorities, and any contract research organisation(s) used in the study of the reason for termination or suspension, as specified by the applicable regulatory requirements. The Investigator shall promptly inform the participant and should assure appropriate participant therapy and/or follow-up.

trans-unit.source: Participants from terminated sites will have the opportunity to be transferred to another site to continue the study.
trans-unit.target: Participants from terminated sites will have the opportunity to be transferred to another site to continue the study.

trans-unit.source: The results of this study may be published or presented at scientific meetings. If this is foreseen, the Investigator agrees to submit all manuscripts or abstracts to the Sponsor before submission. This allows the Sponsor to protect proprietary information and to provide comments.
trans-unit.target: The results of this study may be published or presented at scientific meetings. If this is foreseen, the Investigator agrees to submit all manuscripts or abstracts to the Sponsor before submission. This allows the Sponsor to protect proprietary information and to provide comments.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor will comply with the requirements for publication of study results. In accordance with standard editorial and ethical practice, the Sponsor will generally support publication of multicentre studies only in their entirety and not as individual site data. In this case, a coordinating Investigator will be designated by mutual agreement.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor will comply with the requirements for publication of study results. In accordance with standard editorial and ethical practice, the Sponsor will generally support publication of multicentre studies only in their entirety and not as individual site data. In this case, a coordinating Investigator will be designated by mutual agreement.

trans-unit.source: Authorship will be determined by mutual agreement and in line with International Committee of Medical Journal Editors authorship requirements.
trans-unit.target: Authorship will be determined by mutual agreement and in line with International Committee of Medical Journal Editors authorship requirements.

trans-unit.source: Appendix B Adverse Events: Definitions and Procedures for Recording, Evaluating, Follow-up, and Reporting
trans-unit.target: Appendix B Adverse Events: Definitions and Procedures for Recording, Evaluating, Follow-up, and Reporting

trans-unit.source: An AE is the development of any untoward medical occurrence in a patient or clinical study participant administered a medicinal product and which does not necessarily have a causal relationship with this treatment. An AE can therefore be any unfavourable and unintended sign (eg, an abnormal laboratory finding), symptom (for example nausea, chest pain), or disease temporally associated with the use of a medicinal product, whether or not considered related to the medicinal product.
trans-unit.target: An AE is the development of any untoward medical occurrence in a patient or clinical study participant administered a medicinal product and which does not necessarily have a causal relationship with this treatment. An AE can therefore be any unfavourable and unintended sign (eg, an abnormal laboratory finding), symptom (for example nausea, chest pain), or disease temporally associated with the use of a medicinal product, whether or not considered related to the medicinal product.

trans-unit.source: The term AE is used to include both serious and non-serious AEs and can include a deterioration of a pre-existing medical occurrence.
trans-unit.target: The term AE is used to include both serious and non-serious AEs and can include a deterioration of a pre-existing medical occurrence.

trans-unit.source: B 2 Definition of Serious Adverse Events
trans-unit.target: B 2 Definition of Serious Adverse Events

trans-unit.source: An SAE is an AE occurring during any study phase (ie, enrolment, treatment, washout, follow-up), that fulfils one or more of the following criteria:
trans-unit.target: An SAE is an AE occurring during any study phase (ie, enrolment, treatment, washout, follow-up), that fulfils one or more of the following criteria:

trans-unit.source: Requires in-participant hospitalisation or prolongation of existing hospitalisation
trans-unit.target: Requires in-participant hospitalisation or prolongation of existing hospitalisation

trans-unit.source: Results in persistent or significant disability or incapacity
trans-unit.target: Results in persistent or significant disability or incapacity

trans-unit.source: Is a congenital anomaly or birth defect
trans-unit.target: Is a congenital anomaly or birth defect

trans-unit.source: Is an important medical event that may jeopardise the participant or may require medical treatment to prevent one of the outcomes listed above.
trans-unit.target: Is an important medical event that may jeopardise the participant or may require medical treatment to prevent one of the outcomes listed above.

trans-unit.source: AEs for malignant tumours reported during a study should generally be assessed as SAEs. If no other seriousness criteria apply, the ‘Important Medical Event’ criterion should be used. In certain situations, however, medical judgement on an individual event basis should be applied to clarify that the malignant tumour event should be assessed and reported as a non-serious AE. For example, if the tumour is included as medical history and progression occurs during the study, but the progression does not change treatment and/or prognosis of the malignant tumour, the AE may not fulfil the attributes for being assessed as serious, although reporting of the progression of the malignant tumour as an AE is valid and should occur. Also, some types of malignant tumours, which do not spread remotely after a routine treatment that does not require hospitalisation, may be assessed as non-serious; examples in adults include Stage 1 basal cell carcinoma and Stage 1A1 cervical cancer removed via cone biopsy.
trans-unit.target: AEs for malignant tumours reported during a study should generally be assessed as SAEs. If no other seriousness criteria apply, the ‘Important Medical Event’ criterion should be used. In certain situations, however, medical judgement on an individual event basis should be applied to clarify that the malignant tumour event should be assessed and reported as a non-serious AE. For example, if the tumour is included as medical history and progression occurs during the study, but the progression does not change treatment and/or prognosis of the malignant tumour, the AE may not fulfil the attributes for being assessed as serious, although reporting of the progression of the malignant tumour as an AE is valid and should occur. Also, some types of malignant tumours, which do not spread remotely after a routine treatment that does not require hospitalisation, may be assessed as non-serious; examples in adults include Stage 1 basal cell carcinoma and Stage 1A1 cervical cancer removed via cone biopsy.

trans-unit.source: ‘Life-threatening’ means that the participant was at immediate risk of death from the AE as it occurred or it is suspected that use or continued use of the product would result in the
trans-unit.target: ‘Life-threatening’ means that the participant was at immediate risk of death from the AE as it occurred or it is suspected that use or continued use of the product would result in the

trans-unit.source: participant’s death. ‘Life-threatening’ does not mean that if an AE occurred in a more severe form it might have caused death (eg, hepatitis that resolved without hepatic failure).
trans-unit.target: participant’s death. ‘Life-threatening’ does not mean that if an AE occurred in a more severe form it might have caused death (eg, hepatitis that resolved without hepatic failure).

trans-unit.source: Outpatient treatment in an ER is not in itself an SAE, although the reasons for it may be (eg, bronchospasm, laryngeal oedema). Hospital admissions and/or surgical operations planned before or during a study are not considered AEs if the illness or disease existed before the participant was enrolled in the study provided that it did not deteriorate in an unexpected way during the study.
trans-unit.target: Outpatient treatment in an ER is not in itself an SAE, although the reasons for it may be (eg, bronchospasm, laryngeal oedema). Hospital admissions and/or surgical operations planned before or during a study are not considered AEs if the illness or disease existed before the participant was enrolled in the study provided that it did not deteriorate in an unexpected way during the study.

trans-unit.source: Important Medical Event or Medical Treatment
trans-unit.target: Important Medical Event or Medical Treatment

trans-unit.source: Medical and scientific judgement should be exercised in deciding whether a case is serious in situations where important medical events may not be immediately life threatening or result in death, hospitalisation, disability, or incapacity but may jeopardise the participant or may require medical treatment to prevent one or more outcomes listed in the definition of serious. These should usually be considered as serious.
trans-unit.target: Medical and scientific judgement should be exercised in deciding whether a case is serious in situations where important medical events may not be immediately life threatening or result in death, hospitalisation, disability, or incapacity but may jeopardise the participant or may require medical treatment to prevent one or more outcomes listed in the definition of serious. These should usually be considered as serious.

trans-unit.source: Simply stopping the suspect drug does not mean that it is an important medical event; medical judgement must be used. The following are examples of an Important Medical Event:
trans-unit.target: Simply stopping the suspect drug does not mean that it is an important medical event; medical judgement must be used. The following are examples of an Important Medical Event:

trans-unit.source: Angioedema not severe enough to require intubation but requiring IV hydrocortisone treatment
trans-unit.target: Angioedema not severe enough to require intubation but requiring IV hydrocortisone treatment

trans-unit.source: Hepatotoxicity caused by paracetamol (acetaminophen) overdose requiring treatment with N-acetylcysteine
trans-unit.target: Hepatotoxicity caused by paracetamol (acetaminophen) overdose requiring treatment with N-acetylcysteine

trans-unit.source: Intensive treatment in an ER or at home for allergic bronchospasm
trans-unit.target: Intensive treatment in an ER or at home for allergic bronchospasm

trans-unit.source: Blood dyscrasias (eg, neutropenia or anaemia requiring blood transfusion, etc.) or convulsions that do not result in hospitalisation
trans-unit.target: Blood dyscrasias (eg, neutropenia or anaemia requiring blood transfusion, etc.) or convulsions that do not result in hospitalisation

trans-unit.source: Development of drug dependency or drug abuse
trans-unit.target: Development of drug dependency or drug abuse

trans-unit.source: Mild (awareness of sign or symptom, but easily tolerated)
trans-unit.target: Mild (awareness of sign or symptom, but easily tolerated)

trans-unit.source: Moderate (discomfort sufficient to cause interference with normal activities)
trans-unit.target: Moderate (discomfort sufficient to cause interference with normal activities)

trans-unit.source: Severe (incapacitating, with inability to perform normal activities)
trans-unit.target: Severe (incapacitating, with inability to perform normal activities)

trans-unit.source: It is important to distinguish between serious and severe AEs. Severity is a measure of intensity whereas seriousness is defined by the criteria in Appendix B 2. An AE of severe intensity need not necessarily be considered serious. For example, nausea that persists for several hours may be considered severe nausea, but not an SAE unless it meets the criteria shown in Appendix B 2. On the other hand, a stroke that results in only a limited degree of disability may be considered a mild stroke but would be an SAE when it satisfies the criteria shown in Appendix B 2.
trans-unit.target: It is important to distinguish between serious and severe AEs. Severity is a measure of intensity whereas seriousness is defined by the criteria in Appendix B 2. An AE of severe intensity need not necessarily be considered serious. For example, nausea that persists for several hours may be considered severe nausea, but not an SAE unless it meets the criteria shown in Appendix B 2. On the other hand, a stroke that results in only a limited degree of disability may be considered a mild stroke but would be an SAE when it satisfies the criteria shown in Appendix B 2.

trans-unit.source: B 3 A Guide to Interpreting the Causality Question
trans-unit.target: B 3 A Guide to Interpreting the Causality Question

trans-unit.source: When making an assessment of causality consider the following factors when deciding if there is a ‘reasonable possibility’ that an AE may have been caused by the drug.
trans-unit.target: When making an assessment of causality consider the following factors when deciding if there is a ‘reasonable possibility’ that an AE may have been caused by the drug.

trans-unit.source: Time Course. Exposure to suspect drug. Has the participant actually received the suspect drug? Did the AE occur in a reasonable temporal relationship to the administration of the suspect drug?
trans-unit.target: Time Course. Exposure to suspect drug. Has the participant actually received the suspect drug? Did the AE occur in a reasonable temporal relationship to the administration of the suspect drug?

trans-unit.source: Consistency with known drug profile. Was the AE consistent with the previous knowledge of the suspect drug (pharmacology and toxicology) or drugs of the same pharmacological class? Or could the AE be anticipated from its pharmacological properties?
trans-unit.target: Consistency with known drug profile. Was the AE consistent with the previous knowledge of the suspect drug (pharmacology and toxicology) or drugs of the same pharmacological class? Or could the AE be anticipated from its pharmacological properties?

trans-unit.source: De-challenge experience. Did the AE resolve or improve on stopping or reducing the dose of the suspect drug?
trans-unit.target: De-challenge experience. Did the AE resolve or improve on stopping or reducing the dose of the suspect drug?

trans-unit.source: No alternative cause. The AE cannot be reasonably explained by another aetiology such as the underlying disease, other drugs, other host or environmental factors.
trans-unit.target: No alternative cause. The AE cannot be reasonably explained by another aetiology such as the underlying disease, other drugs, other host or environmental factors.

trans-unit.source: Re-challenge experience. Did the AE reoccur if the suspected drug was reintroduced after having been stopped? The Sponsor would not normally recommend or support a re- challenge.
trans-unit.target: Re-challenge experience. Did the AE reoccur if the suspected drug was reintroduced after having been stopped? The Sponsor would not normally recommend or support a re- challenge.

trans-unit.source: Laboratory tests. A specific laboratory investigation (if performed) has confirmed the relationship.
trans-unit.target: Laboratory tests. A specific laboratory investigation (if performed) has confirmed the relationship.

trans-unit.source: In difficult cases, other factors could be considered such as:
trans-unit.target: In difficult cases, other factors could be considered such as:

trans-unit.source: Is this a recognised feature of overdose of the drug?
trans-unit.target: Is this a recognised feature of overdose of the drug?

trans-unit.source: Causality of ‘related’ is made if following a review of the relevant data, there is evidence for a ‘reasonable possibility’ of a causal relationship for the individual case. The expression ‘reasonable possibility’ of a causal relationship is meant to convey, in general, that there are facts (evidence) or arguments to suggest a causal relationship.
trans-unit.target: Causality of ‘related’ is made if following a review of the relevant data, there is evidence for a ‘reasonable possibility’ of a causal relationship for the individual case. The expression ‘reasonable possibility’ of a causal relationship is meant to convey, in general, that there are facts (evidence) or arguments to suggest a causal relationship.

trans-unit.source: The causality assessment is performed based on the available data including enough information to make an informed judgement. With no available facts or arguments to suggest a causal relationship, the event(s) will be assessed as ‘not related’.
trans-unit.target: The causality assessment is performed based on the available data including enough information to make an informed judgement. With no available facts or arguments to suggest a causal relationship, the event(s) will be assessed as ‘not related’.

trans-unit.source: Causal relationship in cases where the disease under study has deteriorated due to lack of effect should be classified as no reasonable possibility.
trans-unit.target: Causal relationship in cases where the disease under study has deteriorated due to lack of effect should be classified as no reasonable possibility.

trans-unit.source: B 4 Medication Error, Drug Abuse, and Drug Misuse
trans-unit.target: B 4 Medication Error, Drug Abuse, and Drug Misuse

trans-unit.source: For the purposes of this clinical study a medication error is an unintended failure or mistake in the treatment process for an IMP or Sponsor NIMP that either causes harm to the participant or has the potential to cause harm to the participant.
trans-unit.target: For the purposes of this clinical study a medication error is an unintended failure or mistake in the treatment process for an IMP or Sponsor NIMP that either causes harm to the participant or has the potential to cause harm to the participant.

trans-unit.source: A medication error is not lack of efficacy of the drug, but rather a human- or process-related failure while the drug is in control of the study site staff or participant.
trans-unit.target: A medication error is not lack of efficacy of the drug, but rather a human- or process-related failure while the drug is in control of the study site staff or participant.

trans-unit.source: Medication error includes situations where an error:
trans-unit.target: Medication error includes situations where an error:

trans-unit.source: Was identified and intercepted before the participant received the drug
trans-unit.target: Was identified and intercepted before the participant received the drug

trans-unit.source: Did not occur, but circumstances were recognised that could have led to an error Examples of events to be reported in clinical studies as medication errors:
trans-unit.target: Did not occur, but circumstances were recognised that could have led to an error Examples of events to be reported in clinical studies as medication errors:

trans-unit.source: Dispensing error, eg, medication prepared incorrectly, even if it was not actually given to the participant
trans-unit.target: Dispensing error, eg, medication prepared incorrectly, even if it was not actually given to the participant

trans-unit.source: Drug not administered as indicated, eg, wrong route or wrong site of administration
trans-unit.target: Drug not administered as indicated, eg, wrong route or wrong site of administration

trans-unit.source: Drug not taken as indicated, eg, tablet dissolved in water when it should be taken as a solid tablet
trans-unit.target: Drug not taken as indicated, eg, tablet dissolved in water when it should be taken as a solid tablet

trans-unit.source: Drug not stored as instructed, eg, kept in the refrigerator when it should be at room temperature
trans-unit.target: Drug not stored as instructed, eg, kept in the refrigerator when it should be at room temperature

trans-unit.source: Wrong participant received the medication (excluding IRT/RTSM errors)
trans-unit.target: Wrong participant received the medication (excluding IRT/RTSM errors)

trans-unit.source: Wrong drug administered to participant (excluding IRT/RTSM errors) Examples of events that do not require reporting as medication errors in clinical studies:
trans-unit.target: Wrong drug administered to participant (excluding IRT/RTSM errors) Examples of events that do not require reporting as medication errors in clinical studies:

trans-unit.source: Errors related to or resulting from IRT/RTSM – including those which led to one of the above listed events that would otherwise have been a medication error
trans-unit.target: Errors related to or resulting from IRT/RTSM – including those which led to one of the above listed events that would otherwise have been a medication error

trans-unit.source: Participant accidentally missed drug dose(s), eg, forgot to take medication
trans-unit.target: Participant accidentally missed drug dose(s), eg, forgot to take medication

trans-unit.source: Accidental overdose (will be captured as an overdose)
trans-unit.target: Accidental overdose (will be captured as an overdose)

trans-unit.source: Participant failed to return unused medication or empty packaging
trans-unit.target: Participant failed to return unused medication or empty packaging

trans-unit.source: Medication errors are not regarded as AEs but AEs may occur as a consequence of the medication error.
trans-unit.target: Medication errors are not regarded as AEs but AEs may occur as a consequence of the medication error.

trans-unit.source: For the purposes of this study, drug abuse is defined as the persistent or sporadic intentional, non-therapeutic, excessive use of IMP or Sponsor NIMP for a perceived reward or desired non-therapeutic effect.
trans-unit.target: For the purposes of this study, drug abuse is defined as the persistent or sporadic intentional, non-therapeutic, excessive use of IMP or Sponsor NIMP for a perceived reward or desired non-therapeutic effect.

trans-unit.source: Any events of drug abuse, with or without associated AEs, are to be captured and forwarded to the DES using the Drug Abuse Report Form. This form should be used both if the drug abuse happened in a study participant or if the drug abuse involves a person not enrolled in the study (such as a relative of the study participant).
trans-unit.target: Any events of drug abuse, with or without associated AEs, are to be captured and forwarded to the DES using the Drug Abuse Report Form. This form should be used both if the drug abuse happened in a study participant or if the drug abuse involves a person not enrolled in the study (such as a relative of the study participant).

trans-unit.source: Examples of drug abuse include, but are not limited to:
trans-unit.target: Examples of drug abuse include, but are not limited to:

trans-unit.source: The drug is used with the intent of receiving a perceived reward (by the study participant or a person not enrolled in the study).
trans-unit.target: The drug is used with the intent of receiving a perceived reward (by the study participant or a person not enrolled in the study).

trans-unit.source: The drug in the form of a tablet is crushed and injected or snorted with the intent of getting high.
trans-unit.target: The drug in the form of a tablet is crushed and injected or snorted with the intent of getting high.

trans-unit.source: Drug misuse is the intentional and inappropriate use (by a study participant) of IMP or Sponsor NIMP for medicinal purposes outside of the authorised product information, or for unauthorised IMPs or Sponsor NIMPs, outside the intended use as specified in the protocol and includes deliberate administration of the product by the wrong route.
trans-unit.target: Drug misuse is the intentional and inappropriate use (by a study participant) of IMP or Sponsor NIMP for medicinal purposes outside of the authorised product information, or for unauthorised IMPs or Sponsor NIMPs, outside the intended use as specified in the protocol and includes deliberate administration of the product by the wrong route.

trans-unit.source: Events of drug misuse, with or without associated AEs, are to be captured and forwarded to the DES using the Drug Misuse Report Form. This form should be used both if the drug misuse happened in a study participant or if the drug misuse regards a person not enrolled in the study (such as a relative or study participant).
trans-unit.target: Events of drug misuse, with or without associated AEs, are to be captured and forwarded to the DES using the Drug Misuse Report Form. This form should be used both if the drug misuse happened in a study participant or if the drug misuse regards a person not enrolled in the study (such as a relative or study participant).

trans-unit.source: Examples of drug misuse include but are not limited to:
trans-unit.target: Examples of drug misuse include but are not limited to:

trans-unit.source: The drug is used with the intention to cause an effect in another person.
trans-unit.target: The drug is used with the intention to cause an effect in another person.

trans-unit.source: The drug is sold to other people for recreational purposes.
trans-unit.target: The drug is sold to other people for recreational purposes.

trans-unit.source: The drug is used to facilitate assault in another person.
trans-unit.target: The drug is used to facilitate assault in another person.

trans-unit.source: The drug is deliberately administered by the wrong route.
trans-unit.target: The drug is deliberately administered by the wrong route.

trans-unit.source: The drug is split in half because it is easier to swallow, when it is stated in the protocol that it must be swallowed whole.
trans-unit.target: The drug is split in half because it is easier to swallow, when it is stated in the protocol that it must be swallowed whole.

trans-unit.source: Only half the dose is taken because the study participant feels that he/she is feeling better when not taking the whole dose.
trans-unit.target: Only half the dose is taken because the study participant feels that he/she is feeling better when not taking the whole dose.

trans-unit.source: Someone who is not enrolled in the study intentionally takes the drug.
trans-unit.target: Someone who is not enrolled in the study intentionally takes the drug.

trans-unit.source: Appendix C Handling of Human Biological Samples
trans-unit.target: Appendix C Handling of Human Biological Samples

trans-unit.source: A full chain of custody is maintained for all samples throughout their lifecycle.
trans-unit.target: A full chain of custody is maintained for all samples throughout their lifecycle.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator at each centre maintains full traceability of collected biological samples from the participants while in storage at the centre until shipment or disposal (when appropriate) and records relevant to processing information related to the samples while at site.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator at each centre maintains full traceability of collected biological samples from the participants while in storage at the centre until shipment or disposal (when appropriate) and records relevant to processing information related to the samples while at site.

trans-unit.source: The sample receiver keeps full traceability of the samples while in storage and during use until used or disposed of or until further shipment and keeps record of receipt of arrival and onward shipment or disposal.
trans-unit.target: The sample receiver keeps full traceability of the samples while in storage and during use until used or disposed of or until further shipment and keeps record of receipt of arrival and onward shipment or disposal.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor or delegated representatives will keep oversight of the entire life cycle through internal procedures, monitoring of study sites, auditing or process checks, and contractual requirements of external laboratory providers.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor or delegated representatives will keep oversight of the entire life cycle through internal procedures, monitoring of study sites, auditing or process checks, and contractual requirements of external laboratory providers.

trans-unit.source: Samples retained for further use will be stored in the Sponsor-assigned biobanks or other sample archive facilities and will be tracked by the appropriate Sponsor team for the remainder of the sample life cycle.
trans-unit.target: Samples retained for further use will be stored in the Sponsor-assigned biobanks or other sample archive facilities and will be tracked by the appropriate Sponsor team for the remainder of the sample life cycle.

trans-unit.source: If required, the Sponsor will ensure that remaining biological samples are returned to the site according to local regulations or at the end of the retention period, whichever is sooner.
trans-unit.target: If required, the Sponsor will ensure that remaining biological samples are returned to the site according to local regulations or at the end of the retention period, whichever is sooner.

trans-unit.source: C 2 Withdrawal of Informed Consent for Donated Biological Samples
trans-unit.target: C 2 Withdrawal of Informed Consent for Donated Biological Samples

trans-unit.source: If a participant withdraws consent to the use of donated biological samples, the samples will be disposed of/destroyed/repatriated, and the action documented. If samples are already analysed, the Sponsor is not obliged to destroy the results of this research.
trans-unit.target: If a participant withdraws consent to the use of donated biological samples, the samples will be disposed of/destroyed/repatriated, and the action documented. If samples are already analysed, the Sponsor is not obliged to destroy the results of this research.

trans-unit.source: Following withdrawal of consent for biological samples, further study participation should be considered in relation to the withdrawal processes outlined in the informed consent.
trans-unit.target: Following withdrawal of consent for biological samples, further study participation should be considered in relation to the withdrawal processes outlined in the informed consent.

trans-unit.source: Ensures participant’s withdrawal of informed consent to the use of donated samples is highlighted immediately to the Sponsor or delegate.
trans-unit.target: Ensures participant’s withdrawal of informed consent to the use of donated samples is highlighted immediately to the Sponsor or delegate.

trans-unit.source: Ensures that relevant human biological samples from that participant, if stored at the study site, are immediately identified, disposed of as appropriate, and the action documented.
trans-unit.target: Ensures that relevant human biological samples from that participant, if stored at the study site, are immediately identified, disposed of as appropriate, and the action documented.

trans-unit.source: Ensures that the participant and the Sponsor are informed about the sample disposal.
trans-unit.target: Ensures that the participant and the Sponsor are informed about the sample disposal.

trans-unit.source: The Sponsor ensures the organisation(s) holding the samples is/are informed about the withdrawn consent immediately and that samples are disposed of or repatriated as appropriate, and the action is documented and study site is notified.
trans-unit.target: The Sponsor ensures the organisation(s) holding the samples is/are informed about the withdrawn consent immediately and that samples are disposed of or repatriated as appropriate, and the action is documented and study site is notified.

trans-unit.source: C 3 International Airline Transportation Association 6.2 Guidance Document
trans-unit.target: C 3 International Airline Transportation Association 6.2 Guidance Document

trans-unit.source: LABELLING AND SHIPMENT OF BIOHAZARD SAMPLES
trans-unit.target: LABELLING AND SHIPMENT OF BIOHAZARD SAMPLES

trans-unit.source: International Airline Transportation Association (IATA) (https://www.iata.org/whatwedo/cargo/dgr/Pages/download.aspx) classifies infectious substances into 3 categories: Category A, Category B, or Exempt
trans-unit.target: International Airline Transportation Association (IATA) (https://www.iata.org/whatwedo/cargo/dgr/Pages/download.aspx) classifies infectious substances into 3 categories: Category A, Category B, or Exempt

trans-unit.source: Category A Infectious Substances are infectious substances in a form that, when exposure occurs, is capable of causing permanent disability, life-threatening or fatal disease in otherwise healthy humans or animals.
trans-unit.target: Category A Infectious Substances are infectious substances in a form that, when exposure occurs, is capable of causing permanent disability, life-threatening or fatal disease in otherwise healthy humans or animals.

trans-unit.source: Category A Pathogens are, eg, Ebola, Lassa fever virus. Infectious substances meeting these criteria which cause disease in humans or both in humans and animals must be assigned to UN 2814. Infectious substances which cause disease only in animals must be assigned to
trans-unit.target: Category A Pathogens are, eg, Ebola, Lassa fever virus. Infectious substances meeting these criteria which cause disease in humans or both in humans and animals must be assigned to UN 2814. Infectious substances which cause disease only in animals must be assigned to

trans-unit.source: Category B Infectious Substances are infectious substances that do not meet the criteria for inclusion in Category A. Category B pathogens are, eg, hepatitis A, C, D, and E viruses. They are assigned the following UN number and proper shipping name:
trans-unit.target: Category B Infectious Substances are infectious substances that do not meet the criteria for inclusion in Category A. Category B pathogens are, eg, hepatitis A, C, D, and E viruses. They are assigned the following UN number and proper shipping name:

trans-unit.source: UN 3373 – Biological Substance, Category B are to be packed in accordance with UN 3373 and IATA 650
trans-unit.target: UN 3373 – Biological Substance, Category B are to be packed in accordance with UN 3373 and IATA 650

trans-unit.source: Exempt – Substances which do not contain infectious substances or substances which are unlikely to cause disease in humans or animals are not subject to these regulations unless they meet the criteria for inclusion in another class.
trans-unit.target: Exempt – Substances which do not contain infectious substances or substances which are unlikely to cause disease in humans or animals are not subject to these regulations unless they meet the criteria for inclusion in another class.

trans-unit.source: Clinical study samples will fall into Category B or exempt under IATA regulations.
trans-unit.target: Clinical study samples will fall into Category B or exempt under IATA regulations.

trans-unit.source: Clinical study samples will routinely be packed and transported at ambient temperature in IATA 650 compliant packaging (https://www.iata.org/contentassets/b08040a138dc4442a4f066e6fb99f32a/dge/- 62enpi650.pdf).
trans-unit.target: Clinical study samples will routinely be packed and transported at ambient temperature in IATA 650 compliant packaging (https://www.iata.org/contentassets/b08040a138dc4442a4f066e6fb99f32a/dge/- 62enpi650.pdf).

trans-unit.source: Biological samples transported in dry ice require additional dangerous goods specification for the dry-ice content.
trans-unit.target: Biological samples transported in dry ice require additional dangerous goods specification for the dry-ice content.

trans-unit.source: Appendix D Actions Required in Cases of Increases in Liver Biochemistry and Evaluation of Hy’s Law
trans-unit.target: Appendix D Actions Required in Cases of Increases in Liver Biochemistry and Evaluation of Hy’s Law

trans-unit.source: This appendix describes the process to be followed in order to identify and appropriately report potential Hy’s Law cases and Hy’s Law cases. It is not intended to be a comprehensive guide to the management of elevated liver biochemistries. Specific guidance on managing liver anomalies can be found in Section 8.3.7 of the CSP.
trans-unit.target: This appendix describes the process to be followed in order to identify and appropriately report potential Hy’s Law cases and Hy’s Law cases. It is not intended to be a comprehensive guide to the management of elevated liver biochemistries. Specific guidance on managing liver anomalies can be found in Section 8.3.7 of the CSP.

trans-unit.source: During the study, the Investigator will remain vigilant for increases in liver biochemistry. The Investigator is responsible for determining whether a participant meets potential Hy’s Law criteria at any point during the study.
trans-unit.target: During the study, the Investigator will remain vigilant for increases in liver biochemistry. The Investigator is responsible for determining whether a participant meets potential Hy’s Law criteria at any point during the study.

trans-unit.source: All sources of laboratory data are appropriate for the determination of potential Hy’s Law and HL events; this includes samples taken at scheduled study visits and other visits including central and all local laboratory evaluations even if collected outside of the study visits; for example, potential Hy’s Law criteria could be met by an elevated ALT from a central laboratory and/or elevated TBL from a local laboratory.
trans-unit.target: All sources of laboratory data are appropriate for the determination of potential Hy’s Law and HL events; this includes samples taken at scheduled study visits and other visits including central and all local laboratory evaluations even if collected outside of the study visits; for example, potential Hy’s Law criteria could be met by an elevated ALT from a central laboratory and/or elevated TBL from a local laboratory.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator will also review AE data (for example, for AEs that may indicate elevations in liver biochemistry) for possible potential Hy’s Law events.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator will also review AE data (for example, for AEs that may indicate elevations in liver biochemistry) for possible potential Hy’s Law events.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator participates, together with Sponsor clinical project representatives, in review and assessment of cases meeting potential Hy’s Law criteria to agree whether Hy’s Law criteria are met. Hy’s Law criteria are met if there is no alternative explanation for the elevations in liver biochemistry other than DILI caused by the IP.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator participates, together with Sponsor clinical project representatives, in review and assessment of cases meeting potential Hy’s Law criteria to agree whether Hy’s Law criteria are met. Hy’s Law criteria are met if there is no alternative explanation for the elevations in liver biochemistry other than DILI caused by the IP.

trans-unit.source: The Investigator is responsible for recording data pertaining to PHL/HL cases and for reporting SAEs and AEs according to the outcome of the review and assessment in line with standard safety reporting processes.
trans-unit.target: The Investigator is responsible for recording data pertaining to PHL/HL cases and for reporting SAEs and AEs according to the outcome of the review and assessment in line with standard safety reporting processes.

trans-unit.source: AST or ALT ≥ 3× ULN together with TBL ≥ 2× ULN at any point during the study following the start of study medication irrespective of an increase in ALP.
trans-unit.target: AST or ALT ≥ 3× ULN together with TBL ≥ 2× ULN at any point during the study following the start of study medication irrespective of an increase in ALP.

trans-unit.source: AST or ALT ≥ 3× ULN together with TBL ≥ 2× ULN, where no other reason, other than the IP, can be found to explain the combination of increases (eg, elevated ALP indicating cholestasis, viral hepatitis, another drug).
trans-unit.target: AST or ALT ≥ 3× ULN together with TBL ≥ 2× ULN, where no other reason, other than the IP, can be found to explain the combination of increases (eg, elevated ALP indicating cholestasis, viral hepatitis, another drug).

trans-unit.source: For potential Hy’s Law and Hy’s Law, the elevation in transaminases must precede or be coincident with (ie, on the same day) the elevation in TBL, but there is no specified timeframe within which the elevations in transaminases and TBL must occur.
trans-unit.target: For potential Hy’s Law and Hy’s Law, the elevation in transaminases must precede or be coincident with (ie, on the same day) the elevation in TBL, but there is no specified timeframe within which the elevations in transaminases and TBL must occur.

trans-unit.source: D 3 Identification of Potential Hy’s Law Cases
trans-unit.target: D 3 Identification of Potential Hy’s Law Cases

trans-unit.source: In order to identify cases of potential Hy’s Law it is important to perform a comprehensive review of laboratory data for any participant who meets any of the following identification criteria in isolation or in combination:
trans-unit.target: In order to identify cases of potential Hy’s Law it is important to perform a comprehensive review of laboratory data for any participant who meets any of the following identification criteria in isolation or in combination:

trans-unit.source: When a participant meets any of the potential Hy’s Law identification criteria, in isolation or in combination, the central laboratory will immediately send an alert to the Investigator (also sent to the Sponsor representative).
trans-unit.target: When a participant meets any of the potential Hy’s Law identification criteria, in isolation or in combination, the central laboratory will immediately send an alert to the Investigator (also sent to the Sponsor representative).

trans-unit.source: The Investigator will also remain vigilant for any local laboratory reports where the potential Hy’s Law identification criteria are met; where this is the case the Investigator will:
trans-unit.target: The Investigator will also remain vigilant for any local laboratory reports where the potential Hy’s Law identification criteria are met; where this is the case the Investigator will:

trans-unit.source: Request a repeat of the test (new blood draw) by the central laboratory without delay
trans-unit.target: Request a repeat of the test (new blood draw) by the central laboratory without delay

trans-unit.source: Complete the appropriate unscheduled laboratory eCRF module(s) with the original local laboratory test result
trans-unit.target: Complete the appropriate unscheduled laboratory eCRF module(s) with the original local laboratory test result

trans-unit.source: When the identification criteria are met from central or local laboratory results the Investigator will without delay:
trans-unit.target: When the identification criteria are met from central or local laboratory results the Investigator will without delay:

trans-unit.source: Determine whether the participant meets potential Hy’s Law identification criteria (refer to Section D 2 for definition) by reviewing laboratory reports from all previous visits (including both central and local laboratory results)
trans-unit.target: Determine whether the participant meets potential Hy’s Law identification criteria (refer to Section D 2 for definition) by reviewing laboratory reports from all previous visits (including both central and local laboratory results)

trans-unit.source: The Investigator will without delay review each new laboratory report and if the identification criteria are met will:
trans-unit.target: The Investigator will without delay review each new laboratory report and if the identification criteria are met will:

trans-unit.source: Determine whether the participant meets potential Hy’s Law identification criteria (refer to Section D 2 for definition) by reviewing laboratory reports from all previous visits
trans-unit.target: Determine whether the participant meets potential Hy’s Law identification criteria (refer to Section D 2 for definition) by reviewing laboratory reports from all previous visits

trans-unit.source: Promptly enter the laboratory data into the laboratory eCRF
trans-unit.target: Promptly enter the laboratory data into the laboratory eCRF

trans-unit.source: D 4.1 Potential Hy’s Law Criteria not met
trans-unit.target: D 4.1 Potential Hy’s Law Criteria not met

trans-unit.source: If the participant does not meet potential Hy’s Law identification criteria the Investigator will:
trans-unit.target: If the participant does not meet potential Hy’s Law identification criteria the Investigator will:

trans-unit.source: Inform the Sponsor representative that the participant has not met PHL criteria.
trans-unit.target: Inform the Sponsor representative that the participant has not met PHL criteria.

trans-unit.source: Perform follow-up on subsequent laboratory results according to the guidance provided in the CSP.
trans-unit.target: Perform follow-up on subsequent laboratory results according to the guidance provided in the CSP.

trans-unit.source: D 4.2 Potential Hy’s Law Criteria met
trans-unit.target: D 4.2 Potential Hy’s Law Criteria met

trans-unit.source: If the participant does meet potential Hy’s Law criteria the Investigator will:
trans-unit.target: If the participant does meet potential Hy’s Law criteria the Investigator will:

trans-unit.source: Notify the Sponsor representative who will then inform the central Study Team
trans-unit.target: Notify the Sponsor representative who will then inform the central Study Team

trans-unit.source: Within one day of potential Hy’s Law criteria being met, the Investigator will report the case as an SAE of potential Hy’s Law; serious criteria ‘Important medical event’ and causality assessment ‘yes/related’ according to CSP process for SAE reporting.
trans-unit.target: Within one day of potential Hy’s Law criteria being met, the Investigator will report the case as an SAE of potential Hy’s Law; serious criteria ‘Important medical event’ and causality assessment ‘yes/related’ according to CSP process for SAE reporting.

trans-unit.source: For participants that met potential Hy’s Law criteria prior to starting IP, the Investigator is not required to submit a potentially Hy’s Law SAE unless there is a significant change a in the participant’s condition
trans-unit.target: For participants that met potential Hy’s Law criteria prior to starting IP, the Investigator is not required to submit a potentially Hy’s Law SAE unless there is a significant change a in the participant’s condition

trans-unit.source: The study physician contacts the Investigator, to provide guidance, discuss, and agree on an approach for the study participants’ follow-up (including any further laboratory testing) and the continuous review of data
trans-unit.target: The study physician contacts the Investigator, to provide guidance, discuss, and agree on an approach for the study participants’ follow-up (including any further laboratory testing) and the continuous review of data

trans-unit.source: Subsequent to this contact the Investigator will:
trans-unit.target: Subsequent to this contact the Investigator will:

trans-unit.source: Monitor the participant until liver biochemistry parameters and appropriate clinical symptoms and signs return to normal or baseline levels, or as long as medically indicated. Complete follow-up SAE Form as required.
trans-unit.target: Monitor the participant until liver biochemistry parameters and appropriate clinical symptoms and signs return to normal or baseline levels, or as long as medically indicated. Complete follow-up SAE Form as required.

trans-unit.source: Investigate the aetiology of the event and perform diagnostic investigations as discussed with the study physician. This includes deciding which tests available in the Hy’s law lab kit should be used.
trans-unit.target: Investigate the aetiology of the event and perform diagnostic investigations as discussed with the study physician. This includes deciding which tests available in the Hy’s law lab kit should be used.

trans-unit.source: Complete the three Liver eCRF Modules as information becomes available
trans-unit.target: Complete the three Liver eCRF Modules as information becomes available

trans-unit.source: a A ‘significant’ change in the participant’s condition refers to a clinically relevant change in any of the individual liver biochemistry parameters (ALT, AST, or TBL) in isolation or in combination, or a clinically relevant change in associated symptoms. The determination of whether there has been a significant change will be at the discretion of the Investigator; this may be in consultation with the study physician if there is any uncertainty.
trans-unit.target: a A ‘significant’ change in the participant’s condition refers to a clinically relevant change in any of the individual liver biochemistry parameters (ALT, AST, or TBL) in isolation or in combination, or a clinically relevant change in associated symptoms. The determination of whether there has been a significant change will be at the discretion of the Investigator; this may be in consultation with the study physician if there is any uncertainty.

trans-unit.source: D 5 Review and Assessment of Potential Hy’s Law Cases
trans-unit.target: D 5 Review and Assessment of Potential Hy’s Law Cases

trans-unit.source: The instructions in this section should be followed for all cases where potential Hy’s Law criteria are met.
trans-unit.target: The instructions in this section should be followed for all cases where potential Hy’s Law criteria are met.

trans-unit.source: As soon as possible after the biochemistry abnormality is initially detected, the study physician contacts the Investigator in order to review available data and agree on whether there is an alternative explanation for meeting potential Hy’s Law criteria other than DILI caused by the IP, to ensure timely analysis and reporting to health authorities within
trans-unit.target: As soon as possible after the biochemistry abnormality is initially detected, the study physician contacts the Investigator in order to review available data and agree on whether there is an alternative explanation for meeting potential Hy’s Law criteria other than DILI caused by the IP, to ensure timely analysis and reporting to health authorities within

trans-unit.source: 15 calendar days from date PHL criteria was met. The Sponsor Global Clinical Lead or equivalent and Global Safety Physician will also be involved in this review together with other subject matter experts as appropriate.
trans-unit.target: 15 calendar days from date PHL criteria was met. The Sponsor Global Clinical Lead or equivalent and Global Safety Physician will also be involved in this review together with other subject matter experts as appropriate.

trans-unit.source: According to the outcome of the review and assessment, the Investigator will follow the instructions below.
trans-unit.target: According to the outcome of the review and assessment, the Investigator will follow the instructions below.

trans-unit.source: Where there is an agreed alternative explanation for the ALT or AST and TBL elevations, a determination of whether the alternative explanation is an AE will be made and subsequently whether the AE meets the criteria for a SAE:
trans-unit.target: Where there is an agreed alternative explanation for the ALT or AST and TBL elevations, a determination of whether the alternative explanation is an AE will be made and subsequently whether the AE meets the criteria for a SAE:

trans-unit.source: If the alternative explanation is not an AE, record the alternative explanation on the appropriate eCRF
trans-unit.target: If the alternative explanation is not an AE, record the alternative explanation on the appropriate eCRF

trans-unit.source: If the alternative explanation is an AE/SAE: update the previously submitted Potential Hy’s Law SAE and AE eCRFs accordingly with the new information (reassessing event term; causality and seriousness criteria) following the Sponsor standard processes.
trans-unit.target: If the alternative explanation is an AE/SAE: update the previously submitted Potential Hy’s Law SAE and AE eCRFs accordingly with the new information (reassessing event term; causality and seriousness criteria) following the Sponsor standard processes.

trans-unit.source: If it is agreed that there is no explanation that would explain the ALT or AST and TBL elevations other than the IP:
trans-unit.target: If it is agreed that there is no explanation that would explain the ALT or AST and TBL elevations other than the IP:

trans-unit.source: Send updated SAE (report term ‘Hy’s Law’) according to Sponsor standard processes.
trans-unit.target: Send updated SAE (report term ‘Hy’s Law’) according to Sponsor standard processes.

trans-unit.source: The ‘Medically Important’ serious criterion should be used if no other serious criteria apply
trans-unit.target: The ‘Medically Important’ serious criterion should be used if no other serious criteria apply

trans-unit.source: As there is no alternative explanation for the Hy’s Law case, a causality assessment of ‘related’ should be assigned.
trans-unit.target: As there is no alternative explanation for the Hy’s Law case, a causality assessment of ‘related’ should be assigned.

trans-unit.source: If, there is an unavoidable delay, of over 15 calendar days in obtaining the information necessary to assess whether or not the case meets the criteria for Hy’s Law identification, then it is assumed that there is no alternative explanation until such time as an informed decision can be made:
trans-unit.target: If, there is an unavoidable delay, of over 15 calendar days in obtaining the information necessary to assess whether or not the case meets the criteria for Hy’s Law identification, then it is assumed that there is no alternative explanation until such time as an informed decision can be made:

trans-unit.source: Provide any further update to the previously submitted SAE of potential Hy’s Law, (report term now ‘Hy’s Law case’) ensuring causality assessment is related to IP and seriousness criteria is medically important, according to CSP process for SAE reporting.
trans-unit.target: Provide any further update to the previously submitted SAE of potential Hy’s Law, (report term now ‘Hy’s Law case’) ensuring causality assessment is related to IP and seriousness criteria is medically important, according to CSP process for SAE reporting.

trans-unit.source: Continue follow-up and review according to agreed plan. Once the necessary supplementary information is obtained, repeat the review and assessment to determine whether Hy’s Law
trans-unit.target: Continue follow-up and review according to agreed plan. Once the necessary supplementary information is obtained, repeat the review and assessment to determine whether Hy’s Law

trans-unit.source: criteria are still met. Update the previously submitted potential Hy’s Law SAE report following CSP process for SAE reporting, according to the outcome of the review and amending the reported term if an alternative explanation for the liver biochemistry elevations is determined.
trans-unit.target: criteria are still met. Update the previously submitted potential Hy’s Law SAE report following CSP process for SAE reporting, according to the outcome of the review and amending the reported term if an alternative explanation for the liver biochemistry elevations is determined.

trans-unit.source: Hy’s Law Lab Kit for Central Laboratories
trans-unit.target: Hy’s Law Lab Kit for Central Laboratories

trans-unit.source: Additional standard chemistry and coagulation tests
trans-unit.target: Additional standard chemistry and coagulation tests

trans-unit.source: IgG anti-HSV, and HSV 1/2 IgM testing or HSV PCR c
trans-unit.target: IgG anti-HSV, and HSV 1/2 IgM testing or HSV PCR c

trans-unit.source: a HBV DNA is only recommended when IgG anti-HBc is positive.
trans-unit.target: a HBV DNA is only recommended when IgG anti-HBc is positive.

trans-unit.source: b HCV RNA is only recommended when IgG anti-HCV is positive or inconclusive.
trans-unit.target: b HCV RNA is only recommended when IgG anti-HCV is positive or inconclusive.

trans-unit.source: c HSV 1/2 IgM testing or HSV 1/2 PCR testing depending on region.
trans-unit.target: c HSV 1/2 IgM testing or HSV 1/2 PCR testing depending on region.

trans-unit.source: Females are considered to be of childbearing potential unless they meet either of the criteria, as follows:
trans-unit.target: Females are considered to be of childbearing potential unless they meet either of the criteria, as follows:

trans-unit.source: Surgically sterilised (including bilateral salpingectomy, bilateral oophorectomy, or hysterectomy), or
trans-unit.target: Surgically sterilised (including bilateral salpingectomy, bilateral oophorectomy, or hysterectomy), or

trans-unit.source: For females aged < 50 years, post-menopausal is defined as having both:
trans-unit.target: For females aged < 50 years, post-menopausal is defined as having both:

trans-unit.source: A history of ≥ 12 months amenorrhoea, without an alternative cause, following cessation of exogenous sex-hormonal treatment and,
trans-unit.target: A history of ≥ 12 months amenorrhoea, without an alternative cause, following cessation of exogenous sex-hormonal treatment and,

trans-unit.source: A FSH level in the post-menopausal range.
trans-unit.target: A FSH level in the post-menopausal range.

trans-unit.source: For females aged ≥ 50 years, post-menopausal is defined as having a history of ≥ 12 months amenorrhoea, without an alternative cause, following cessation of exogenous sex-hormonal treatment.
trans-unit.target: For females aged ≥ 50 years, post-menopausal is defined as having a history of ≥ 12 months amenorrhoea, without an alternative cause, following cessation of exogenous sex-hormonal treatment.

trans-unit.source: A highly effective method of contraception is defined as one that results in a low failure rate (ie, less than 1% per year) when used consistently and correctly. The acceptable methods of contraception are described in Table E1 below. Female patients must use one of the following non-hormonal or hormonal methods along with a condom.
trans-unit.target: A highly effective method of contraception is defined as one that results in a low failure rate (ie, less than 1% per year) when used consistently and correctly. The acceptable methods of contraception are described in Table E1 below. Female patients must use one of the following non-hormonal or hormonal methods along with a condom.

trans-unit.source: Periodic abstinence, the rhythm method, and withdrawal are NOT acceptable methods of contraception.
trans-unit.target: Periodic abstinence, the rhythm method, and withdrawal are NOT acceptable methods of contraception.

trans-unit.source: Table E1 Highly Effective Methods of Contraception
trans-unit.target: Table E1 Highly Effective Methods of Contraception

trans-unit.source: Bilateral tubal occlusion (caveat: failure rate >1%) Vasectomised partner a
trans-unit.target: Bilateral tubal occlusion (caveat: failure rate >1%) Vasectomised partner a

trans-unit.source: Combined (estrogen and progestogen containing hormonal contraception)
trans-unit.target: Combined (estrogen and progestogen containing hormonal contraception)

trans-unit.source: Hormone containing intrauterine devices (IUD, eg, Mirena, Liletta)
trans-unit.target: Hormone containing intrauterine devices (IUD, eg, Mirena, Liletta)

trans-unit.source: Oral (combined pill) Medroxyprogesterone injections (eg, Depo- Provera)
trans-unit.target: Oral (combined pill) Medroxyprogesterone injections (eg, Depo- Provera)

trans-unit.source: a With appropriate post-vasectomy medical testing of surgical success (ie, absence of sperm in ejaculate).
trans-unit.target: a With appropriate post-vasectomy medical testing of surgical success (ie, absence of sperm in ejaculate).

trans-unit.source: b Sexual abstinence is considered a highly effective method only if defined as refraining from heterosexual intercourse during the entire period of the study and if it is the preferred and usual lifestyle of the participant.
trans-unit.target: b Sexual abstinence is considered a highly effective method only if defined as refraining from heterosexual intercourse during the entire period of the study and if it is the preferred and usual lifestyle of the participant.

trans-unit.source: The following constitute ineffective methods of contraception:
trans-unit.target: The following constitute ineffective methods of contraception:

trans-unit.source: Other non-hormonal or non-copper-containing IUDs (or others not listed in the acceptable methods of contraception)
trans-unit.target: Other non-hormonal or non-copper-containing IUDs (or others not listed in the acceptable methods of contraception)

trans-unit.source: Sponge, diaphragm, or male or female condoms without secondary methods
