trans-unit.target: Sponge, diaphragm, or male or female condoms without secondary methods

trans-unit.source: Tubal occlusion without a secondary method
trans-unit.target: Tubal occlusion without a secondary method

trans-unit.source: Male participants are considered non-sterile unless they have previously had bilateral orchidectomy, which should be confirmed by medical assessment and documented in the subject’s medical records. Males who have undergone vasectomy, which should be documented in the subject’s medical records, must have the lack of sperm in the ejaculate confirmed a minimum of 30 days after the procedure by their physician on 2 separate occasions separated by a minimum of 30 days.
trans-unit.target: Male participants are considered non-sterile unless they have previously had bilateral orchidectomy, which should be confirmed by medical assessment and documented in the subject’s medical records. Males who have undergone vasectomy, which should be documented in the subject’s medical records, must have the lack of sperm in the ejaculate confirmed a minimum of 30 days after the procedure by their physician on 2 separate occasions separated by a minimum of 30 days.

trans-unit.source: Appendix F National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases and Food Allergy and Anaphylaxis Network Guidance for Anaphylaxis Diagnosis
trans-unit.target: Appendix F National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases and Food Allergy and Anaphylaxis Network Guidance for Anaphylaxis Diagnosis

trans-unit.source: The National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases and Food Allergy and Anaphylaxis Network define anaphylaxis as a serious allergic reaction that is rapid in onset and may cause death (Sampson et al 2006). They recognise 3 categories of anaphylaxis, with criteria designated to capture from 80% of cases (Category 1) to > 95% of all cases of anaphylaxis (for all 3 categories).
trans-unit.target: The National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases and Food Allergy and Anaphylaxis Network define anaphylaxis as a serious allergic reaction that is rapid in onset and may cause death (Sampson et al 2006). They recognise 3 categories of anaphylaxis, with criteria designated to capture from 80% of cases (Category 1) to > 95% of all cases of anaphylaxis (for all 3 categories).

trans-unit.source: Acute onset of an illness (minutes to several hours) with involvement of the skin, mucosal tissue, or both (eg, generalised hives, pruritus or flushing, swollen lips-tongue-uvula) AND AT LEAST ONE OF THE FOLLOWING:
trans-unit.target: Acute onset of an illness (minutes to several hours) with involvement of the skin, mucosal tissue, or both (eg, generalised hives, pruritus or flushing, swollen lips-tongue-uvula) AND AT LEAST ONE OF THE FOLLOWING:

trans-unit.source: Respiratory compromise (eg, dyspnoea, wheeze-bronchospasm, stridor, hypoxaemia).
trans-unit.target: Respiratory compromise (eg, dyspnoea, wheeze-bronchospasm, stridor, hypoxaemia).

trans-unit.source: Reduced BP or associated symptoms of end-organ dysfunction (eg, hypotonia [collapse], syncope, incontinence).
trans-unit.target: Reduced BP or associated symptoms of end-organ dysfunction (eg, hypotonia [collapse], syncope, incontinence).

trans-unit.source: Two or more of the following that occur rapidly after exposure to a likely allergen for that participant (minutes to several hours):
trans-unit.target: Two or more of the following that occur rapidly after exposure to a likely allergen for that participant (minutes to several hours):

trans-unit.source: Involvement of the skin-mucosal tissue (eg, generalised hives, itch-flush, swollen lips-tongue-uvula).
trans-unit.target: Involvement of the skin-mucosal tissue (eg, generalised hives, itch-flush, swollen lips-tongue-uvula).

trans-unit.source: Respiratory compromise (eg, dyspnoea, wheeze-bronchospasm, stridor, hypoxaemia).
trans-unit.target: Respiratory compromise (eg, dyspnoea, wheeze-bronchospasm, stridor, hypoxaemia).

trans-unit.source: Reduced BP or associated symptoms (eg, hypotonia [collapse], syncope, incontinence)
trans-unit.target: Reduced BP or associated symptoms (eg, hypotonia [collapse], syncope, incontinence)

trans-unit.source: Persistent gastrointestinal symptoms (eg, crampy abdominal pain, vomiting).
trans-unit.target: Persistent gastrointestinal symptoms (eg, crampy abdominal pain, vomiting).

trans-unit.source: Reduced BP after exposure to known allergen for that participant (minutes to several hours):
trans-unit.target: Reduced BP after exposure to known allergen for that participant (minutes to several hours):

trans-unit.source: Adults: systolic BP of less than 90 mmHg or greater than 30% decrease from that person’s baseline.
trans-unit.target: Adults: systolic BP of less than 90 mmHg or greater than 30% decrease from that person’s baseline.

trans-unit.source: For the purpose of AE reporting, the above criteria should be used to guide retrospective judgment as to whether an event was true anaphylaxis. Guidance on the recognition of possible anaphylaxis at the time of the event is provided (Appendix G).
trans-unit.target: For the purpose of AE reporting, the above criteria should be used to guide retrospective judgment as to whether an event was true anaphylaxis. Guidance on the recognition of possible anaphylaxis at the time of the event is provided (Appendix G).

trans-unit.source: Appendix G Signs and Symptoms and Management of Acute Anaphylaxis
trans-unit.target: Appendix G Signs and Symptoms and Management of Acute Anaphylaxis

trans-unit.source: Appropriate drugs, such as epinephrine, antihistamines, corticosteroids, etc, and medical equipment to treat anaphylactic reactions must be immediately available at study sites, and study personnel should be trained to recognise and treat anaphylaxis. Local or national guidelines for the recognition and management of acute anaphylaxis should be followed where available. Where not available, guidance is provided below.
trans-unit.target: Appropriate drugs, such as epinephrine, antihistamines, corticosteroids, etc, and medical equipment to treat anaphylactic reactions must be immediately available at study sites, and study personnel should be trained to recognise and treat anaphylaxis. Local or national guidelines for the recognition and management of acute anaphylaxis should be followed where available. Where not available, guidance is provided below.

trans-unit.source: G 1 Signs and Symptoms of Acute Anaphylaxis
trans-unit.target: G 1 Signs and Symptoms of Acute Anaphylaxis

trans-unit.source: Anaphylaxis is an acute and potentially lethal multi-system allergic reaction in which some or all of the following signs and symptoms occur:
trans-unit.target: Anaphylaxis is an acute and potentially lethal multi-system allergic reaction in which some or all of the following signs and symptoms occur:

trans-unit.source: Other earlier or concomitant signs and symptoms can include:
trans-unit.target: Other earlier or concomitant signs and symptoms can include:

trans-unit.source: Itchy nose, eyes, pharynx, genitalia, palms, and soles.
trans-unit.target: Itchy nose, eyes, pharynx, genitalia, palms, and soles.

trans-unit.source: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea, abdominal cramps, and bloating.
trans-unit.target: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea, abdominal cramps, and bloating.

trans-unit.source: Assessment of airway, breathing, circulation, and adequacy of mentation.
trans-unit.target: Assessment of airway, breathing, circulation, and adequacy of mentation.

trans-unit.source: Administer epinephrine IM every 5 to 15 minutes, in appropriate doses, as necessary, depending on the presenting signs and symptoms of anaphylaxis, to control signs and symptoms and prevent progression to more severe symptoms such as respiratory distress, hypotension, shock, and unconsciousness.
trans-unit.target: Administer epinephrine IM every 5 to 15 minutes, in appropriate doses, as necessary, depending on the presenting signs and symptoms of anaphylaxis, to control signs and symptoms and prevent progression to more severe symptoms such as respiratory distress, hypotension, shock, and unconsciousness.

trans-unit.source: G 2.2 Possibly Appropriate, Subsequent Measures Depending on Response to Epinephrine
trans-unit.target: G 2.2 Possibly Appropriate, Subsequent Measures Depending on Response to Epinephrine

trans-unit.source: Place participant in recumbent position and elevate lower extremities.
trans-unit.target: Place participant in recumbent position and elevate lower extremities.

trans-unit.source: Normal saline IV for fluid replacement.
trans-unit.target: Normal saline IV for fluid replacement.

trans-unit.source: G 2.3 Specific Measures to Consider After Epinephrine Injections, Where Appropriate
trans-unit.target: G 2.3 Specific Measures to Consider After Epinephrine Injections, Where Appropriate

trans-unit.source: Consider nebulised beta-2 agonist [eg, albuterol (salbutamol)] for bronchospasm resistant to epinephrine.
trans-unit.target: Consider nebulised beta-2 agonist [eg, albuterol (salbutamol)] for bronchospasm resistant to epinephrine.

trans-unit.source: Consider glucagon for participant taking b-blocker.
trans-unit.target: Consider glucagon for participant taking b-blocker.

trans-unit.source: Consider transportation to an ED or an intensive care facility.
trans-unit.target: Consider transportation to an ED or an intensive care facility.

trans-unit.source: For cardiopulmonary arrest during anaphylaxis, high-dose epinephrine and prolonged resuscitation efforts are encouraged, if necessary.
trans-unit.target: For cardiopulmonary arrest during anaphylaxis, high-dose epinephrine and prolonged resuscitation efforts are encouraged, if necessary.

trans-unit.source: If a suspected anaphylactic reaction occurs during or within a 24-hour period after administration of IP, blood samples for serum tryptase should be collected as soon as possible after the event, at 60 ± 30 minutes after the event, at discharge, and between 2 and 4 weeks post discharge. Immediate care of the participant and treatment of the reaction must take priority over collecting blood samples.
trans-unit.target: If a suspected anaphylactic reaction occurs during or within a 24-hour period after administration of IP, blood samples for serum tryptase should be collected as soon as possible after the event, at 60 ± 30 minutes after the event, at discharge, and between 2 and 4 weeks post discharge. Immediate care of the participant and treatment of the reaction must take priority over collecting blood samples.

trans-unit.source: Appendix H Changes Related to Mitigation of Study Disruptions Due to Cases of Civil Crisis, Natural Disaster, or Public Health Crisis
trans-unit.target: Appendix H Changes Related to Mitigation of Study Disruptions Due to Cases of Civil Crisis, Natural Disaster, or Public Health Crisis

trans-unit.source: Changes below should be implemented only during study disruptions due to any of or a combination of civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis (eg, during quarantines and resulting site closures, regional travel restrictions and considerations if site personnel or study participants become infected with SARS-CoV-2 or similar pandemic infection) during which participants may not wish to or may be unable to visit the study site for study visits. These changes should only be implemented if allowable by local/regional guidelines and following notification from the Sponsor, and instructions on how to perform these procedures will be provided at the time of implementation.
trans-unit.target: Changes below should be implemented only during study disruptions due to any of or a combination of civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis (eg, during quarantines and resulting site closures, regional travel restrictions and considerations if site personnel or study participants become infected with SARS-CoV-2 or similar pandemic infection) during which participants may not wish to or may be unable to visit the study site for study visits. These changes should only be implemented if allowable by local/regional guidelines and following notification from the Sponsor, and instructions on how to perform these procedures will be provided at the time of implementation.

trans-unit.source: During civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis, some study assessment and procedures may not be conducted due to international or local policies or guidelines, hospital or clinic restrictions, and other measures implemented to ensure participant’s safety. If in doubt, please contact the Sponsor study physician.
trans-unit.target: During civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis, some study assessment and procedures may not be conducted due to international or local policies or guidelines, hospital or clinic restrictions, and other measures implemented to ensure participant’s safety. If in doubt, please contact the Sponsor study physician.

trans-unit.source: H 1 Consent of Study Participants During Study Interruptions
trans-unit.target: H 1 Consent of Study Participants During Study Interruptions

trans-unit.source: During study interruptions, it may not be possible for the participants to complete study visits and assessments on site and alternative means for carrying out the visits and assessments may be necessary, eg, remote visits. Consent should be obtained for the alternative means of carrying out visits and assessments and should be obtained prior to performing the procedures described in Sections H 2 to H 6. Local and regional regulations and/or guidelines regarding reconsent of study participants should be checked and followed. Consent may be verbal if allowed by local and regional guidelines (note, in the case of verbal consent the ICF should be signed at the participant’s next contact with the study site). Visiting the study sites for the sole purpose of obtaining consent should be avoided.
trans-unit.target: During study interruptions, it may not be possible for the participants to complete study visits and assessments on site and alternative means for carrying out the visits and assessments may be necessary, eg, remote visits. Consent should be obtained for the alternative means of carrying out visits and assessments and should be obtained prior to performing the procedures described in Sections H 2 to H 6. Local and regional regulations and/or guidelines regarding reconsent of study participants should be checked and followed. Consent may be verbal if allowed by local and regional guidelines (note, in the case of verbal consent the ICF should be signed at the participant’s next contact with the study site). Visiting the study sites for the sole purpose of obtaining consent should be avoided.

trans-unit.source: H 2 Re-enrolment Procedures for Participants to Reconfirm Study Eligibility
trans-unit.target: H 2 Re-enrolment Procedures for Participants to Reconfirm Study Eligibility

trans-unit.source: Additional re-enrolment procedures for enrolment failure due to study disruption can be performed in previously participants that previously underwent enrolment procedures. The Investigator should confirm this with the designated Sponsor study physician.
trans-unit.target: Additional re-enrolment procedures for enrolment failure due to study disruption can be performed in previously participants that previously underwent enrolment procedures. The Investigator should confirm this with the designated Sponsor study physician.

trans-unit.source: In addition, during study disruption there may be a delay between confirming eligibility of a participant and either enrolment into the study or commencing of dosing with IP. If this delay occurs, the participant will need to re-undergo enrolment procedures to reconfirm eligibility before commencing study procedures. This will provide another opportunity to re-perform enrolment procedures for an enrolment failure participant in addition to that detailed in Section 5.4. The procedures detailed in Section 5 must be undertaken to confirm eligibility using the same randomisation number as for the participant.
trans-unit.target: In addition, during study disruption there may be a delay between confirming eligibility of a participant and either enrolment into the study or commencing of dosing with IP. If this delay occurs, the participant will need to re-undergo enrolment procedures to reconfirm eligibility before commencing study procedures. This will provide another opportunity to re-perform enrolment procedures for an enrolment failure participant in addition to that detailed in Section 5.4. The procedures detailed in Section 5 must be undertaken to confirm eligibility using the same randomisation number as for the participant.

trans-unit.source: H 3 Home or Remote Visit to Replace Onsite Visit (Where Applicable)
trans-unit.target: H 3 Home or Remote Visit to Replace Onsite Visit (Where Applicable)

trans-unit.source: A qualified HCP from the study site or TPV service will visit the participants’ home or other remote location as per local Standard Operating Procedures, as applicable. Supplies will be provided for a safe and efficient visit. The qualified HCP will be expected to collect information per the CSP.
trans-unit.target: A qualified HCP from the study site or TPV service will visit the participants’ home or other remote location as per local Standard Operating Procedures, as applicable. Supplies will be provided for a safe and efficient visit. The qualified HCP will be expected to collect information per the CSP.

trans-unit.source: If applicable, safety procedures and sample collection may be performed according to the revised SoA in the Study Instruction for Mitigation Due to Civil Crisis, Natural Disaster, or Public Health Crisis.
trans-unit.target: If applicable, safety procedures and sample collection may be performed according to the revised SoA in the Study Instruction for Mitigation Due to Civil Crisis, Natural Disaster, or Public Health Crisis.

trans-unit.source: H 4 Telemedicine Visit to Replace Onsite Visit (Where Applicable)
trans-unit.target: H 4 Telemedicine Visit to Replace Onsite Visit (Where Applicable)

trans-unit.source: In this appendix, the term telemedicine visit refers to remote contact with the participants using telecommunications technology including phone calls, virtual or video visits, and mobile health devices.
trans-unit.target: In this appendix, the term telemedicine visit refers to remote contact with the participants using telecommunications technology including phone calls, virtual or video visits, and mobile health devices.

trans-unit.source: During a civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis, onsite visits may be replaced by a telemedicine visit if allowed by local/regional guidelines. Having a telemedicine contact with the participants will allow AEs, concomitant medication, medical and COPD history, COPD treatment, COPD exacerbations, smoking status, supplemental oxygen status, HRU, and other information at deemed necessary by the Investigator to be reported and documented.
trans-unit.target: During a civil crisis, natural disaster, or public health crisis, onsite visits may be replaced by a telemedicine visit if allowed by local/regional guidelines. Having a telemedicine contact with the participants will allow AEs, concomitant medication, medical and COPD history, COPD treatment, COPD exacerbations, smoking status, supplemental oxygen status, HRU, and other information at deemed necessary by the Investigator to be reported and documented.

trans-unit.source: H 5 At-home or Remote Location IP Administration Instructions
trans-unit.target: H 5 At-home or Remote Location IP Administration Instructions

trans-unit.source: If an onsite visit is not possible, at-home or remote location administration of IP may be performed by a qualified HCP, provided this is acceptable within local regulation/guidance. The option of at-home or remote location IP administration ensures participants’ safety in cases of a pandemic where participants may be at increased risk by traveling to the site/clinic. This will also minimise interruption of IP administration during other study disruptions (eg, site closures due to natural disaster).
trans-unit.target: If an onsite visit is not possible, at-home or remote location administration of IP may be performed by a qualified HCP, provided this is acceptable within local regulation/guidance. The option of at-home or remote location IP administration ensures participants’ safety in cases of a pandemic where participants may be at increased risk by traveling to the site/clinic. This will also minimise interruption of IP administration during other study disruptions (eg, site closures due to natural disaster).

trans-unit.source: Section H 5.1 to be used only when applicable for the IP formulation being used, eg, with injectable biologics.
trans-unit.target: Section H 5.1 to be used only when applicable for the IP formulation being used, eg, with injectable biologics.

trans-unit.source: H 5.1 At-home or Remote Location IP Administration by a Qualified HCP or TPV Service
trans-unit.target: H 5.1 At-home or Remote Location IP Administration by a Qualified HCP or TPV Service

trans-unit.source: A qualified HCP from the study site or TPV service should administer the IP at the participant’s home or other remote location according to the CSP. All necessary supplies and instructions for administration and documentation of IP administration will be provided.
trans-unit.target: A qualified HCP from the study site or TPV service should administer the IP at the participant’s home or other remote location according to the CSP. All necessary supplies and instructions for administration and documentation of IP administration will be provided.

trans-unit.source: Additional information related to the visit can be obtained via a telemedicine or home visit.
trans-unit.target: Additional information related to the visit can be obtained via a telemedicine or home visit.

trans-unit.target: H 6 Data Capture During Telemedicine or Home/Remote Visits

trans-unit.target: Data collected during telemedicine or home / remote visits will be captured by the qualified HCP from the study site or TPV service.

trans-unit.target: 160 µg of budesonide × 0.78 = 124.8 µg fluticasone propionate equivalent.

trans-unit.source: As per Section 6.5.2 of the CSP all participants are required to be treated with stable optimised background maintenance inhaled therapy (ICS/LABA/LAMA triple therapy, or dual therapy if triple is not considered appropriate) during the study. Both dual and triple therapy may be in the form of separate inhalers (ATC codes R03AL, R03BB, R03BA) or fixed dose combination inhalers (ATC codes R03AL or R03AK).
trans-unit.target: As per Section 6.5.2 of the CSP all participants are required to be treated with stable optimised background maintenance inhaled therapy (ICS/LABA/LAMA triple therapy, or dual therapy if triple is not considered appropriate) during the study. Both dual and triple therapy may be in the form of separate inhalers (ATC codes R03AL, R03BB, R03BA) or fixed dose combination inhalers (ATC codes R03AL or R03AK).

trans-unit.source: Short-acting β2-agonists (SABAs, [ATC codes R03AC]), SAMAs [ATC codes R03BB], SABA/SAMA [ATC code R03AL]) combination or alternative symptom relief medications, as per local SoC, may be used during the study in the event of worsening of COPD symptoms and referred throughout the protocol as “rescue medications”. Day-to-day variation of symptoms requiring use of symptom relief (rescue) medications is expected in patients with COPD, and use of such medication does not necessarily reflect on the efficacy of the IMP.
trans-unit.target: Short-acting β2-agonists (SABAs, [ATC codes R03AC]), SAMAs [ATC codes R03BB], SABA/SAMA [ATC code R03AL]) combination or alternative symptom relief medications, as per local SoC, may be used during the study in the event of worsening of COPD symptoms and referred throughout the protocol as “rescue medications”. Day-to-day variation of symptoms requiring use of symptom relief (rescue) medications is expected in patients with COPD, and use of such medication does not necessarily reflect on the efficacy of the IMP.

trans-unit.source: Salbutamol (ATC code R03AC02) will also be used for post-BD spirometry.
trans-unit.target: Salbutamol (ATC code R03AC02) will also be used for post-BD spirometry.

trans-unit.source: In markets where pregnancy testing has to be performed at monthly intervals during the same period as contraception is mandatory (in this study, until 14 weeks after the last dose of IP), two additional pregnancy tests in FOCBP will be performed at Week 56 and Week 62.
trans-unit.target: In markets where pregnancy testing has to be performed at monthly intervals during the same period as contraception is mandatory (in this study, until 14 weeks after the last dose of IP), two additional pregnancy tests in FOCBP will be performed at Week 56 and Week 62.

trans-unit.source: Participants can perform this test at home and confirm the results of the test via a phone call/virtual call with the site staff. Alternatively, participants may visit the site to perform this test, if needed. If a participant discontinues IP early, then a pregnancy test will be performed monthly until 14-weeks post-last IP administration. If a participant discontinues IP, then a pregnancy test must be completed monthly until 14-weeks post-last IP administration. The results of the pregnancy must be documented in the eCRF.
trans-unit.target: Participants can perform this test at home and confirm the results of the test via a phone call/virtual call with the site staff. Alternatively, participants may visit the site to perform this test, if needed. If a participant discontinues IP early, then a pregnancy test will be performed monthly until 14-weeks post-last IP administration. If a participant discontinues IP, then a pregnancy test must be completed monthly until 14-weeks post-last IP administration. The results of the pregnancy must be documented in the eCRF.

trans-unit.source: Results of the pregnancy test at Week 56 and Week 62 should be entered in the eCRF as an unscheduled visit.
trans-unit.target: Results of the pregnancy test at Week 56 and Week 62 should be entered in the eCRF as an unscheduled visit.

trans-unit.target: For China, ADA samples will be retained for a maximum of 1 year.

trans-unit.source: With reference to Section 6.2.2.4 of the CSP. After administration of IP, the participant should be observed for potential acute reactions in line with local clinical practice for mAbs. In markets where a specific defined time period for observation of potential acute reactions after IP dose is required, it is recommended that for the first 2 doses of study intervention, the participant is to remain at the site for ≥ 2 hours after study intervention administration to be observed by the qualified HCP. For subsequent doses of study intervention, participants are to remain at the site for ≥ 1 hour after study intervention to be observed by the qualified HCP. Self-administration of IP at home by the participant or their caregiver is not applicable in the Czech Republic.
trans-unit.target: With reference to Section 6.2.2.4 of the CSP. After administration of IP, the participant should be observed for potential acute reactions in line with local clinical practice for mAbs. In markets where a specific defined time period for observation of potential acute reactions after IP dose is required, it is recommended that for the first 2 doses of study intervention, the participant is to remain at the site for ≥ 2 hours after study intervention administration to be observed by the qualified HCP. For subsequent doses of study intervention, participants are to remain at the site for ≥ 1 hour after study intervention to be observed by the qualified HCP. Self-administration of IP at home by the participant or their caregiver is not applicable in the Czech Republic.

source.g: Appendix D, Actions Required in Cases of Increases in Liver Biochemistry and Evaluation of

target.g: Appendix D, Actions Required in Cases of Increases in Liver Biochemistry and Evaluation of

trans-unit.source: With reference to Appendix D Actions Required in Cases of Increases in Liver Biochemistry and Evaluation of Hy’s Law. Further clarification in addition to Appendix D is provided to Investigators.
trans-unit.target: With reference to Appendix D Actions Required in Cases of Increases in Liver Biochemistry and Evaluation of Hy’s Law. Further clarification in addition to Appendix D is provided to Investigators.

trans-unit.source: In the event that there is an increase in liver enzymes AST and/or ALT ≥ 3× ULN and TBL ≥ 2× ULN with no alternative explanation, and the Investigator is confident that Hy’s
trans-unit.target: In the event that there is an increase in liver enzymes AST and/or ALT ≥ 3× ULN and TBL ≥ 2× ULN with no alternative explanation, and the Investigator is confident that Hy’s

trans-unit.source: Law criteria have been met and that the abnormalities are due to the IP, and if the PI believes it is in the best interest of the participant, then the IP should be immediately suspended until all liver tests are corrected to within normal limits. The Investigator should arrange any further appropriate investigations required and the Medical Monitor must be notified. This will be documented as a SAE in line with standard safety reporting processes.
trans-unit.target: Law criteria have been met and that the abnormalities are due to the IP, and if the PI believes it is in the best interest of the participant, then the IP should be immediately suspended until all liver tests are corrected to within normal limits. The Investigator should arrange any further appropriate investigations required and the Medical Monitor must be notified. This will be documented as a SAE in line with standard safety reporting processes.

trans-unit.source: If further use of the IP is commenced, with the agreement of the Investigator, participant, and Sponsor, and if there is a further increase in the liver enzymes after reintroduction, then the participant will be definitively excluded from further doses of the IP. Discontinuation from IP does not automatically lead to a complete withdrawal from the study. Participants discontinuing from IP are strongly encouraged to continue on-study for collection of safety and clinical data.
trans-unit.target: If further use of the IP is commenced, with the agreement of the Investigator, participant, and Sponsor, and if there is a further increase in the liver enzymes after reintroduction, then the participant will be definitively excluded from further doses of the IP. Discontinuation from IP does not automatically lead to a complete withdrawal from the study. Participants discontinuing from IP are strongly encouraged to continue on-study for collection of safety and clinical data.

trans-unit.source: This amendment is considered to be substantial based on the criteria set forth in Article 10(a) of Directive 2001/20/EC of the European Parliament and the Council of the EU, and in the EU CTR Article 2, 2 (13).
trans-unit.target: This amendment is considered to be substantial based on the criteria set forth in Article 10(a) of Directive 2001/20/EC of the European Parliament and the Council of the EU, and in the EU CTR Article 2, 2 (13).

trans-unit.source: The aim of this amendment is to change the primary endpoint from time to first severe COPD exacerbation to annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations in the primary population (previously a key secondary endpoint). The key secondary endpoint is correspondingly changed as a reflection of this from annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations to time to first severe COPD exacerbation.
trans-unit.target: The aim of this amendment is to change the primary endpoint from time to first severe COPD exacerbation to annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations in the primary population (previously a key secondary endpoint). The key secondary endpoint is correspondingly changed as a reflection of this from annualised rate of severe COPD exacerbations to time to first severe COPD exacerbation.

trans-unit.source: Rate of exacerbations is a well-established and clinically relevant endpoint for COPD trials. Based upon a review of blinded aggregate data, greater rates of severe exacerbation events and higher dispersion have been detected throughout the predecessor studies OBERON and TITANIA and through this LTE PROSPERO than was predicted when the studies were initiated, while discontinuation rates have also been lower. In this situation there can be attenuation of treatment effects in a time to event analysis compared to a rate-based analysis and so a rate-based analysis is expected to provide greater statistical power.
trans-unit.target: Rate of exacerbations is a well-established and clinically relevant endpoint for COPD trials. Based upon a review of blinded aggregate data, greater rates of severe exacerbation events and higher dispersion have been detected throughout the predecessor studies OBERON and TITANIA and through this LTE PROSPERO than was predicted when the studies were initiated, while discontinuation rates have also been lower. In this situation there can be attenuation of treatment effects in a time to event analysis compared to a rate-based analysis and so a rate-based analysis is expected to provide greater statistical power.

trans-unit.source: The primary objective of this amendment is to account for an update in the predecessor studies to reduce the sample size of the tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm and to update the duration of the treatment period.
trans-unit.target: The primary objective of this amendment is to account for an update in the predecessor studies to reduce the sample size of the tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm and to update the duration of the treatment period.

trans-unit.source: The rationale for this change follows recent data from Phase II studies in moderate to severe atopic dermatitis and asthma, in which lower trough PK concentrations (30% to 35%) were observed compared with the Phase I healthy volunteer study. With this updated exposure data, predicted IL-33 lung inhibition reduces from 94% to 92%, but remains above 90% for the
trans-unit.target: The rationale for this change follows recent data from Phase II studies in moderate to severe atopic dermatitis and asthma, in which lower trough PK concentrations (30% to 35%) were observed compared with the Phase I healthy volunteer study. With this updated exposure data, predicted IL-33 lung inhibition reduces from 94% to 92%, but remains above 90% for the

trans-unit.source: 300 mg Q4W regimen; the 2% drop suggests this dose is on the flat portion of the dose- response curve. However, the predicted IL-33 inhibition for the 300 mg Q8W regimen reduces by approximately 5% to below 80%.
trans-unit.target: 300 mg Q4W regimen; the 2% drop suggests this dose is on the flat portion of the dose- response curve. However, the predicted IL-33 inhibition for the 300 mg Q8W regimen reduces by approximately 5% to below 80%.

trans-unit.source: On this basis, together with the acceptable safety profile of tozorakimab across all dosing regimens, randomisation to the tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm will be stopped in the predecessor studies, OBERON and TITANIA. It follows that no new participants will be allocated to tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W in this study after the amendment to PROSPERO.
trans-unit.target: On this basis, together with the acceptable safety profile of tozorakimab across all dosing regimens, randomisation to the tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm will be stopped in the predecessor studies, OBERON and TITANIA. It follows that no new participants will be allocated to tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W in this study after the amendment to PROSPERO.

trans-unit.source: The treatment period in this study is reduced to 28 weeks since this is a sufficient treatment period, when pooled with the predecessor studies, to allow the collection of efficacy, long- term safety data, and the timely completion of the study. Those participants who are already enrolled under CSP Version 1.0 can continue to complete the study with the same treatment regimen as assigned and for a treatment period of 52 weeks after this amendment.
trans-unit.target: The treatment period in this study is reduced to 28 weeks since this is a sufficient treatment period, when pooled with the predecessor studies, to allow the collection of efficacy, long- term safety data, and the timely completion of the study. Those participants who are already enrolled under CSP Version 1.0 can continue to complete the study with the same treatment regimen as assigned and for a treatment period of 52 weeks after this amendment.

trans-unit.source: Participants will therefore be enrolled in the study prior to the amendment under CSP Version 1.0 or under this version (Version 2.0) as follows:
trans-unit.target: Participants will therefore be enrolled in the study prior to the amendment under CSP Version 1.0 or under this version (Version 2.0) as follows:

trans-unit.source: CSP Version 1.0: Participants receiving tozorakimab in the predecessor study will have been allocated to the same active treatment regimen as in the predecessor study.
trans-unit.target: CSP Version 1.0: Participants receiving tozorakimab in the predecessor study will have been allocated to the same active treatment regimen as in the predecessor study.

trans-unit.source: Participants from the placebo arm of the predecessor study will have been re-randomised in PROSPERO in a 1:1:2 ratio to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W, tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W and placebo, for a treatment period of 52 weeks.
trans-unit.target: Participants from the placebo arm of the predecessor study will have been re-randomised in PROSPERO in a 1:1:2 ratio to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W, tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W and placebo, for a treatment period of 52 weeks.

trans-unit.source: CSP Version 2.0: Participants on 300 mg Q4W in the predecessor studies will remain on 300 mg Q4W. Participants on 300 mg Q8W in the predecessor studies will be allocated to 300 mg Q4W in PROSPERO, and participants on placebo from the predecessor studies will be re-randomised 1:1 to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W or placebo. The treatment period will be 28 weeks. Those participants who are already enrolled under CSP Version 1.0 can continue to complete the study with the same treatment regimen as assigned and for a treatment period of 52 weeks after this amendment.
trans-unit.target: CSP Version 2.0: Participants on 300 mg Q4W in the predecessor studies will remain on 300 mg Q4W. Participants on 300 mg Q8W in the predecessor studies will be allocated to 300 mg Q4W in PROSPERO, and participants on placebo from the predecessor studies will be re-randomised 1:1 to tozorakimab 300 mg Q4W or placebo. The treatment period will be 28 weeks. Those participants who are already enrolled under CSP Version 1.0 can continue to complete the study with the same treatment regimen as assigned and for a treatment period of 52 weeks after this amendment.

trans-unit.source: It follows that under CSP Version 2.0 no new participants from the predecessor studies will be allocated to 300 mg Q8W.
trans-unit.target: It follows that under CSP Version 2.0 no new participants from the predecessor studies will be allocated to 300 mg Q8W.

trans-unit.source: The data collected from the tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm will only be used for characterisation of the dose-response curve, and not for confirmatory hypothesis testing. The overall sample size and multiple testing procedure have been amended accordingly.
trans-unit.target: The data collected from the tozorakimab 300 mg Q8W arm will only be used for characterisation of the dose-response curve, and not for confirmatory hypothesis testing. The overall sample size and multiple testing procedure have been amended accordingly.

trans-unit.source: Additional non-substantial updates were also made to: implement mandatory changes bringing the AstraZeneca global CSP template into alignment with industry standards; update per requirements for EU CTR; clarify procedures, statistical analyses, and CSP and appendices wording.
trans-unit.target: Additional non-substantial updates were also made to: implement mandatory changes bringing the AstraZeneca global CSP template into alignment with industry standards; update per requirements for EU CTR; clarify procedures, statistical analyses, and CSP and appendices wording.

trans-unit.source: No new participants from the predecessor studies will be allocated to 300 mg Q8W after the amendment and sample size of the Q8W arm is reduced as such. Study design, sample size, and statistical analysis (including confirmatory hypothesis testing and the multiple testing procedure) are updated to reflect this change.
trans-unit.target: No new participants from the predecessor studies will be allocated to 300 mg Q8W after the amendment and sample size of the Q8W arm is reduced as such. Study design, sample size, and statistical analysis (including confirmatory hypothesis testing and the multiple testing procedure) are updated to reflect this change.

trans-unit.source: Update of the percentage of IL33:ST2 inhibition expected in lung at trough for both tozorakimab dose regimens based on emerging Phase II data.
trans-unit.target: Update of the percentage of IL33:ST2 inhibition expected in lung at trough for both tozorakimab dose regimens based on emerging Phase II data.

trans-unit.source: The rationale for this change follows recent data from Phase II studies in moderate to severe atopic dermatitis (D9182C00001) and asthma, in which lower trough PK concentrations (30% to 35%) were observed compared with the Phase I healthy volunteer study (D9182C00001).
trans-unit.target: The rationale for this change follows recent data from Phase II studies in moderate to severe atopic dermatitis (D9182C00001) and asthma, in which lower trough PK concentrations (30% to 35%) were observed compared with the Phase I healthy volunteer study (D9182C00001).
